# Thread: How can creation of an aurora teach you something deep about Redox?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-creation-of-an-aurora-teach-you-something-deep-about-redox.20492/>

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-22T16:55:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-creation-of-an-aurora-teach-you-something-deep-about-redox.20492/post-228633>

Light is an electromagnetic wave. To be precise light is really a moving oscillating electric and magnetic field.
Light is not affected by other magnetic fields by external sources in vacuum like space. This is primarily due Principle of Superposition which states that fields themselves don't interact with each other. But remember, light can affect the behavior of electron and protons. All one has to do is the interaction of the solar plasma and the magnetosphere of Jupiter to see the effects of sunlight on a magnetic field loaded with charge particles. The interaction of light energy and highly charge particles in the magnetosphere makes light visible in the form of planetary aurora's in the solar system. Jupiter's magnetosphere is 20,000 more powerful than Earth and this creates a physics on this planet far different than Earth. Weather and lightening on Jupiter are far more powerful on Jupiter because of this effect.

So light is affected by external magnetic fields in a vacuum when that magnetic field has electrons and protons present in it. Mitochondria have electrons and protons in it and MEG data tells us tissues with different mitochondrial density have different magnetic power. This means the light that interacts with variable magnetic fields and the charges they contain differently. This is the easiest example that nature provides us to observe the phenomena is Jupiter. The larger the magnetic field the more phase transitions of light energy occurs. In Jupiter's case, additional charged particles are added to its magnetic field by Io volcano's super charging its aurora's.

[http://lasp.colorado.edu/education/outerplanets/giantplanets\_magnetospheres.php](http://lasp.colorado.edu/education/outerplanets/giantplanets_magnetospheres.php)

The released electric power is so powerful some scientist believe that in the storms of Jupiter hail is made of diamonds because methane has its 4 hydrogen's stripped from the carbon to form a diamond lattice. When those solid diamonds fall through the atomosphere as the temperature gets to 14,400 F because of lightening, the diamond hail stones melt and fall into an ocean of liquid diamond forming an ocean of carbon above the H+metal plasma below. Lightning storms turn methane into soot which hardens into chunks of graphite and then diamond as it falls to the surface of the planet. This shows you how a planet's physics (environment) determine what can or can not happen in a system. This is why bio-chemistry cannot determine things 100% on Earth if the environment is varying. For 4.6 billion years it has been stable.

Today's use of the electromagnetic spectrum to communicate has changed that. So how we use light is now in flux in our cells. This is why bio-chemistry is no longer a golden rule of biology. Few realize it because they do not understand physics. This example of Jupiter compared to Earth shows you this side of the coin. As a persons' environment changes so can their bio-chemistry. Understanding how the aurora's are made helps you see this possibility in living systems on Earth. The Aurora's on Earth and Jupiter mechanistically are made the same way by the laws of physics, but the environment on earth planet lead to a different reality. For those of you who don't know chloroplasts and hemoglobin are porphyrin molecules with 4 Nitrogen atoms that form a cages that turn the electric and magnetic fields of sun light into lightening in leaves or in the hemoglobin of our blood. This is called electric or magnetic induction. The implications are astounding when you have this perspective. WHY?

Few people realize how when you change the physics of any environment it changes the electric and magnetic induction possible of incident light. Aurora's teach us this story and few people see how it links to chloroplast or hemoglobin biology. Science is catching on to the electricity story in living systems folks: Quantum biology is Darwin’s unfinished symphony. Why do I say this? Power laws of energy transfer are screaming to us in science that Darwin is missing some fundamental pillars within nature's recipes. Quantum mechanics is the fountain all living things drink from, hence how each thread of life builds its tapestry is always related to how light scatters in a cell to distribute a quilt of electric charge in the redox potential in that tissue that is correlated to energy flux via its mitochondrial heteroplasmy rate. This is why the spin of electrons and protons are critical to get right in your life. As these change what can occur in biochemical pathways change. This is really how evolution works. Electro-magnetic induction with resonant coupling is how living systems connect with the sun naturally via chlorohyll and hemoglobin.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-29T02:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-creation-of-an-aurora-teach-you-something-deep-about-redox.20492/post-229083>

Picture of the Day described gigantic “plasma tornadoes”, or flux tubes, entering Earth’s magnetosphere from space. They were found by the fleet of THEMIS satellites when they flew through the highly charged structures. The tubes are spinning at more than 1.5 million kilometers per hour and generating over 100,000 amps of electric charge flow.-----

Jupiter’s Great Red Spot from the Juno spacecraft and citizen scientists. Original image credit: NASA-JPL/SwRI/MSSS/Gerald Eichstädt/Seán Doran.

So what does it mean when the spot is shrinking?

Yes, Jupiter’s Red Spot is shrinking.

For over 300 years, the Great Red Spot has persisted in Jupiter’s atmosphere—longer than telescopes have existed to see it. It is commonly believed to be a cyclonic storm driven by thermal convection from deep inside Jupiter’s atmosphere. How it was formed and why it has lasted so long remain a mystery.

No one is sure why it is red in color. For that matter, scientists are not sure why any of the gas giant planets possess unique color schemes. Neptune has a blue tint, Uranus green, Saturn is pale yellow, and Jupiter is a rusty red, overall.

According to a recent press release, when Voyager 2 flew by Jupiter almost 50 years ago, the _Great Red Spot was as large as three_ Earth diameters. Now, it’s _only about one_ Earth wide. Glenn Orton, a planetary scientist at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory said:

“If you look at pictures from the late 19th century, you’d swear it was the great red sausage.”

Conventional theories suggest that warmer areas in the center of the Spot are enough to cause its rotation, also turning the center a darker red. The red color’s origin—whether from chemical changes in the atmosphere or upwelling of other materials from below—requires further study. Some investigators think that “vertical flow” from warmer regions below maintain the Great Red Spot. Identifying it as a storm means that those vertical flows circulate hot and cold gas in and out of it, thus preventing it from dissipating.

From an Electric Universe perspective, the Great Red Spot could have a different origin altogether: Jupiter’s turbulent atmosphere might be receiving its energy from an external source. The sun or filaments in space.

A previous Picture of the Day described gigantic “plasma tornadoes”, or flux tubes, entering Earth’s magnetosphere from space. They were found by the fleet of THEMIS satellites when they flew through the highly charged structures. The tubes are spinning at more than 1.5 million kilometers per hour and generating over 100,000 amps of electric charge flow.

As mentioned before, twisted electromagnetic filaments are the characteristic signature of Birkeland currents (aurora). Electric fields generate magnetic fields. The fields interact with magnetic fields generated by other flows of charge, forming twisted electromagnetic strands named after the one who originally discovered them, Kristian Birkeland. Birkeland currents “pull” with a force that can be orders of magnitude greater than gravity. Electric Universe advocate Donald Scott found that they can attract each other with a force that falls off with the square root of the distance, as opposed to gravity’s square of the distance.

Birkeland currents rotate around each other, beginning as many strands spinning around a common center in pairs. At first, there might be 56 filaments that merge into 28, then 14, and so on. As more current flows through the circuit, there are fewer filaments, but each pair becomes more energetic. Where Birkeland currents intersect, z-pinch compression zones occur, glowing brightly as the plasma density increases, releasing abundant radiation.

Perhaps it is the influx of plasma filaments into Jupiter’s electrically charged environment that creates the Great Red Spot. In an image of Jupiter, several bright points can be seen surrounding the Spot in an arc-shaped array. Below it is another bright arc-shape, indicative of increased infrared radiation emissions.

Rather than vertical flow and convection from below, the Spot is probably rotating because external electromagnetic fields are influencing the atmosphere. The Great Red Spot, as well as other rotating vortices on Jupiter, could be where variable Birkeland currents impinge on the giant gas planet. Those variations would cause changes in the size and activity of the vortices on Jupiter.

### Max.E — 2018-12-20T03:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-full-body-red-light.20495/post-258497>

Hi John.

There is no such thing as a cheap light therapy device. The cheaper the device the less effective it will be. Let me explain.

The 2 most important factors to look at when purchasing a device or creating your own is

1\. Wavelength (nm)
2\. Irradiance (mW/cm2 or J/cm2)

1\. The wavelengths we should all be familiar with e.i. 660/670nm for the red range and 830/850 for the near infrared range.

2\. The next most important factor is the irradiance, this is measured in Joules/cm2 or milliWatts/cm2. What this tells us is the power output per square centimeter. This is important because a low power output, like all cheap devices have, will require far longer exposure time for the same therapeutic effects.

Joovv do a great job explaining this on the link below and have a formula converging mW/cm2 to Joules
[https://joovv.com/blogs/joovv-blog/joovv-versus-other-led-light-therapy-devices](https://joovv.com/blogs/joovv-blog/joovv-versus-other-led-light-therapy-devices)

I have contacted various manufacturers on Alibaba who provide these therapy devices and asked them to provide test results for both wavelengths and irradiance. Let's just say the irradiance results were pitiful and came in at around 1/15 less effective than the joovv.

I have tried to find LED manufacturers who provide high irradiance LEDs and the average price per 1 LED has been about $12. This proves to me that all cheap devices produce a subpar output.

Long story short. What you pay for is what you get

### Sheddie — 2018-12-20T04:10:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-full-body-red-light.20495/post-258500>

Another option. [http://www.lumenphoton.com/](http://www.lumenphoton.com/)
"Cheap" is relative if values like quality, durability, trouble-free... are important and an investment for long-term use.

I've had the largest pads since before 2011, and still going strong. The lights are wired in the pad which is flexible and can comfortably be placed under the body, something the hard box designs don't seem to permit.

A larger, more "full body" mat would be wonderful!

# Thread: Lean Seafood and Additional Fat for Epi-Paleo Ketosis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Technosmith — 2017-11-23T14:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228719>

Hey folks,

There is a 'Keto-coach' called Stephanie Person who, having worked with hundreds of individuals attempting to adapt to a ketogenic diet, is adamant that it requires 200 grams per day of dietary fat to programme the body to adapt to fat burning.

So my question is as follows - Do people tend to supplement additional fat to seafood meals when utilising ketosis in winter due to seafood being relatively lean? If so, how much are you guys adding roughly per meal?

I know Jack suggests to avoid saturated fats with seafood but I'm wary of the quality most olive oils, so do we think animal-based fats could be utilised?

Thanks,

Phil

### kris90 — 2017-11-23T14:54:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228721>

I think chasing a "magic number" is pointless. It's no different than calculating macros, and aiming for targets.

Whatever you don't eat, your body will use somewhere. Ketosis is about fasting, not about shoving as much fat down the hatch as possible, or purposely trying to increase your fat intake. In my experience, I let my leptin receptor account for energy and tell me if I need more fat or not. TBH, I have no idea how many calories or how much fat I consume. I just eat 2 meals a day: one a seafood feast with nuts, the other a high saturated animal fat meal.

I wouldn't purposely try to add fats to reach a target. I would just eat high fat foods, and if adding oils, lard or butters, do it not for reaching a target, but for enhancing the taste of your foods. Being cold adapted naturally gives you a palate that favors fat, so just enjoy your diet.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-23T16:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228728>

Eating lean anything is not a deuterium depleting plan.

### Technosmith — 2017-11-23T17:41:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228731>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=228721)
>
> I think chasing a "magic number" is pointless. It's no different than calculating macros, and aiming for targets.
>
> Ketosis is about fasting, not about shoving as much fat down the hatch as possible, or purposely trying to increase your fat intake.

Hey Kris,

Thanks for that pal. I tend to agree that chasing magic food numbers could be a pointless task.

What you say makes sense and is completely logical but this chick Stephanie has worked with hundreds of people and is adamant that it requires 200 grams per day of dietary fat to programme the body to adapt to fat burning. She seems genuine as well.

Interesting as to why she has found this to be the case.

### Technosmith — 2017-11-23T17:44:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228732>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=228728)
>
> Eating lean anything is not a deuterium depleting plan.

Nice one Jack. But most seafood is naturally pretty lean though. Even farmed salmon is not majorly fatty.

So my point was in regards to supplementing additional fat to the seafood meal, whether it is necessary, and perhaps alternatives to adding processed oils (even olive oil)

### kris90 — 2017-11-23T17:53:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228733>

> [Technosmith said:](/goto/post?id=228731)
>
> What you say makes sense and is completely logical but this chick Stephanie has worked with hundreds of people and is adamant that it requires 200 grams per day of dietary fat to programme the body to adapt to fat burning. She seems genuine as well.
>
> Interesting as to why she has found this to be the case.

Why 200g from food? A lean individual such as myself has 12 lbs of body fat (which is equal to about 5455g). If I simply stop eating, my fat requirements will come from the 5455g I have stored in my tissue.

Eating more fat is not going to magically help you fat adapt any faster. Fasting, low carb, low calorie all do the same thing: allow you to tap into your own fat stores. Pounding more fat is not going to speed that up.

### Sean Waters — 2017-11-23T19:59:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228745>

> [Technosmith said:](/goto/post?id=228719)
>
> Hey folks,
>
> There is a 'Keto-coach' called Stephanie Person who, having worked with hundreds of individuals attempting to adapt to a ketogenic diet, is adamant that it requires 200 grams per day of dietary fat to programme the body to adapt to fat burning.
>
> So my question is as follows - Do people tend to supplement additional fat to seafood meals when utilising ketosis in winter due to seafood being relatively lean? If so, how much are you guys adding roughly per meal?
>
> I know Jack suggests to avoid saturated fats with seafood but I'm wary of the quality most olive oils, so do we think animal-based fats could be utilised?
>
> Thanks,
>
> Phil

Hi Phil...

I cook all my Seafood in liberal fat, fuck that lean shit. Stephanie is probably wrong on her justification, I think we can fat burn in a fasted state without food... but she's right in that adding lots of fat to food is a good thing.

For me this morning was Trout, in about 30-50 grams of butter... Sometimes I add Olive Oil to that.. But today, I had an Orange and Reisling Butter that my local fishmonger whipped me up. It's like Orange Zest blended with butter. It was stunning.

I use liberal amounts of Nut Butter when stir frying with my vegetables on a lot of days. turns them into a satay veg stir fry. So good.

I also eat about 6 eggs cooked in the same amount of butter when I eat them, and about 4 rashes of bacon and 4 sausages. That's probably somewhere around 100grams of fat just for breakfast.

Dinner will be the Trout for example, with vegetables fried in butter/CO and peanut butter/ almond butter/ macadamia butter. That nut butter is frowned upon in this forum for the Omega 6 content (I think!)... But nuts and seeds are also a part of the Epi Paleo RX... I use it liberally anyway and have no issues. Check your own response with these.

If I'm not feeling the nut butter, I melt some 90% dark choc in a pan with butter/ coconut oil, flax seed, stevia, double cream and mix it all up, I either cool it and eat later when it is hard or just spoon it like a crazed 6 year old in a chocolate factory. It's heavy though, so your stomach quickly tells you when you've had enough. But that is literally loaded in fat... Proably 200grams alone.

TL;DR...... YES, ADD FATS LIKE YOU MEAN IT

### kris90 — 2017-11-23T20:27:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lean-seafood-and-additional-fat-for-epi-paleo-ketosis.20497/post-228752>

Sean, you're making me hungry!

The nice thing about being a fat burner and being cold adapted is that calories don't matter. I tend to do the same, always cook with a ton of butter or CO, and I usually eat some raw almond butter with coconut oil and chocolate. I've done the 90% lindt with butter, CO, heavy cream and nut butter in a pan, and let them cool into what looks like Reese Peanut butter cups.

Point is, I do a lot of fasting (18 hrs per day), so I think I'm burning a lot of my own fat regardless of my dietary fat intake. Recycling my protons FTW.

### Sean Waters — 2017-11-23T20:37:11+0200

### kris90 — 2017-11-23T21:06:53+0200

Haha, I'm a HUGE foodie also. But I also love fasting. When I eat, I eat big. When I fast, I get shit done without even thinking about food.

I try to educate people about the "ancient pathway"/cold adaptation/fat burning because once you activate it, you never become a slave to counting calories, and you can get ridiculously ripped while force-feeding yourself high fat foods!  Nobody believes me, yet I'm living proof. I lost 12 lbs since beginning of summer COMPLETELY unintentionally. I even stopped exercising (cuz my sleep is not optimal for now with a newborn), and the weight still keeps coming off. Back in my bodybuilding days, I used to dream of reaching single digit bodyfat % while starving myself and exercising 6 days in the gym, and now I forcefeed myself and maintain 8% bodyfat LOL.

IME, CT is WAY better than bodybuilding workouts for getting ripped.

Her website is riddled with inaccuracies, many of which are patently false. Not a good start for someone selling herself as a "keto coach":

>

>

The more fat you can consume in these early stages, the easier it will be for your brain to make that metabolic shift from carb burning to fat burning as your primary source of fuel.

I can put myself into ketosis in a couple of days without eating a single gram of fat.

### Brent Patrick — 2017-11-26T09:14:39+0200

An indvidual today at work made a negative comment about my Blueblockers I was wearing whilst in front of computer screen..the same indvidual who is on several medications one being Benzo"s for sleep...the same indvidual who walks around with a flat battery and lifeless...the same indvidual who is negative all day and the same individual who is also overweight.

Intersting thing was my awareness and focus on what I consider important, has made unwarranted comments such as this and other indviduals with the same mindset irrelevant.
It is a great feeling to be in a flow state. I am not sure words can really articulate or convey how I feel at this present time and it has been gradually building the past 6 months especially with synchronicity becoming more noticeable.
So as you say Jack focus on whats important and not the things that are not, having higher Dopamine levels has certainly shown me this.

**Have your feelings about your job changed as you progress on this optimal journey?** Yes like Jack has said "you cannot help everyone and I am cool with that" some are just a lost cause ,so I devote energy to those who want to change. So prioritising where my energy and focus goes has been more beneficial to those who need it and has certainly been a big plus for me ,actually I have my enthusiasm back which I did lose there for a few years .I don't leave the Gaol mentally and physically trashed as previous years. Knowing a little more about Dopamine and the importance of building and preserving it as best I can, has given me more than I ever expected becoming a Optimal Klub Member.

I am so proud of you Brent - you work so hard at all of this ......great job for so many reasons!

Thanks for all the help you give me......

Quantum health improved my heteroplasmy and effectively slowed down the inevitable, but I'm under no illusion that it's still out there ticking for when my heteroplasmy drops in the coming decades. Recognizing is not morbid, anxiety-ridden, or scary because it helps me focus more time on people I love and activities that extend my time with them.

# Thread: Glad to meet you all. Nobody else from France  ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glad-to-meet-you-all-nobody-else-from-france.20501/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### baudinus — 2017-11-24T18:36:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glad-to-meet-you-all-nobody-else-from-france.20501/post-228802>

Hello. I am a 57 year old woman.
I have lived several years in Texas and New Hampshire where I raised a family. I came back in the South of France in 2002.
I used to be a teacher here... then a PR in a nuclear company, then a real estate agent. Then I got really fed up with our political system, our modern slavery. The French are often criticized but we live in probably one of the most controlled environment, from birth to death, beginning with our school system and entering it at the age of 2.

Living in the US really opened my mind to the danger of vaccinating, what a real natural birthing process is, all things that you never talk about in France, especially not in 1990s. I ended up not vaccinating, having my last chil at home and with the years grew more and more different from the people I could see everyday.
At that time I met wonderful people (at church) and great female friends and this brought me warmth that I had not shared before. Then I went back and it broke my heart.

Then on December 21, 2012 (not a joke), I made some research on the Sandy Hook Massacre... and the veil opened and my life was turned upside down, realizing I had been lied to for so long.

I sold my business, educated myself on matters of politics, economics, spirituality, history... and moved to an old farm in the centre of the country with my companion where we strive to get back to a more natural rhythm, growing our own food, raising our hens and ducks. We are vegetarians but for a long time I practised blood group and genotype food program. I do not want a salary, I want nothing to do with welfare. I consider myself a sovereign being and will try to live like one which is very difficult here.

About a month ago, I read a very interesting bit on facebook by Dr Kruse and watched the 2017 Vermont video then the 2016 and a lot of things fell into place : why out tomatoes did not ripen at all outside (but did inside !), why chemtrails, why I spent hours facing the sun, why our health was so good, etc...
The next night, there were only candles in our home and I purchased orange glasses.
We sleep even better, are even more relaxed, and love it.
This is where I am just now but I hope to train my unscientific mind to learn about this fascinating subject.

As a good friend of mine, an architect, used to repeat when talking about his job : "YOu know, Pascale, in fact this is very simple, there is only SPACE and LIGHT."

I look forward to reading you and learning and finding new ways of living a more natural and healthy life. Thanks for reading me.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-24T18:40:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glad-to-meet-you-all-nobody-else-from-france.20501/post-228803>

Welcome. Yes we have people in France.

### baudinus — 2017-11-28T17:57:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glad-to-meet-you-all-nobody-else-from-france.20501/post-229019>

Thanks Caroline and Jack.
I am now reading Ott's book which is easy reading and feeling sad that his research kind of stopped in its track, well at least for the greatest number's benefit. He was so hopeful. I noticed the book was never translated in French, if I am not mistaken.

# Thread: Hello & Tammy's first journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-tammys-first-journal.20503/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TammyRae17 — 2017-11-25T03:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-tammys-first-journal.20503/post-228823>

Hello everyone! I've been following Jack Kruse for 2-3 years. I first found this site when I was looking for proof that the sun does not cause cancer. In the last 5 years, I began waking up to the wisdom of nature. For the first time in my life, I began craving the great outdoors & I found that not only did I feel better outside, but I also had deeper insights into various aspects of my life. I currently live in sunny Leander, Texas (very close to Austin). This city is probably one of the fastest growing areas in central Texas and I have recently had health issues pop up...itchy rashes on my stomach and side (eczema? Psoriasis?) as well as digestive problems and weight gain. I also notice that if I have to use my cell phone, my hands start to tingle. I spend at least 15-30 min in the morning sun every day (barefoot, shorts or bikini). I use a black light at night and CT 2-3 times per week. Still, the health issues remain. I worry that the increasing population in Leander is overriding my efforts at improving health. Another HUGE concern I have is that my 13-yr old daughter is attending a public school that REQUIRES laptops. Ugh! I've been urging my husband to consider moving to a less populated place. Until then, what else can I do?

### caroline — 2017-11-25T10:58:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-tammys-first-journal.20503/post-228829>

Hi Tammy and welcome. Have you thought of going to Mexico for a couple of weeks?

Do you have a meter to check nnEMF at your home? workplace? Does your house have a smart meter?

What about camping in the bush for awhile? biohack how you feel.....

What do you eat? seafood? oysters?

What water do you drink?

Do you listen to Jack's podcasts? Patreon blogs?

Are you following Jack's blogs on deuterium?

### taiyang — 2017-11-25T14:36:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-lenses-that-let-in-nir-near-infra-red-light.20504/post-228835>

does anyone have information of materials that are/can be used for making lenses (for glasses) and windows that let in the type of light we want to get in the AM? is that near infra red light? i do the hacks of cracking car windows open in the morning driving to work in cold weather, going out in morning light with my glasses off whenever possible, etc. but i have an intellectual interest in how we can access this healing am light through our eye glasses and windows.

I found this info:
N-BK7Low-Cost Material, Works Well in Visible and NIR Applications
at
[https://www.edmundoptics.com/resour...e-correct-material-for-infrared-applications/](https://www.edmundoptics.com/resources/application-notes/optics/the-correct-material-for-infrared-applications/)

we have glasses to block out blue light, but is it possible to have glasses that block blue light and let in the kind of light made by the am sun? also, a separate but related topic- how can plants grow in green houses/hot houses when the sunlight reaching them has to go through glass or transparent plastic???

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-lenses-that-let-in-nir-near-infra-red-light.20504/post-228861>

You really don't want anything between your eyes and the sun=tarp and it is best for optimal health if you expose your entire body to the morning sun, even if it's only for 15 minutes . # Thread: Hello from the south west of England
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-south-west-of-england.20505/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Seanss — 2017-11-25T15:19:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-south-west-of-england.20505/post-228837>

This introduction may be incredibly lengthy as the work of Dr Kruse has transformed my life in no un-substational form, I have been a lurker on this site for 2 years but recently became a patreon subscriber. I have read jack Kruse's book and i believe i have read 90% of his blog posts, most a few times over. This is the background of my related experiences as far as I understand them, I am however aware that I may have mis-interpreted some of the scenarios.

Background
I was born in a tropical island in the south pacific to bi-racial parents and I am the second of 4 children. I spent the first 10 years of my life there and experienced excellent health, I excelled in both sports and academics and was generally very content as far as I can remember. My father was an electrical engineer and lecturer and my mother was a say at home mother and therefore we lived on a very 'native' like diet. We had no television within the house and therefore we spent nearly every day playing outside however my father did have many electrical items within the house related to his teaching which i would often play with.

Move to the uk
At the age of 10 my father (who was from the uk) decided to move us to the UK so that we could have a 'western eduction'. We moved directly to the south west and I can remember within the first two months of moving here my health started to decline. I remember experiencing asthma and allergy like symptoms for the first time. These symptoms slowly and progressively got worse as time went on and by the age of 13 i had severe excma, mild fatigue, asthma like symptoms and many allergies. I started to spend all my time watching tv and using a computer which at the time had a cathode ray fluorescent screen. I still loved sports however was too easily fatigued so i resorted to playing cricket (a slower paced version of baseball) as it required less cardiovascular exertion. My health continued to decline and by the age of 16 i had incredibly poor digestion and would only pass stools every few days, my nasal respiratory system was always blocked and thus i was a mouth breather, i had terrible concentration, acne and would sleep for 12 hours a day. I was on a very high carbohydrate diet and drank about 3 litres of supermarket milk every day. At this point the house router was also installed in my room and looking back my fatigue increased from that point. I did however remember that my health dramatically improved every time i went camping.

University
At the age of 19 i started university study however spent the much of my first year in my room sleeping as i was constantly tired, i barely drunk alcohol, ate what i thought was a very healthy diet, but had far less energy than my peers. In my second year i started playing american football as it seemed to require far less cardiovascular exertion, i could however tolerate a reasonable amount of high intensity exertion. The working out and being outside that came with it seemed to improve my fatigue somewhat. In my final year i decided to stop playing american football to focus on my studies as i had performed so poorly in my first two years, i was then spending all day infront of my laptop screen.

Chronic Fatigue
A few months in I caught a bad flu and was in bed for two weeks, the next few months my health started to rapidly decline to the point where i could not complete a few of my final exams. I had got to the state where i could barely leave my bed for a few days in a week and therefore had to be picked up from university by family and moved back home. I was diagnosed with 'chronic fatigue syndrome' by my doctor and then spent the next two years completely bed bound for the majority of the time. In the periods of better health i would book a cheap holiday with a family member to a hot country and in such weeks my health would dramatically improve which i found astonishing only to return to such a low level when i came back to the uk.

Research
I then decided to dedicate as much time as I could to researching opinions regarding the symptoms i was experiencing. I spent the next year trying hundred of health reccomendations such as supplements, dietary changes, physical lifestyle changes, psychological methods, all of which had only small or no significant positive effects. I then came across the Weston A Price foundation, who's nutritional advice really didn't bring me much success however they had some information on electromagnetic sensitivity and circadian biology. I then spent time researching these issues and for some reason it took me a year before I found Jack Kruse's blog. When i started reading it so much started to click and make sense, i read for weeks and then decided to make as many relevant changes as possible to my lifestyle.

Changes based on Jack Kruse's work
At this point i was 26 living at my familys home still unable to leave my bed for 4 days a week. The first thing i did was I bought a couple of EMF meters. I then got rid of the wi-fi in my house and gave everybody a hard wired connection either through the router or via power line adapters. I then adopted a low-ish carb and seafood oriented diet. I then moved from the top floor where the RF radiation was significantly higher from surrounding sources to the middle floor on the far end of our end of terrace house. Within the first week of these changes I had noticeably more energy and for the first time in 10 years was able to breath through my nose and thus i was encouraged. The 60hz fields coming from the floor ceiling and walls were very high in this room however, so due to the fact that i had a basic understanding of electromagnetism, i rewired the whole house electrical circuit so that i only had electricity coming into my room on the near wall, none to the lights or through the floorboards, however all other rooms still had the connection (it was a long hard week haha). I then built a drop-leaf wall mounted standing desk that would allow me to stand next to my windows with them open and read/do work when i was able so as to utilise the natural light outside. It also allowed me to have double layer blackout curtains drawn when needed as the table could drop down. I disconnected the boiler which was right underneath my room and let of a large magnetic field and purchased oil filled electric radiators for each room.

These changes alone allowed my health to increase to the point where iw as able to go back and take my final university exams and finish with a first class honours degree. Incredibly fascinated i also made the following changes. I purchased a electrical spectrum analyser, earthed all of my charging plugs and mains electrical devices, purchased a yotaphone which has a e-ink/kindle screen on one side which emits not non-native light. I also created a diy LCD monitor which i removed the backlight from and inserted reflective material and encased it using and old LCD monitor mains housing which was earthed. I therefore can use my mac mini computer or watch tv on a purely reflective monitor that emits no light as it has a hdmi connection. I took an infrared keyboard and mouse and created a DIY firbreoptic connect which means there are no non-native emf or light fields coming off the keyboard/mouse. I then built a 13ft x 13ft outdoor metal shed and created a half-transparent roof and converted it to a gym with a lot of second hand commercial equipment. Due to the transparent roof it stays warm and no artificial lighting is required. I also built and outdoor section next to it with pull up bars and a lifting area etc. My friends have all cancelled their gym membership and utilise mine now haha. I then purchased my dream car which was a BMW 3 series however it had extremely high magnetic fields of 600mg. A friend and i spent two months rewiring the whole system and moving the battery to the engine bay from the boot and got it down to 6mg in the occupants seating areas. I have installed red incandescent lighting wherever possible and purchased a latex (no spring) mattress for my bed. I have also started following a cold thermogenesis regime. I have done numerous other circadian oriented electrical projects that as this is now somewhat of a hobby for me and i love being able to adapt products to reduce their non-native emissions.

My fascination with the electromagnetism of the body and the dramatic health improvements these changes have created have enabled me to gain a position studying a masters course in neuroscience at one of the best universities in the UK which I began in September. As jack has recommended in the past, I did look into a msc in electrical engineering however my undergraduate degree was not specific enough to allow me to gain a position. I would like to thank Jack Kruse for making so much information freely available for so long, and although not all his recommendations have purely positively perceived results, i can say 90% of what ive implemented, recommended on his work is now intrinsic to my every day lifestyle and thinking and i would consider at least 60% of these changes as life transforming.

Although so much of my health has dramatically improved and thus so has my standard of living, I still unfortunately have quite bad allergies and asthma like reactions to chemical smells and high cardiovascular exercise, I have searched for as much as I can on these issues and understand that they may slowly improve as I continue to function under a more native-like EMF environment. However, if Jack or anybody else can point me towards any research or advice that might be helpful in eradicating asthma like reactions to smells i would be very grateful.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-25T19:27:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-south-west-of-england.20505/post-228849>

The smells are EMF waves too......and it appears you have an allergy to EMF's. I personally believe that all AI's are tied to this mechanism via receptor biology changes of how electrons and protons work. Luca Turin work in this area might really interest you.

nnEMF ruins our instincts because it ruins out ability to decipher the ciphers in nature's wave forms. This creates an allergy which really is a change in the signal to noise ratio in our ability to decipher information.
The unseen enemy is always the most fearsome because you cannot prepare for its consequences. I think your story above is a perfect example of this. And the corollary is that the unseen benefits in nature are always discounted by man because we experience life in it is linear forms while the manner in which it is built all manifest from its non linear behaviors. This is why I say anyone who says grounding and blue light are not important to the living state is someone you need to distance yourself from. The same is true for living up high in the ionsophere where most of nnEMF is aimed. The same is true for the effect of power gird radiations. They have to be closer than comfort because of our technology addiction brought to us by the change of our economy from industrial to the service side. No one sees these connective threads to our demise. They are only an expert in ignorance in broadcasting their linear thoughts about what is behind our demise. That is usually tied to food exercise or detoxing from some chemicals which is effeictively nonsense in my opinion. I am always open to these ideas when the evidence is strong. So far for me, nothing trumps the effect of nnEMF on the living state.. We need to surround ourselves with people who have attuned their senses to the non linear waves around us, that speak to our intuition and instincts so that we once again might cipher nature's music to us.

### caroline — 2017-11-26T05:51:40+0200

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-25T18:05:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-felt-like-youre-talking-but-nobody-is-listening.20507/post-228843>

[https://www.ted.com/talks/julian\_treasure\_how\_to\_speak\_so\_that\_people\_want\_to\_listen/discussion](https://www.ted.com/talks/julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_people_want_to_listen/discussion)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-25T18:13:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-felt-like-youre-talking-but-nobody-is-listening.20507/post-228844>

When your job hinges on how well you talk to people, you learn a lot about how to have conversations — and that most of us don't converse very well. Celeste Headlee has worked as a radio host for decades, and she knows the ingredients of a great conversation: Honesty, brevity, clarity and a healthy amount of listening. In this insightful talk, she shares 10 useful rules for having better conversations. "Go out, talk to people, listen to people," she says. "And, most importantly, be prepared to be amazed."

[https://www.ted.com/talks/celeste\_headlee\_10\_ways\_to\_have\_a\_better\_conversation](https://www.ted.com/talks/celeste_headlee_10_ways_to_have_a_better_conversation)

# Thread: Hello from New England
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-england.20509/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inna — 2017-11-25T23:43:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-england.20509/post-228856>

Hello, my name is Inna. I live in Mass, North Shore of Boston,

I am so very grateful for having found Jack Kruse! I discovered that i am a mitochondrac!

I was born in St. Petersburg Russia, and I have been fortunate to grow up in a family that appreciated and marveled our connection to nature. And thus staying connected to our instinct - since we are part of that nature.
When I was 7 I was diagnosed with chronic glomerulonephritis, and my parents were told that disease would progress to cripple me eventually kill me in early 20. I did not feel sick at all though!
Since young age i would sneak out of the house to the lake in the country and swim in ice cold water and I came to love it, i was spending time outside on every occasion, no matter the temperature was outside.
Never in my life did I wear a sunscreen, neither did my parents. I loved saunas and particularly how my body felt after immersion into the ice cool pool of water right after sauna - and going back and forth each time.
I called that feeling - “born again”.
My glomerulonephritis disappeared without a trace whereas common medical wisdom at the time considered that the disease was doomed to relentless progression.
I was very curious about the workings of the human body, so I became a doctor. I thought I would know more as i learned medicine, however what struck me the most was how little of workings of the body we actually understood.
I was a good student, and I trained as a pathologist after graduating medical school, wanting to get to the root of problems.
I married and had 2 girls, in my early 20s.
I was in my late 20s, when we immigrated to the States. I did my medical training - internal medicine and pulmonary/critical care in Boston and started practicing pulmonary medicine in New Hampshire in late 90s. My biggest frustration was (and is) that what I learned in my training did very little to help my patients. I managed to stay fairly healthy despite stress of medical training and medical practice, though I must admit that from time to time I would descend into - what Jack calls “low dopamine state”.

I discovered that cold water ocean swimming - Atlantic Ocean May through Nov would make huge difference in my energy level and general sense of well-being. And so did Sauna. In a place called Kripalu (Lenox, MA) i discovered sauna that was as hot as I had in Russia, and to my delight water in the ice pool was actually ice cold - like i had in Russia. Needless to say, I was the only person using the ice pool there.
I have a portable far infra red sauna which i use (nearly daily) before bedtime, it is rather crappy one, but i still love to use it particularly in winter.
I exercise a lot, always enjoyed moving, The missing link for me was ketogenic diet and once I incorporated it into my lifestyle several month ago, I feel absolutely great.
I was delighted to find Jack Kruse through the keto connection. After learning I changed timing of my meals according to Jack's leptin prescription, rearranged my work schedule to get maximal sun exposure and changed timing of workout from morning to late afternoon (and to outside).

My hope is to be able to better help my patients;

After reviewing this stuff and realizing it’s validity, and also finding validation in what I was doing for myself, I decided to join the tribe!

I have to admit that my knowledge of physics, particularly quantum physics, light, magnetism, is rather rudimentary. Till now, I paid very little attention to blue light, EMF, etc. I am much better in biochemistry, since we learned it in medical school.

Often I have a feeling that what Jack is talking about is way above my head. However I am driven by thirst for knowledge and understanding and strong desire to be of service to my patients. I love the idea of teaching people “how to fish” so they could learn and excel in the art of Self Care.
Having gained mastery of self care and having experienced the ecstasy of feeling connected to nature and being vibrantly alive I would love to share that-state! It is like when I swim in the ice cold water in Atlantic Ocean in November on a day when the air is unusually warm and experience sun radiance on my face and the sun rays dancing in the water and myself feeling fully alive, I think of people who express amazement that I am doing such a thing, as “those people don’t know what they are missing”!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-25T23:51:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-england.20509/post-228858>

Welcome. Nature is not over your head. If lions and hippo's can manage it so can you.

# Thread: former paleo success story now dealing with sarcopenia/poor sleep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-11-26T14:15:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228878>

_hi fellow sun worshippers and hopefully potential helper-encourager-colleagues_. back in 2009 (44 yrs old) i discovered art de vany via nicholas nassim taleb. using de vany's techniques- carb restriction and once a week fasted (plus blackcoffee) exercise lifting/sprinting, i went from a size 12/14 to a size 6 in 3 months . i kept the weight off for 3 years. then at around 47 or 48 i started to gain. i was still pretty strict with carb restriction, but i was having worse and worse hot flashes. **the worst were the nights i did the fasted exercise** that had previously helped me lose weight/gain muscle. i had to abandon the fasted morning exercise. now i am 52. i just stopped my period literally a month ago. since about 48 yrs old i starting having a lot of visible loss in muscle tone in my forearms and calves. my sleep is better (but still have nights of slight insomnia/hot flashes at least 3x a week) because i have to be very strict with blue light exposure. since i have been strict with my blue light restriction with sunset, i no longer have severe hot flashes with headaches _EXCEPT if i try to do heavy fasted exercise in the am._ my clothes size is about an 8, but my abdomen and trunk are really getting bigger.

**i feel like i had the secret in paleo and have now lost it!!!!!!!! now restricting blue light is my new secret, but i need more than that to lose abdominal weight**. both parents are/were diabetic. mother U4a diagnosed with diabetes in her 50s. went totally to insulin within 5 years. father is diagnosed with type 2 (still alive at 81. can still drive and does light farm work), just takes 2 metformin but has tooth loss and now wears dentures. he is in remission from colon cancer. i had colon polyps removed at 42. paternal grandfather died at 94 with colon cancer. i go in every 5 years for a colonoscopy. the last one i did without anesthesia as i would like to protect my brain. mother died from metastatic breast cancer at 78. i had an A1c of 5.9 in 2008. i lost 15 pounds in 2009 and had an a1c of 5.7 with paleo and some fruit (130 pounds). i cut out the fruit, regained some weight to 138 lbs. last a1c 5.1. for me weight loss is NOT the key to better blood sugar- carb restriction is. in the summer i have found that i can eat more carbs and still have good bg control. i am not insulin resistant at all. my fasting insulin is in the 3's. i have the TCF7L2 snp that has been the most highly associated with the development of type 2 diabetes. people with this snp are insulin sensitive. they don't produce enough insulin to deal with the amount of carbs eaten by most people. also snps in sert transporters and vdr's. oops!!!! forgot to mention that i live in new jersey. starting 2 summers ago with the advent of my husband's dopamine-boosting pokemon obsession/positive addiction, i spend 4-5 hours in the sun walking around. no sunscreen and no burning. seldom burned in the past anyway. lots of moles. signing off.

### taiyang — 2017-11-27T12:28:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228933>

Thanks, Richelle and SlamStack for replying. i absolutely believe in the power of natural light and the dangers of excess artificial light. i have seen these factors work in my life before i knew of any of the information here: when i lived in a tropical country for years and ate lots of rice carbs, i was thin without trying. when i started monitoring my blood sugar in 2012 after i found out i had a genetic vulnerability for diabetes (i was not overweight and my fasting blood sugar was always good, it was testing 1 and 2 hours after meals that uncovered problems), i found that i could handle more carbs in summer.

however, i don't agree with everything jack writes if it doesn't jive with my experience. i also know he doesn't have time to argue with me. he has also written that he will practice tough love when it comes to people who detract from his message. i've already been banned from another fb group because i guess i was perceived as an energy vampire/message disruptor.

i will post one thing jack writes that i disagree with:
"What happens if you are deficient in UV and IR and get too much blue light at night? You get a form of diabetes." i come from a farm family- we are like throw backs to another age in some ways. my father has diabetes which was diagnosed 30 years ago when there was just tv and incandescent lighting. he was/is always up at the crack of dawn. he has no problems getting natural light. his grandmother also had diabetes. i believe you need a genetic vulnerability for diabetes to manifest itself. also jack writes that the problem with diabetes is insulin resistance, which means that the receptors can not respond to the insulin signal- they are deaf to it usually because of over production of insulin. this also does not jive with my experience. i am highly insulin sensitive. i am insulin deficient; i don't over produce insulin. my receptors are working well.

i'll be interested to see if jack thinks that what i write detracts from his message. also, being from a farm family, i know that seasonal veggies stop with the first frost. that means that starting sometime in november to maybe april/may, i should be eating NO plant matter. my paternal grandparents who lived to 94 did not live on that kind of diet. they had a 'root cellar'- in their cool basement they kept produce that could keep a long time without refrigeration- potatoes, etc. my grandmother also canned a lot of stuff and put it in the cellar. growing up the 3 generations worked together on the farm. we lived just down the road from my grandparents, so i saw their lifestyle close up and asked a lot of questions about their lives when they were younger.

Never heard that diabetes can be genetic so won't comment on that part - Sounds interesting though
But what he is referring to in that statement is not directly diabetes
There are a few intermediate steps that you need to look into before putting that statement down
Leptin resistance is the thing that happens before you get diabetes (see the first blog posts)
Blue light causes LR that is the short explanation to his statement

Of course this might chance if you can get diabetes from genetics

>

Hi taiyang! Could you please share more about your paternal grandparents environment that they lived in on a daily basis? What was their exposure to RF and nnemf like and location, population density, cell towers, wifi, etc.? I'm curious because the environments that our grandparents lived and thrived in is far, far different from the ones that most live in now. Thanks!

### Inger — 2017-11-27T14:34:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228939>

well, what comes into my mind is this;

Was your grandfather sans clothes outside? Or was he normally clothed? Was he barefoot?

I totally believe poeple can get diabetes even if they do not have much nnEMF hen they have clothes on all the time outside, no tanning naked, and using shoes all the time.... and also eating unatural carbs (potatoes and rice) they do not grow wild in nature and are heavily cultivated, as are fruit. IMHO these are not really health foods for anyone. If you are genetically sensible to get diabetes, such things sure might bring you there.

Populations that were hunter gatherers, and people who live almost naked in the sun barefoot, sure will not get diabetes.. I am pretty sure of that....

Many people in the south and strong sunshine that have moved away from the natural tribe life into agriculture, get diabetes eating lots of corn and cultivated carbs. But they use clothes too, and shoes. And all those carbs.

### taiyang — 2017-11-27T18:15:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228952>

I focus in on the fact that some people have genetic vulnerabilities maybe that's where my biggest difference with Jack lies you have two people with similar genetic backgrounds living in the exact same environment and one person will develop diabetes and the other won't

### Lahelada — 2017-11-27T22:19:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228961>

Taiyang,read up on Dr Wallace. That is the part that you are missing. MTDNA can be affected by a lot of things.I could tell you a story of a friend of mine who lived on a farm without electricity, tropical, carb heavy diet. Physically active, but no heavy labour. Moved to the city , still physically active,kept the same diet. T2D within 2 years of arriving in the big city! Heavy mobile and screen user. Two sisters moved with her and also went off the rails healthwise. Mother "well" but had 12 children..

Re your grandparents, environment is not only modern forces. Trauma,childbirth ( how many children did their mother have) malnutrition by the mother, the child thereafter.

May I suggest that you have not got enough information yet to challenge what is being presented to you. You define genetic as nuclear ,while the genetics and epigenetics mostly referrred to here are mitochondrial DNA. Your last statement re exact same environment is also very broad. Example, two neighbouring houses, one has a smartmeter fitted, you have just changed the environment. One marries a smoker the other does not,one works shift the other does not.yet they are two neighbours same age,went to school together but one is sick the other not. You need to read up on Doug Wallace and the etiology of diseases to get where Dr K is coming from.

Another thing about insulin resistance. You are quoting incorrectly. This is from Leptin Chapter One :
"The only difference between type 2 and type 1.5 is the time is shorter and the immune response much greater. Again, all mediated by leptin resistance. The chronic leptin elevation leads to eventual leptin resistance and usually occurs 5-7 years before someone becomes insulin resistant! (Type 2 DM) Today’s medicine focuses in on insulin to treat diabetes. In my view, this is completely off target. It makes no sense to treat [insulin resistance](http://blog.kettleandfire.com/how-to-reverse-insulin-resistance/) after it has occurred. It makes more sense to target leptin resistance because it occurs 5-7 years before insulin resistance occurs!" [https://www.jackkruse.com/chapter-one-on-leptin/](https://www.jackkruse.com/chapter-one-on-leptin/)

From Why you need to think for yourself :

"When I was on Jimmy Moore’s podcast this May, I estimated that the numbers in the USA for type two diabetes were north of 150 million people. The reason behind my beliefs is that the way in which we diagnose type two diabetes is outdated. By the time a patient is diagnosed via our current clinical methods they have already destroyed 50% of their beta cells that make insulin."
[https://www.jackkruse.com/why-you-need-to-think-for-yourself/](https://www.jackkruse.com/why-you-need-to-think-for-yourself/)

### taiyang — 2017-11-27T23:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228966>

lahelada, I have only seen 2 Wallace videos. would you agree that some people are more sensitive to the poor environment that can lead to type 2 diabetes? just as some people are more sensitive to nnEMF? if yes, i can live with that.

### Lahelada — 2017-11-28T00:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228971>

I cannot agree with you because of the terminology you use. Sensitive because of level of heteroplasmy yes, sensitive because of nuclear genetics,no. Sensitive because of previous poor environments and choices I agree with you but even that is affected by whether your mitochondrial line is good or not. And do not forget that the pathways to disease are different for the different haplotypes.
A different argument , if the disease was congenital it would not be reversible. Even type 1 diabetes has recently been shown to be reversible. Although, if you believe that the underlying problem is really an energy deficit then you can only call it a reversal not a cure. [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/a...iotic-pill-rewires-body-scientists-claim.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2937602/Both-types-diabetes-CURED-daily-probiotic-pill-rewires-body-scientists-claim.html)

### Inna — 2017-11-28T03:48:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228982>

Might consider taking dhea, pg:e ratio is likely low, might consider checking it and supplementing progesterone

### taiyang — 2017-11-28T11:51:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-228994>

i do whatever shows me results regardless of explanation of etiology. having said that, i appreciate your explanation. i speculate that there are snps that might affect the mtDNA. otherwise, why is jack interested in knowing about certain snp's in the individual?? here is some info on my snp that shows not all of T2DM is caused by over-secretion of insulin: "The _TCF7L2_ gene is strongly associated with T2D, **impaired insulin secretion**,[4](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4629960/#b4-dddt-9-5835),[14](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4629960/#b14-dddt-9-5835)–[16](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4629960/#b16-dddt-9-5835) and cardiovascular risk,[17](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4629960/#b17-dddt-9-5835) encoding for a transcription factor involved in Wnt (wingless and INT-1) signaling.[18](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4629960/#b18-dddt-9-5835)..." [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4629960/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4629960/)

some questions that i haven't discovered the answer to so far in my reading and video watching:
1) how do you know what your rate of heteroplasmy is?
2) can we know which of our tissues has a high rate of heteroplasmy?
3) how do we lower our rate of heteroplasmy?

thanks to whomever has the time and knowledge to answer the above questions. i am trying to learn all i can and i appreciate every reply i get
PS I just found [https://www.mitomap.org/MITOMAP](https://www.mitomap.org/MITOMAP) and have to find out how to use it. i also have to search this site to see if anything has been written about MITOMAP

### Inna — 2017-11-28T13:47:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-229002>

The question is where you put your energy. There is not much you can do about you genetics, inherited dna structure, however less than 5% of our dna is actually expressed.
It is your environment that influences such an expression, hence focussing on environment is your point of leverage. Gives you most result for your effort.
The term Environment is broad and include your thinking and attitudes, foods you eat, location and place where you live, what you do and don't do, people you interact with, etc

### taiyang — 2017-11-29T12:45:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-229103>

i am reading up more on what wallace has written about mtDNA: "Alteration in mitochondrial bioenergetics would increase mtDNA damage and mutation rate, perturb mtDNA replication and mitophagy, and lead to the accumulation of somatic mtDNA mutations. This would result in the progressive decline in mitochondrial function with age. Cells that are sufficiently energetically impaired would malfunction and ultimately undergo apoptosis and necrosis. Thus, the accumulation of somatic mtDNA mutations becomes the aging clock in individuals born with a normal mitochondrial function."
\*\*\*i have no problem shifting my focus from variations in nDNA to mtDNA. my haplogroup U is thought to have come into existence before the development of agriculture. also, high concentration of my sub clade U4a are found in the far far north.
it seems the focus of this site is to directly address restoring a normal state of mitochondrial bioenergetics in an effort to lower the proportion of mutant mitochondria. i find that my nDNA and my mtDNA seem to work in concert at least when it comes to the TCF7L2 gene snp, which is associated with reduced insulin secretion. if my mtDNA ancestors were hunter-gatherers and not farmers, they perhaps did not develop the ability to secrete the levels of insulin seen today in people with mtDNA's more reflective of farming populations. Further, if i eat higher carb-levels than farmer mtDNAs can eat , i will see higher levels of blood sugar that take longer to dissipate. this difference in the ability to handle carbs (even at normal body weight and not type 1) is what i see when i compare my bg to my Chinese husband's when we eat the same amount of carbs. there is a big, consistent difference, not a subtle one.

### Lahelada — 2017-11-29T13:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-229104>

"When we starve, we use our own fat stores to make ketones, which act to drop NADH levels, raise NAD+ levels, because we are using protons liberated from fat stores. We are also feeding electrons from those fat stores into FADH2 and electrons at cytochrome 2. So this simply explains why non-native EMFs make you think you need carbs, so the vagus nerve creates a carbohydrate craving response in your brain by raising a chemical called neuropeptide Y. These chronic actions also stimulate mitochondria that can no longer make superoxide well. We call this heteroplasmy. We can handle some heteroplasmy but as its percentage rises there becomes a critical mass where a mitochondria must be replaced because ECT is too slow. We need a superoxide burst to make this signal. In diabetics this signal is lost. Their tissues are forced to work with defective mitochondria and ECT remains forever slowed. When you do not understand this quantum dance you believe that foods, specifically carbohydrates cause diabetes when the reality is that they just amplify the signals in bad mitochondria. Slow ECT is what is behind most metabolic diseases and diseases of aging. The percentage of heteroplasmy in a cell is far important than the foods we eat. Lowering our carbohydrate burden helps but we have to add back UV light from the sun because this can help us regain our superoxide burst."

About SNPs
[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1646870818710599](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1646870818710599)

I think the answer as to why you handle carbs less well lies more in the above. In your picture you are weraing galsses.If they are glasses for myopia you may well not get enough sun,even if you take sun. The eyes have it !Just a suggestion

### taiyang — 2017-11-29T18:32:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-229132>

what reduces my carb craving is eating lower carb. blue light is also related to lowering my carb craving because when I lower exposure to Bluelight, I sleep better and my carb craving is reduced. To test whether non-native EMF is influencing my carb craving I can make no conscious effort to reduce carbs while at the same time lowering my non-native EMF exposure as much as possible and then see what the effect is on my carb craving. Have you done a similar experiment? did lowering non-native EMF affect your craving for carbs?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/former-paleo-success-story-now-dealing-with-sarcopenia-poor-sleep.20512/post-229142>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=229132)
>
> did lowering non-native EMF affect your craving for carbs?

Lowering emf and increasing natural light reduced my craving for food, carbs included.

This study pretty much summed it up for me way back:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3184892/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3184892/)

48 participants had increased brain glucose metabolism when exposed to an active cell phone. I'm thinking, even on a basic first order level, if the brain is pushed into burning through glucose faster it's going to require more glucose sooner than it would have otherwise.

This was a well conducted experiment and the scientist has described it really well. If you are detail oriented it's probably worth a youtube search.

### shiran — 2017-11-27T15:44:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-broth-dha-curcum-root.20516/post-228943>

Today I went to the port in the old city of Acre I bought a fresh white grouper and fresh shrimp..
The head and the bones of the fish as well as the shrimp scallop with the head and tails plus :
Carrot
Onion
Garlic a lot
Fresh curcum root
Dried Lemon grass
Fresh Herbs
Ginger
Hop pepper
Selery
Black pepper
Zaafran
Fresh Lemon
Bokchoy

And lots of love that is what you see in the pic
I'll filter everything out and to the broth I'll add coconut cream and fish meat
That's our dinner for today
Bon appetite

Can you see the eye in the pic ?

# Thread: ME and where to start
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/me-and-where-to-start.20518/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Goldenboy — 2017-11-27T18:37:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/me-and-where-to-start.20518/post-228955>

Hi Jack and all -

My main question is to all those on this forum with ME or similar. I’d love to hear from anyone, who could help me in where to get started, what to read first and what tests I might need.

My health history

I have been diagnoses with ME, which here is related to metabolic syndrome. I have read Jack’s book, some of which I have/had started to implement and then had a downturn in health after stopping smoking first tobacco and then cannabis in spring and summer respectively this year.

I was prescribed a benzodiazepine drug for the cannabis , which affected me badly (i’m hyper sensitive to medication) and I have only recently after a very slow tail off become free of them. I was suffering mentally quite badly and also started to drink heavily (i had not been drinking very much from around April to August) as this was the only thing in my weakened state that I knew would stop me feeling like i was going insane and no, i’m not an advocate for that as a solution long term

I have tried and failed many times to find a solution to ME and have started treatments and often ended up being sicker. Moving to Ibiza has helped enormously; there’s a lot of sunshine here, and that has always lifted me.

I can feel very overwhelmed quickly; i take on a lot and then can’t cope. I’m keen to learn from Jack’s approach, though have to say that the volumes of info scare me. It’s not a lack of intelligence, it’s simply because i feel overwhelmed.

Quantum protocols

-   Moved to Ibiza, 39 degrees latitude

-   Avoid blue light in the evenings and protect against it when using computers with amber gaming glasses; orange tinted glasses used at night

-   No Wi-Fi at home, though 3 mornings a week at work, I am exposed to it

-   CT: cold showers daily; regular dips in the sea (2-3 times weekly) for up to 30 mins at a time. Getting ready to do a Wim Hof video course. Cold adaptation is growing well

-   High protein, high fat, diet - not totally ketogenic: grains have crept back (working to eliminate again)

-   Making a lot more effort to spend time with friends

-   Daily meditation and qi gong exercises in my schedule

Drug history:

-   Prozac 2 x periods of one year (prescribed for ME) - stopped

-   Ecstasy, cocaine, cannabis amd and tobacco - all ceased

-   Lorezapam - took for 3 months, just stopped

Energy levels

I feel like a battery not holding onto charge. LOVE the sun, always have, but when it’s gone, so has my energy.

Grey days (of which there are many in England can make me feel immediately low in energy and mood.

Have always felt the best mentally and energetically when in water and in the sun - I especially love being in water and find it hard to get out.

Food

I seem to be intolerant of many food stuffs and gas is a serious issue, frequent and often foul smelling after e.g. wine, beer, any type of bean and sometimes it seems just about every food.

Little control when I start to drink alcohol - did control it but now it’s outta control, has a rapid effect in my brain

Sensitivities

-   Sounds: feeling of frequency change, high pitch noise that no-one else can hear. Recently started with tinnitus, in my case, a humming sound in my ears

-   Smells, especially petrol, perfume

-   People - I pick upon other people’s moods and feelings very quickly and easily

-   Cold: esp, hands, feet and backs of arms and cold generally EXCEPT when I have a cold shower or do CT, this regulates me a lot

Exercise

Never really done a lot of sport. Always very fatigued after I did sport, even at school, though think that this started to get really bad from around age 32. A single gym session would have me tired for days and if sessions sustained over a 2-week period, i am bedridden for 2x weeks.

Pain

Muscles fatigue easily. I can feel tiredness in muscles after a short brisk walk.

Whenever i start to feel ill, I always have lower back pain. Suffer from an annual recurrent knee pain (feels like knee ligaments very weak also); usually lasts for 5-6 weeks then disappears.

Geography

Lived near electricity generator in London

Also, lived in Marylebone, London, which I found out to be one of, if not THE most poluted part of the capital.

Other symptoms of note

-   Have constant thirst and pee lots, much more than anyone drinking the same amount of liquid as me. Always going to the loo to pee.

-   Sharp sudden stabbing pains in the head

-   Attention span very short

-   Feel palpitations as if heart beating heavily

-   Disturbed sleep, rarely refreshing

Health chronology

Age 4

hospitalised with a lymph gland issue.

Age 5

Measles

Age 6

Got chickenpoxAged 10

Psoriasis around lip area for around a year

Aged 12

Glandular fever reported

Parents split up, making me very introspective after having just attained a big level of popularity at school

Age 14

Started to have protracted periods off school, two weeks at a time, feeling dizzy and flu like - PERIODS LIKE THIS HAVE CONTINUED TO PRESENT DAY.

As well as ME ‘attack’ when my system shut down dramatically, being sick for long periods of at least 2 weeks at a time occurs 2-3 times a year

Age 24

Cold sores started appearing - outbreaks have occurred 6-7 times a year up until the last couple of years when I have limited drugs and alcohol and improved diet so have had 1-2 outbreaks. Each outbreak knocks me out energy wise for the first two days

Age 25

First started noticing that going to the gym fatigued me dramatically

Age 37

First ME collapse - duration, around 6 months. Very severe with the following symptoms: very low energy; tender lymph glands; severe disorientation; severe dizziness on standing; diarrhea, easily fatigued; sleep hard to achieve with frequent waking in the night, being unable to sleep for 2-3 hours, slept a lot (and easily) during the day . Symptoms would relieve in the evening.

Prescribed prozac

Age 40

Second ME collapse

Age 42

Third ME collapse

Age 45

I stopped supplements a while ago because I seemed to get either sleepy or very hyper. Lately I have been feeling worse because of more clouds and a weak sun along with gallbladder pain. Well I added DGL for gallstones pain and it works. Next was k2/k4 500mcg sublingual melts and ubiquinol 50 mgs at night started 2 nights ago after I eat, well it is helping my muscle and joint pain so far, I felt a difference the first night. I know ubiquinol makes me feel warm. No sure what the vit k should do if it's helps being regular in the bathroom. .yep. lol sorry, I take those at night because I am not sure how they will react. The other is L-glutamine. I want to try taking vit D again but it made me sleepy and my joints would hurt the next day, and yes I tried different brands with vit k without vit k and with magnesium as well. As for multivitamin and multi b's I get very hyper and crave carbs and broke out with acne so stopped them, but thinking of starting them again but would like know why I react like that to vit d, multi's.
