### JanSz — 2018-05-15T15:41:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-241229>

62.99+24.99= 87.98

For $87.98 I can have 6 liters of Highland Spring Still Natural Spring Water
After I freeze it once there will be less than 6 liters, but it will have 139.6ppm
87.98/6=14.6633 $/liter

I tested my tap water and it have 150.2ppm (assume that it cost nothing)
Qlarivia cost $8.52 per 1/2 liter bottle, shipping included, so $17.04 per liter, it was tested to have much less that its nominal 25 ppm, but assume 25ppm
If I take 1L of Qlarivia and mix it with X liters of tap water I will get (X+1) liters of mix that have 139.6ppm (for the total cost of $17.04)
(X+1)=11.81 liters
17.04/11.81=1.4428 $/liter
Highland Spring Still Natural Spring Water is
14.6633/1.4428=10.16308567
10x more expensive than Qlarivia

[https://www.amazon.com/Highland-Spr...words=Highland+Spring+Still+Water+12+X+500+Ml](https://www.amazon.com/Highland-Spring-Still-Natural-6x500ml/dp/B0111ZIBTY/ref=sr_1_fkmr0_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1526386334&sr=8-1-fkmr0&keywords=Highland+Spring+Still+Water+12+X+500+Ml)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-241334>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=241229)
>
> 62.99+24.99= 87.98
>
> For $87.98 I can have 6 liters of Highland Spring Still Natural Spring Water
> After I freeze it once there will be less than 6 liters, but it will have 139.6ppm
> 87.98/6=14.6633 $/liter
>
> I tested my tap water and it have 150.2ppm (assume that it cost nothing)
> Qlarivia cost $8.52 per 1/2 liter bottle, shipping included, so $17.04 per liter, it was tested to have much less that its nominal 25 ppm, but assume 25ppm
> If I take 1L of Qlarivia and mix it with X liters of tap water I will get (X+1) liters of mix that have 139.6ppm (for the total cost of $17.04)
> (X+1)=11.81 liters
> 17.04/11.81=1.4428 $/liter
> Highland Spring Still Natural Spring Water is
> 14.6633/1.4428=10.16308567
> 10x more expensive than Qlarivia
>
>
> [https://www.amazon.com/Highland-Spr...words=Highland+Spring+Still+Water+12+X+500+Ml](https://www.amazon.com/Highland-Spring-Still-Natural-6x500ml/dp/B0111ZIBTY/ref=sr_1_fkmr0_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1526386334&sr=8-1-fkmr0&keywords=Highland+Spring+Still+Water+12+X+500+Ml)
>
> [View attachment 7579](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/7579/)

I think before freezing highland spring is around the 146.3 ppm mark, and I'd guesstimated seasonal variation on my well water at 148 to (worst case scenario)-150 ppm based on my location. So the first freeze drop of highland spring to 139.6 is interesting. Hints at my well water after a first freeze at 142-144. With everything else I'm doing to deplete deuterium, that first freeze was enough to trigger a positive change. I can buy highland spring from a national supermarket local to me for £3.00 for 12 x 500 ml. Bought in larger bottles it's even cheaper, so I'll hack a 2 hour 1 litre freeze and see what volume of 139.6 water I get, and how much it costs. But I reckon pence/cents versus £/$ per litre.  Might be useful for anyone in the UK who wants to hack it, who can pick the water up cheaply. For my own n=1, I can also hack if dropping the ppm to under 140 makes a difference.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-05-19T07:05:31+0300

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=241218)
>
> I also had a serious reduction in thirst after my first DDW-protocol (currently on my second, very mild protocol). I only drink water when I'm thirsty, a year or two ago I could drink 2 bottles of San Pellegrino a day. Right now it's a lot if I drink one bottle. Of course it depends on activity, but by and large I can drink less water.
>
> Thinking out loud here: the body tries to remove deuterium up to a point (probably differs by person/location). When intake of deuterium is high, the body will have plenty of methods to try to reduce that (urination, sweating, breathing, etc.). Likewise, when intake of deuterium is lowered (DDW and/or high fat diet), the body won't use those methods that would dehydrate a person, because there is less need for deuterium depletion. Lastly, it can take months or years for that change to become apparent, in other words it doesn't happen overnight.

Walter it is interesting you mention the reduction in DDW consumption ,I have noticed the same especially being in Autumn and soon heading into winter in Australia. Having reduced Carbs due to lack of strong UV , my need to urinate especially at night has become less and my water intake is reduced as well. My Energy levels are what really stand out since drinking **QLARIVIA** and faster recovery after exercise is noticeable the past two weeks.

"... many pollutants can cause swelling and oedema by actively reducing levels of the body's natural diuretic hormone, vasopressin, (ADH, anti diuretic hormone). The less chemical damage the body has, the higher the level of vasopressin and the less excess fluid your body will carry. " etc etc etc

Therapeutically, the source of X could be very important.

### Sajid Mahmood — 2018-05-22T13:01:05+0300

Mitocondriac’s Guide to Water:
[http://journeytooptimalhealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/Mitocondriacs-guide-to-water-BJ-v2.pdf](http://journeytooptimalhealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/Mitocondriacs-guide-to-water-BJ-v2.pdf)

**
Asia**

Qomolangma Glacier Mineral Water
Source: 6800m> Tibet Plateau
D2O = 12 to 25 PPM

Tibet 5100 Glacier Mineral Water
Source: 5100m> Tibet Plateau
D2O = 50 to 90 PPM

**Europe**

Sno Glacier Mineral Water
Source: Iceland Galcier Eyjafjallajokull
D2O = 85 to 125 PPM

Iluliaq
Source: Greenland Ice Cap
D2O = 100 to 135 PPM

**North America**

Berg
Source: Canada Arctic Sea Iceberg’s
D2O = 110 to 140 PPM

Jackson Spring
Source: Canada Manitoba
D2O = 100 to 135 PPM

### Sue-UK — 2018-05-22T16:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-241770>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=241748)
>
> So what you're saying is that certain tap water should be avoided when mixing with DDW?

Fortunately few UK water authorities add fluoride, but '_EC limits for pesticides'_ is a red flag on its own, because - particularly if I had cancer - I want zero, or as close to zero as I can reasonably get. I don't know if US or Australian limits are similar, but if the dose considered safe is based on an average that healthy people can drink and not get sick, if someone is already sick, its a different ballgame. At best it seems the contaminants are just more shit the body has to deal with. Redox/heteroplasmy alert.

25ppm DDW to bring 150 ppm water down to 105 or 85ppm would mean more tap water contaminants than using 105 or 85ppm DDW. These contaminants may not be deuterium, but I think they have the potential to adversely affect a therapeutic outcome if they _act_ like deuterium.

"MPs enter soils via diffuse aeolian transport.", meaning by wind.

### JanSz — 2018-06-14T16:17:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-243384>

**Freezing as a method of some deuterium removal.**
\----------------------------
at a standard pressure of 1 atm, water is a liquid between 0–100 °C (32–212 °F)
Heavy water
Melting point 3.82 °C; 38.88 °F; 276.97 K
Boiling point 101.4 °C (214.5 °F; 374.5 K)
\---------------------------
Have two same kind bottles. I use 1.5L bottles from Preventa.
Fill one bottle with (your favorite water) almost to the top.
Place thermometer in it.

Place it in the freezer upright in place that is easily seen when opening door to look at thermometer.
For me, that is in the doors in my freezer.

Wait until water temperature (just) reached 32F (but is still clear see thru liquid).
2 hrs in my freezer.
\-----
Note,
there is some time available
from the time water just reached 32F
to time when latent heat is removed and all water is frozen.

Heavy water (when pure) should have been already frozen before 32F is reached,
but it is not since we are dealing with mixture of two or three types of water)
Heavy water
Melting point3.82 °C; 38.88 °F
\------
Take bottle out of the freezer, take thermometer out, close the top.
Shake bottle vigorously.
Look into the bottle,
you should see icy mesh forming within.

Immediately, pour water thru sieve and funnel to the other bottle, make sure to separate the ice.
You can melt that ice, expect 10-20cc volume.

Ice should have higher deuterium content.
Remaining water have less deuterium.

This process can be repeated as many times as one wish.
But when using cold water from the first run it takes 20-30 minutes to get it back to 32F

Regular ice floats.
Heavy water ice sinks.
(I have not tried it yet, but assume that the collected ice will float.)
\====================================================
Every little bit counts
but
Usual tap waters have 150ppm
RO waters have about the same
special super duper waters may have 145ppm and are extremely expensive
when compare to the price of the mix of Qlarivia with ordinary water.

To make any dent in ones deuterium content one should drink
at least 130ppm water
but dr Boros people were recommending 105ppm
and now recommend 85ppm

I drink 105ppm and today (6/14/2018) is my day #186
Have send my day #180 samples for testing.
Will see how this compares with my previous results:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-239774](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-239774)
I will update that post with new results when I get them.
\====================================================
[@WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/)
[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

**My question.**

**How much deuterium is removed in each cycle?**
**If I know initial volume and volume of removed ice and number of cycles,**
**how to use it in calculations?**
**If you know how to calculate this, please do it and post it here.**
\====================================================
CDN IRB220-F-6.5 ProAccurate Insta-Read Beverage and Frothing Thermometer
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000E8DM5S/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o02\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000E8DM5S/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

\======================================================
Note;
The above method is not my invention, it is from my Polish friend.
He uses dedicated freezer adjusted to +3-3.5 C,
so he does not have to worry of taking his water out before it freezes.
\======================================================
Using Eurofins.com
He tested two waters, Qlarivia and his home once frozen water.

24.556 =(-842.35/1000+1)\*155.76 Witek, Qlarivia

145.084 =(-68.54/1000+1)\*155.76 Witek, frozen 1x
\======================================================
Moja metoda wymrażania polega na dobrej lodówce którą przeznaczyłem tylko do tego celu i jest ona wyposażona w dokładny termometr i tak ustawiona, że jak pracuje z termometrem wewnątrz to termometr pokazuje plus 2 stopnie C. Zwykle wieczorem wkładam do niej 2 butelki wody mineralnej ( 2x1.75l) i rano otwieram, wyjmuję butelkę w której (sprawdziłem) jest woda o temp 3-3.5 stopnia i nie ma śladu lodu. Butelką zamkniętą potrząsam ok . pół minuty i nagle widzę jak w środku pojawia się lód w formie puchatej ,nietwardej masy, w ilości ok. 10- 20 cm sześciennych. Biorę sitko i przelewam wodę do naczynia przez sitko i jak wpadnie do sitka lód natychmiast go wyrzucam. To wszystko. Teraz teoria- woda o temp. ~3 stopni nie zamarza, a nawet woda ciężka żeby zamarznąć potrzebuje oddać ciepło zamarzania i to się dzieje jak potrząsam butelką, molekuły
Wody ciężkiej ocierając się o molekuły wody lekkiej oddają ciepło zamarzania i zamarzają. Reszta zostaje płynna.Tyle.
\======================================================

There are some problems when using highly depleted DDW.
One that I know of is from [@WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) , he got some problems, but I have no more details.
I am sure there are some problems.
I still think that it may have to do with low fasting glucose.
I am on DDW since Dec 2017.
On very recent test (8/13/2018) my
**fasting glucose was=65(82-115) so marked in red on test report**
insulin=3.41(1.9-23)
but
I feel fine all over.

I assume that had I started with DDW-25 or close and got to that point quickly, I would have hypoglycemic problems.

I note in my 2008 tests posted above
the glucose range was **normal at (65-99)**
the **new normal range** **(82-115)** seems like a proof that more people are diabetics.
[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I say that because laboratory ranges are statistical function of population being tested.

### Sue-UK — 2018-08-29T18:01:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-249679>

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=249586)
>
> Could DDW be dangerous in any case? Lets say you start drinking 25 ppm and you bring your deuterium lower than Boros reference range... We know that we need D in the plasma but how low is to low??

From the Somlyai book P82. (My emphasis). _"Following surgery, the dose of Dd-water must be decreased by 60-70 per cent for 6-8 days; we have observed that **in some cases Dd-water slowed down the process of wound healing.** We note, however, that in many cases we did not experience the above. Decreasing the dose for some days after the operation is thus indicated as a precaution."_ This implies to me that in humans deuterium is needed for wound healing (Becker's current of injury?) and regeneration, but I've not found anything on what is the minimum plasma level to maintain that.

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=249604)
>
> Deuterium might be able to protect DHA from breaking down from what i read... It can hinder its peroxidation if it is placed on the correct bond in vitro... However , does this happens in us and if it does what can put it in place?? Does DHA properties change if it is deuterated ?? i guess it might be but i dont know quantum mechanics enough to say... I was watching Michael Crawford's talk on youtube yesterday and he said that.... (time at video 20:10)
>

Thanks for that, very interesting.  I'm wondering if it relates to our fat burning/hibernation period at night … Or the liver and visceral fat, DHA and Bazan effect .. ? If we switch out the hydrogen in the right place in DHA for deuterium, it kind of makes sense … particularly for the retina.

I'm not convinced about low deuterium studies done on rats or dogs, as neither of those species are supporting similar brains to ours, frontal lobes etc. Also if we need to have a certain amount of deuterium to improve the protective functionality of DHA in the retina, rats are nocturnal, and the dog's primary sense is smell, not vision. Their need for deuterium as a "nutrient" for brain and eye health may be completely different.

### drezy — 2018-10-15T17:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-253250>

> [John Nicholas said:](/goto/post?id=253202)
>
> i'm on it.

I read Somlyai's instructions to gradually change D fractionation (D/H ratio).

I don't know why exactly he emphasizes that.

I do know that [@WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) seemed to push past that rule and experienced sugar lows. Rules are meant to be broken, but it sure is nice to know the guy who breaks the rule first to see what the consequence is. Besides we would have have lovingly remembered him if the consequences were greater. I would have at least made a sculpture out of used bubble gum and stick it onto the bottom of a Starbucks table to enshrine his memory.

The fractionation would change seasonally for natural dwellers of any area (short of a dessert or the arctic). The seasonal change would likely be somewhat gradual (vs instantaneous) and is probably be used by biology as yet another signaling mechanism.

### WalterNL — 2018-10-15T17:58:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-253260>

Yes that feeling is still vivid in my memory.

Seasonal variation is gradual indeed, and will change around 10 to 20% if I remember correctly. I also heard another reason from the 4-hour with dr. Boros why you should decrease D/H ratio gradually: if you start with 25ppm to combat a certain health issue, there is no way to go lower (I wasn't dealing with a serious health issue, nor am I a top athlete). Maybe 125ppm water is enough for your mitochondria and the deuterium depleting mechanisms in your body to function properly. Even though I don't totally understand the rationale behind what dr. Boros said, we obviously have to respect that small changes can have a big (non linear) effect according to Mother Nature's rules. PPM means parts per million, that means that tap water is 999850 ppm NOT deuterium, and yet it's a big deal whether we have 150ppm or 125ppm water.

Random link about hydrogen fractionation:
[http://www.iceandclimate.nbi.ku.dk/...ature\_moisture/fractionation\_and\_temperature/](http://www.iceandclimate.nbi.ku.dk/research/past_atmos/past_temperature_moisture/fractionation_and_temperature/)

### drezy — 2018-10-15T18:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-253261>

Yep, there are obvious non-linearities that hinder us. It's good to understand and employ the non-linearities that assist us.

It would take spending $$ testing, but
presumably 150ppm tap water from kitchen sink
was frozen once
and tested at 145ppm
\-----------------
The interesting part is
freezing process that freezes only heavy water.
Therefore the discarded heavy ice only minimally impacts water volume
so
this type of freezing can conceivably be repeated many times.

Heavy water freezes at ---(+3.8C)
light water 0C

Chill tap water below 3.8 and only minimally above 0.
Discard heavy ice
repeat

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-11-17T19:31:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-255850>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=255825)
>
> Thank you for sharing.
>
> How are you dealing with thirst?
> Are you monitoring your ADH?
> Supposedly ADH goes up with prolonged thirst and the thirst goes away?
> Describe any helpful ways or tricks that you have found useful.
> .

Reduction in thirst was a natural progression not designed. When I stopped consuming added salt a year ago thirst dropped. When I stopped cooking foods entirely over six months ago, there was a further step reduction in natural thirst. I only really became aware of it after the fact. Incidentally all prior cramping issues resolved entirely.

My habit is to drink in the morning an hour prior to eating. Often this is sufficient for the day or I may drink again between my two meals if thirsty. I never drink at or after dinner. I eat at 830 and 3.

If I was attempting to get thirst under control I would suggest sipping on extra virgin olive oil etc instead of water. But if you are not hi fat already I’m not sure this alone would work, and your requirements for water may be different than one who is fat adapted and furthermore raw in my case.

I’m not saying this is good or bad, but it works for me and makes life simpler which is the goal, and I feel great.

### drezy — 2018-11-18T16:48:11+0200

> Are you testing with a TDS-meter? I have a TDS and my machine-distilled water is 2 ppm, I guess its not as good as DDW?

TDS is used used for total dissolved solids. It's a cool tester.

As you suspect though it will not detect deuterium fractionation.

I had my rainwater tested by a lab Jan recommended. It's higher than VMSOW. I had a little cry and got over it.

If you look at the below chart you'll get the drift that my climate in Central Texas is closer to the upper right is higher δ2H (aka more deuterium).

If instead you're drinking meltwater in Vancouver then you'll be in the lower left(aka less deuterium).

It kinda looks like if your lower latitude naturally taking in more deuterium you sure better be spending time in the sun. At least thats a big part of my takeaway.

Its been bugging me why Boros is involved with DDW yet chooses to use olive oil.  But I've come across something about extra virgin olive oil which might provide a clue."The polyphenol hydroxytyrosol (contained in EVOO) can support complexes of the mitochondrial electron transport chain, stimulate mitochondrial biogenesis and block the lipogenic switch, thereby assisting energy production by mitochondria. Olive oil polyphenols can also protect against neurodegeneration and depression by increasing the expression of neurotrophins such as nerve growth factor (NGF). This neurotrophin helps to concentrate mitochondria at sites for axon growth in neurons. Without mitochondrial ATP, axon growth is inhibited". (References given, book is Mitochondria in Health and Disease. Personalised nutrition and lifestyle medicine for healthcare practitioners. Author Ray Griffiths).

### Sue-UK — 2018-11-22T12:10:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-256269>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=256173)
>
> Manufactured Preventa DDW is just a step in direction of understanding how things work.
> Drink DDW and it helps in general way.
> Induce own DDW production and you are closer repairing the essence of the process.
>
> We are build differently than spiders or camels.
> I think there may be some minimal amount of drinking that body can tolerate, thinking specially of kidneys.
> It would be easier think about if we knew Boros daily urine production.
> I think it should be more than what he drinks.

Apart from specialised water conservation adaptations, camels rehydrate with massive amounts of deuterium loaded water when they get a chance. They aren't permanently relying on matrix generated DDW for all their physiological needs. My thought experiment has been that if 100 healthy camels trekked across the desert in the heat but that at every watering hole the water was deuterium depleted, say to 110 ppm, how long would they remain healthy? With deuterium being in sperm, and if a minimum amount of deuterium was needed in camel milk to ensure healthy growth of offspring, what would happen to fertility rates and infant mortality? REM sleep depletes deuterium, so would chronically low deuterium stop them entering REM sleep to conserve deuterium? Too low blood levels of deuterium is implicated in poor wound healing (Somylai book). The camels would have the high UV, but if there is not enough deuterium in the blood for the solar squeeze to make UVC, what is the increased risk of infection?

Humans have a circadian rhythm for ADH, and getting thirsty and not drinking to satisfy thirst may raise my ADH at a time when it should be low, but what are the repercussions on say the circadian rhythm of mitochondrial fission and fusion? (Excessive fission is linked to breast cancer metastases, and other problems. Excessive fusion is linked to potential of damaged mitochondria fusing with and undermining healthy mitochondria in a process called mitochondrial contagion). Would it affect mitochondrial motility? When we don't have the same specialised water conservation adaptations as the camel, what are the repercussions to the cells giving up matrix DDW to support the survival of the organism as a whole during the active/feeding stage part of the day in strong light cycles?

Boros regime may be right for his n=1 context, but I'd need to understand the day/night, seasonal and latitude dynamics of mitochondrial function in relation to my own context, before accepting that drinking minimal amounts of water to induce my own DDW gets me closer to repairing the essence of the process. I'm interested in what Boros knows, but what he doesn't know may be more important for my n=1.

Thinking about fat burning overnight (when we wouldn't be drinking water) …..

The wiki entry for oleic acid says its the most abundant fatty acid in human adipose tissue, and second to palmitic acid in abundance in human tissues overall. The entry for palmitic acid says that in one study the analysis found palmitic acid to make up 21-30 percent (molar) of human depot fat, and that excess carbs in the body are converted to palmitic acid. Also that palmitic acid is a major, but highly variable, lipid component of human breast milk.

Interesting in relation to what Boros is doing, I don't remember him saying it was EVOO. Over 2% free acidity is classed as "not edible." [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olive\_oil\_acidity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olive_oil_acidity). "Olive oil" is something I wouldn't give house room to, let alone drink therapeutically, because although our refined olive oil is treated to improve the free acidity to make it edible, its the processing, being less likely to be top quality olives, and its usually in clear plastic bottles.

### WalterNL — 2018-11-24T10:37:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-256396>

> [Christina Johansson said:](/goto/post?id=255868)
>
> So you agree that rainwater is DD water?

Rainwater is _relatively_ deuterium depleted compared to the water that evaporated to make the clouds and eventually rain.
Heavier water (deuterium) falls earlier or is not as likely to evaporate as light water (hydrogen). So the deeper rain falls into land, away from the ocean, the more depleted it will be because the heavier isotope will have fallen as rain before the light water did.

But the difference is not that much, nowhere close to Qlarivia or comparable waters.

### WalterNL — 2018-11-24T14:58:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-256401>

> [Christina Johansson said:](/goto/post?id=256398)
>
> Im not sure I understand your answer...did you mean that there is more deuterium in the rainwater, the more away from the ocean you are?

No there's less deuterium in rainwater the farther it gets from the ocean. (Also true the farther you get from the equator).

Here's a more clear answer from a study of Greenland, the values in the clouds & rain/snow indicate how depleted the water is. So you see that inland snow in higher latitudes can be -350 promille depleted compared to VSMOW (155ppm). Which comes to about 100ppm deuterium.

Source: [http://www.iceandclimate.nbi.ku.dk/...ature\_moisture/fractionation\_and\_temperature/](http://www.iceandclimate.nbi.ku.dk/research/past_atmos/past_temperature_moisture/fractionation_and_temperature/)

I drink less water now, to just test what I feel about it. Maybe 1-2 liters/ day instead of 3-4.
Well, it is winter now and I am not working a ton and sweating like in summer, so it comes very naturally. I do realize I pee way less!
And especially I try not to drink much after dark. Just a cup or two.

I do eat a ton of fat. Lots of fatty lamb mince and additionally butter, preferably raw and grassfed. I just got me some grassfed beef fat too, yum! It is so good to eat just sliced and raw, like candy!

I might try out to drink even less when we go further into winter... I want to try out eating less, drinking less.... Gradually, better if it comes naturally. Trying to be aware what I really need.
I know I am a person that easily overindulge in eating and drinking, I do more than I would really need and feel like. Just a habit it is. I am working on being more aware of my body, my hunger, my thirst.

### Sue-UK — 2018-11-25T13:51:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-256553>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=256433)

> [View attachment 9769](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/9769/)

> Remember that that video was not about detailed scientific conference.

> **Emphasizing as a bio-chemist,**

>
> **I thought it was kind of heavy.**
> **Specially that he was the one who figured out the deuterium stuff on Somlyai's request.**
> **Everybody else are just quoting him.**
>
> Whatewer Boros said does not diminish sun's power a one bit.

Yes for me it is heavy on the biochemistry, but weightless on biophysics.  It makes sense for a polar bear to rely on metabolic water because of the cold and lack of sunlight, because it needs to maintain body temperature. I would suspect that it gets a source of deuterium from seal cell membranes etc and blood. A musing  too is that maybe by catching and tucking into a seal immediately, it is also getting a source of UVC from light being emitted from the seals dying cells. Their skin and fur recycles their own light.

It makes sense for a camel with its water conserving adaptations to temporarily use its hump to provide metabolic water as it travels between watering holes where it drinks lots of higher deuterium water which is controlled by strong sunlight. Perhaps the human equivalent is dry fasting overnight, in line with circadian rhythm, rather than the camel's adaptation to between watering holes. With our lesser adaptations, less strain on the heart when done overnight in the resting phase? The longer the night (winter) the more metabolic water?

As you say whatever Boros said does not diminish the sun's power one bit. Humans eating/drinking high fat contrary to a strong light cycle and/or ignoring thirst signals might force the mitochondria to work harder to produce metabolic water to compensate, but its not only what that does to someone who is say leptin resistant, living in a high nnemf environment etc. Cardiomyocytes have around 6000 mitochondria, they have a different system of quality control than other mitochondria. By forcing the heart to work harder in a strong light cycle by ignoring thirst mechanisms, could I risk heart problems, or using up my stem cells prematurely?

I had my first water at about 8 AM, and then my last at 6 PM, I just took a few mouthful of water before going to bed at 9 PM.
All my water was spring water mixed with Qlarivia, 1 part Qlarivia, 4 parts spring water.
I did make one cup of ginger tea from it too. Otherwise only plain water.

I peed about 0,5 liters in the morning yesterday, so I measured today too and it was about the same amount, at 8 AM. I do not pee in the night.

I do have my period, so I have the feeling I always pee a bit more then. I will measure again at the weekend I guess. I want to measure what happens if I drink way more water, or way less water..... so going to try out that too.

### JanSz — 2019-07-09T16:39:33+0300

> [Christina Gagnon said:](/goto/post?id=273886)
>
> How much water do you drink in a day nd is it only detereum depleted ater?

I am 79yo working on 80. 5'8" 145#
drink 1.5L/day to 2L/day on hot days.
Now I drink only DDW-25 ppm
But started with DDW-105ppm
Today, Tuesday 7/9/2019 is my 576 th day of drinking DDW.

Would like to follow dr Boros who is drinking 0.2 - 0.25Liter/day
but lacking mostly on will power to fight bad habit.

When discussing DDW there is a huge misconception.
Most if not all attention is directed at high cost of DDW.
That may be justified when directed at the few (very very sick people) who's mitochondria is not able to produce matrix water.
General population is just conditioned to drink too much.
Water industry does their best to induce people to overdrink.

According to dr Boros
any water over 135ppm **is not fit for consumption.**
That would mean that not only Australian water
and not only ALL fancy waters
but
all available waters are not fit for consumption.
And everybody should drink some DDW.

Usually (other than AU) waters are about 150ppm.
Couple ppm's up or down makes good subject for salesmen to take your $$.
To get 135ppm from 150ppm
just take one bottle (0.5L) of DDW-25
add
3.66667 Liter of tap water
And you have little over 4 Liters of 135ppm, good for two days.
**To make thinks simpler**
**Every day drink half a bottle of straight DDW-25**

Insights from last nights Q & A 7/28/19: Fullerenes can be used in electrostatic membranes to make DDW from potable water. I would not expect a biochemist to know about new physical chemistry being used in biophysics labs. The structural and dynamic effects of lipophilic C60 derivatives in phospholipid bilayers is an area my water researchers are using to lower the costs of DDW. Using the topologic effects of graphene and light is also being employed now too. Our aim is to put science on lowering the costs of the water. I don't believe this is the focus of the group at UCLA at this time. There is a difference between the recommendations of a research biochemist and a clinician. One might align with marketers in their academic center to profit from the sale of DDW and testing of deuterium levels. The other might actually try to use the latest in biophysics research to partner with researchers who want to make DDW available for pennies on the dollar so mankind can benefit mankind It is a novel topologic insulator like graphene that can be used to create DDW because they can be made magnetic cheaply?

-   Did you know that carbon sixty and graphene have these biophysical abilities?

-   Graphene membranes also are magnetic and this helps make DDW because of the varying magnetic moment of deuterons vs H+ = also the domain of biophysics and not biochemistry. These are all effects not covered in standard enzymatic flux reactions in any medical biochemical book. Be careful who packs your parachute folks. Find a center who supports the survival of the wisest instead of supporting the survival of their P & L statement.

### JanSz — 2019-08-20T17:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-free-water.20554/post-276298>

> [Hilde said:](/goto/post?id=276263)
>
> Hi JanSz,
>
> I am newbee here and not familiar with the term **RO water.**
> It's short for....?

Reverse Osmosis.

When I was drinking it, I would buy it at Costco.
I tested that water at ddcenters.com
and it has 149.4ppm (D/H)
That is rather impressive since it is the cheapest available drinking water.

That was at the beginning of the deuterium story when [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) introduced us to deuterium.
At that time, all was about lowering our (grown-up) bodies deuterium content.
That started two years ago, shortly after my DW died in Oct 2017.
I was really upset that such vital news came soo late.
Had it been earlier, I could have possibly helped my wife better than I did.
Anyhow, I started drinking DDW myself and now am drinking only DDW-25ppm.
Also, drinking small total amounts daily, around 1 liter/day was thought to be beneficial.

However, recently it seems that [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) is supporting and drinking copious amounts, (5 - 6) liters/day of good water but no need to worry about its Deuterium content.

.

I used to be able to collect a lot of urine in my bladder. It allowed me to do the work I did.
Enough to last me 5-6 hours when dressed up and doing my work in Nuclear Reactor Containment, (during which not able to relieve myself).
No more. But at least, after a few tough years, my bladder stabilized after Green Laser surgery for a large prostate.
Now, sometimes, I pee and know that I emptied my bladder completely, but also feel that it is getting re-fill.
So I wait, 20-30 seconds, and pee again.

...

### Katie Durham — 2019-11-13T23:14:01+0200

>
> I peed 1,9 liters
> I drank 1,6 liters.
>
> my food did not have any water in it, I just had meat and butter and heart and beef fat.

Cats evolved in a desert environment with a very low thirst drive, to get all their moisture from their food. Which as obligate carnivores is largely meat and heart and fat (and bone and a few other things). There is a lot more water in that than you might expect.

I assume that this water is low of deuterium.
One day will have to measure it, but it looks like it is around 10%.

# Thread: Kidney dynamics, a few initial thoughts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-dynamics-a-few-initial-thoughts.20561/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Leger — 2017-12-09T18:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-dynamics-a-few-initial-thoughts.20561/post-229879>

This post is a continuation of the thread started by @[Technosmith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20046/) :
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...edicine-kidney-yin-and-chi-deficiencey.20468/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/traditional-chinese-medicine-kidney-yin-and-chi-deficiencey.20468/)

From the CCM (Classical Chinese Medicine) perspective, the kidneys are the batteries of the body. This has dramatic implications for the new findings in water and how it functions in our body. A discussion I had with a friend - Dr. Robin Murphy (ND) - on this subject - he was of the opinion that the kidneys main function is to structure water. I was just getting into water structuring at that point in time, but his statement continues to echo through the years. It offers a possible starting place for how water gets structured in our bodies and a way of looking at the kidneys from an energetic or dynamic perspective. The Western perspective is mired in (bio) chemistry. Looking at organ dynamics - their energetic function - possibly informed by some understanding of the CCM perspective - is the way forward.

Relatedly, Biology is the foundation of Reality, physics is a subset. Physics is a way for us to study dead matter, the 'corpses' of Life; basic arithmetic so that we can get our thoughts straight before tackling the advanced 'math'. The discovery of quantum mechanics, etc, is the beginning of a bridge to Life. Quantum Biology extends the bridge, offering tantalizing clues to a much better understanding of Life.

# Thread: Hi from Gloucester UK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-gloucester-uk.20562/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nigel Clack — 2017-12-09T18:42:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-gloucester-uk.20562/post-229881>

Hi, I’m from Gloucester UK although born in Mauritius and left when I was one and a half in 1966.

Married, no kids.

I’ve gone down to working 3 days a week in an office, and I’m using the extra time to work on our new house just outside of the city (less routers to worry about) and to start a natural movement training business initially based on MovNat.

I intend to train people outside as much as possible but have a barn I’m going to kit out for use during British weather and early evenings in the winter.(need a lighting strategy) I also intend to provide health advice once I’m comfortable with enough of it.

I was diagnosed with asthma in 1991, have improved it a lot with going Paleo 5 years ago and moving house recently, also trying Buteyko breath training as I’m a mouth breather. But still sensitive to the cold and animals.

Have recently started to get joint pain in fingers especially the index fingers.

Trying to eat local and seasonal and more fish for the first time this winter, started to grow our own veg. Will get an RO water filter next year.

Looking forward to the CT webinar next week to kick off doing it properly.

Slowly reducing exposure to RF’s and should be fully hard wired next year.

# Thread: protecting yourself at work: reducing blue light from screens vs reducing nnemf exposure from devic
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-12T12:15:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/post-230085>

As a teacher, I work in an environment where the wifi signal keeps being ramped up in my school building. people complain about the weak signals (as a side note, the signal seems to go out when it is raining for some reason), and then more antennas or whatever they use are installed to make the signal stronger. i want to get some type of blue-blocking eye glasses, but those type of glasses don't reduce radiation exposure to the wifi signals, correct? what do members use to reduce their overall radiation exposure in the workplace? i am reading scott compton's journal, but am only on page 9, so i'm just at the parts where he is making homemade hats by sewing in some sort of material and he is about to talk to his child's school principal. i am doing reading, so please don't think i am just another lazy newbie.....has anyone found a meter-verifiable way to reduce their radiation exposure to wifi in the workplace???

### WalterNL — 2017-12-12T13:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/post-230088>

True, glasses don't help against all nnEMF, but they do help against the worst of it which is artificial blue light. But the main thing you can do is avoidance, so limit exposure and move yourself away from the source of nnEMF. If you don't, all other things proposed on this blog help some way or another to mitigate nnEMF damage.

As far as I know there is no way to stop microwaves from influencing your biology if you are within range.

### SlamSlask — 2017-12-12T14:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protecting-yourself-at-work-reducing-blue-light-from-screens-vs-reducing-nnemf-exposure-from-devic.20570/post-230091>

Turn off what you can - after that use the inverse square law to your advantage (get as far away as possible)
After this consider if the radiation is worth your paycheck

I read the post,the summaries of the studies and then read the video transcript of "Deuterium depletion and mitochondrial NADPH production: The link for epigenetic control of oncogenesis" . I personally find the pro Deuterium data too limited ,lacking a coherent narrative. But I share it here as some people may be swayed by the impressive cite list but when you look at it has a limited scope.. There is a lot of contrary data out there and the article on linked in does not go into evaluating the poison aspect of Deuterium, it only elucidates the studies that serve to picture D as a panacea.

Deuterium is not always bad as she seems to think was said. She doesn't know what was said. She is working from hearsay.

DDW is a matrix cancer issue.......not a growth issue. Drugs are deuterated to get affects on receptors. Just as she has made snarky comments about dopamine levels she fails to see the nuanced brush strokes.

Other than that I have no disagreement with what she posted. Now go make sense of the biology to help people with that post. Tell me how far you get.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-12T21:27:26+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-12T21:30:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-post-of-a-rebuttal-of-the-deuterium-theory.20571/post-230161>

### Sean Waters — 2017-12-13T13:52:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-post-of-a-rebuttal-of-the-deuterium-theory.20571/post-230218>

It reads to me like she is pointing out that scientific literature displays a spectrum of effect for Deuterium, some negative and some positive, and that she believe it has it's place in nature and shouldn't be ostracised.

I think we all think the same.

But that just like Blue Light (which is found in natural sunlight), there is too much of it in our man-made world generally. So, for the general visitor of this website who has a health issue, Deuterium Depletion (just like Blue Light mitigation) is advised. However, for the Sherpa at the Himalaya, maybe not so much.

It's all context, but how many Sherpas do we have here?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-13T15:44:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-post-of-a-rebuttal-of-the-deuterium-theory.20571/post-230224>

She is no longer a member so she has no idea what I have said about the effect. She wrongly assumes I've said deuterium is always bad. She also assumed the same thing about dopamine. So she is proving my point about the Dunning Kruger effect I mentioned in the webinars........when you dont have all the facts you can make catastrophic errors in your calculations. Blue light stimulates a stream of deuterium into the retina.......every sunrise to do the things deuterium does to the TCA cycle that we need it to do. Sunlight also has the anitdote to this effect in AM sunlight so this means that the amount of deuterium let in is ALWAYS quantized by the effect of red and by place on the location when UVA first shows up. This is why fat mass increases in autumn and winter in mammals..........because UVA light shows up way late..........Vermont 2017 explains it.

This review on deuterium is missing all the data, and focuses in on the things she wants on the menu. You are going to soon learn what is off the menu she doesn't yet know. And you will see that as members soon in the webinars and blogs. You can bet your ass on it.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-03-22T00:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-post-of-a-rebuttal-of-the-deuterium-theory.20571/post-237703>

Last year I showed definitely this happened in your retina in Vermont in 2017......ten months later they are telling the world I am right. PHYSICISTS ARE ABOUT THE 'IMPOSSIBLE' -- TURNING LIGHT INTO MATTER
Theoretically, it should be possible to turn light into matter. In practice, well - "easier said than done" is an understatement.
Now, 84 years after the process was first theorized, some researchers reckon they're going to be able to do it - and they're about to start the experiment.[https://www.sciencealert.com/light-into-matter-breit-wheeler-process-hohlraum-experiment-start-2018](https://www.sciencealert.com/light-into-matter-breit-wheeler-process-hohlraum-experiment-start-2018)

# Thread: SunKing Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunking-journal.20574/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ozbuck — 2017-12-13T03:43:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunking-journal.20574/post-230198>

Hey team.

I'm from Sydney, Australia. Early 30s, male.

I heard about Kruse's work a few years back, thought it was interesting but it didn't click. I watched the Vermont 2017 talk a couple of weeks back. CLICK!!! I was hooked, it resonated. It made me realize how much our biology has particular EXPECTATIONS that are not being met and, if anything, are being abused by our modern ways.

Ever since then I have been getting to the beach every morning just after the sun rises, earthing and soaking up the light for a couple of hours each day, earthing (land and ocean swimming), not wearing sunglasses anymore, building up the amount of sunshine during the day as much as I can without getting burnt, drinking SP water, using blueblockers when on devices and under blue light, putting the phone on airplane mode when not using it, and getting to bed at a good time for sleep.... and I've felt fucking GREAT these last two weeks! I may be just placebo-ing the hell out of myself, but I don't think so

The brainfog I would consistently experience has diminished GREATLY!!! In the past I would get super tired, drowsy, and really sleepy after eating: that is gone! Sometimes, like yesterday, I felt I had popped some happy pills or something... my mood was just light and happy. I'm sleeping a lot DEEPER.

A year and a half ago, a couple of weeks after I returned from traveling in South America I started getting weird twitches in my body and in my vision, weird feeling of pressure in my head. I got bitten by a tick while I was over there, no rash from it, but these weird symptoms made me think I must have Lyme. I'd never had these symptoms before. After a few months of waiting and seeing them not go away I got some tests done overseas but they all came back negative (including coinfections e.g. Epstein Barr, rickettsia, babesia, etc.) which was relieving but even more confusing. Also, lyme testing in unreliable in many cases so that has always been in the back of my mind.

Anyway, I've always spent a lot of time in front of blue light... I do a lot of photo and video editing (my career), and I had a lot of TV use as a kid, nintendo as a kid, computer games as a kid and teenager, lots of laptop use over the years, phones, staying up late in front my artificial light, using sunglasses, etc. I'm myopic but I don't use glasses for it. Ironically, I went to a local GP a few weeks back who quickly checked my eyes and was surprised I don't use glasses... he could tell with his instrument how myopic I am.

I really think now that these body and vision twitches have a strong connection to dopamine. The last two weeks the body twitches have greatly reduced. Procrastination, concentration issues, lack of motivation, anxiety, social anxiety, etc. have all been there throughout my life. Even though I've lived on in spite of them, they've certainly had their effects despite eating really healthy and doing other things right. Something deeper has always felt like it was at play.

The power of proper light exposure that my biology expects for proper functioning is now monumental in my mind. The more I read Jack's work the more it makes damn sense. I need to work on cutting out as much eEmf (I live in the inner city) and start CT, but I'm on my way! These last two weeks have shown me the potential of how good things can be when one is aligned with nature as much as we're expected to be.

# Thread: A success story overcoming fatigue
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mbhemming — 2017-12-13T06:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230202>

I'm now about 9 months in to following Dr. Kruses suggestions. I'm happy to report a success in overcoming fatigue. I have been keeping data on my fatigue issue for 209 days now. The attached graph shows the decrease in my fatigue symptoms.

Interpreting the scatter plot:
Each data point in the plot represents an average of the 90 days. So the first data point is my average amount of fatigue from day 1 to day 90. The second data point is the average from day 2 to day 91.

The red line is linear model that was fit to data to clearly show the decrease.

I'm happy to report that I have had no symptoms for the past 48 days!!!

I would like to personally thank Dr. Kruse!

Am I totally better yet? No, I still have some cognitive impairment symptoms that come periodically.

### Susie Cahill — 2017-12-14T09:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230274>

Wow, that’s awesome.
I didn’t keep a diary but after finding JK in May this year I’m a new person. I had stage 3 adrenal fatigue and really only did a loose version of the diet but added heaps of oysters. I had 3 holidays in the tropics of 10 days duration on his advice, religiously saw the sun rise and wore blu blockers, went to bed at 9 pm and tried to stay out in the am light as much as possible and totally rearranged my social life; no dinners only lunches. The game changer for me was the Novothor light pod. I did it 2-3x/week and in a 12 sessions I had more energy than I’ve had in a decade. Just need to check my cortisol levels now and I can’t imagine they haven’t gone north. I’m one happy camper. Keen to start CT.

### taiyang — 2017-12-15T12:09:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230342>

congratulations mbhemming! can you give some specifics of what you did?
susie, how did you find a place offering the light pod? i googled my state and the name of that pod and came up with nothing.

### mbhemming — 2017-12-16T02:33:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-230400>

@[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/)
I didn't do everything perfectly, I just did what I could.
\-eat seafood approximately every second day
\-kept reducing EMF as much as I could. I bought a gaussmeter and RF meter. I haven't been able to get rid of my cell phone but I reduced the use as much as possible.
\-I starting doing CT in september but took a really non-aggressive approach to it. I was doing around 2 or 3 times per week in the cold tub. But then it started getting cold where I live so I stopped doing the cold tub and just didn't turn on the heat
\-moved out of the city in a little house that is at ground level, has a well, is in the woods, is 100m to the ocean, and has a fireplace
\-I was doing paleo at first but I had a hard time keeping a healthy weight so now I just eat relatively natural
\-stopped alcohol and caffeine(I started them again recently because I haven't had fatigue symptoms)
\-I also got a tonne of sun last summer. I tried to get 5 hours/day for each day that it was sunny
\-took over a gram of magnesium per day
\-did a lot of walking for exercise
\-I did a lot of circadian synchronization
\*you can check out my health journal for more info. I just tried to summarize it here

\-All three graph are on a 90 day window. This means that each data point is the average of 90 consecutive days. So the first data point is the average from day 1 to day 90, and the second data point is the average from day 2 to day 91.
\-The sequence of events are as follows. I started implementing Dr. K's ideas, then a couple months later I started collect the 'fatigue' data, after 3 months of collecting the 'fatigue' data I started collecting the 'energy' and 'cognition' data.
\-the red line is a linear model that fits the blue data

### Susie Cahill — 2018-04-09T10:46:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-239027>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=230342)
>
> congratulations mbhemming! can you give some specifics of what you did?
> susie, how did you find a place offering the light pod? i googled my state and the name of that pod and came up with nothing.

Taiyang,
I’ve had a breakthrough with maintaining my energy levels without the Novothor light pod. When I was really bad with adrenal fatigue I was doing Novothor sessions 3x/week. I then got to a stage of backing it off to 1x/week which I maintained for about 2 months. I always wondered how much emf’s the machine gave off and when I saw a Jack Kruse comment somewhere, maybe here or Facebook, suggesting a guy go swimming rather than purchase a Joove lamp, I thought maybe I should try swimming too. The bay is the same travel time from my home as the Novothor so I started swimming. It’s not too cold here yet hovering around 60-62 degrees but for me, who’s always felt the cold it a big thing but I’m past dreading it now and I feel great. I get the same energy kick I was getting from the light pod. I also do a sauna then get back in the cold water again. I’m now swimming .75 kilometre, when I started I was swimming about 100 metres.
Apparently the water temp gets down to 46 or 8c in winter. The club is over 120 yrs old and the swim group is called the Iceburgers. I been pick the brains of the veterans and they say things like, this is our flu injection, we never get sick, I don’t feel right if I don’t start my day this way. Their mean age would probably be around 70 yrs.
Are you close to the beach?

### taiyang — 2018-04-09T13:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-239032>

I am 10 minutes walking distance from a big river. I am very cold intolerant. Even on a super hot day in the summer, I won't go in the ocean past my calves. Right now i am in one of my cyclic bouts with anemia due to heavy periods. i am being more aggressive with stopping the flow of blood with norethindrone pills since i am getting weak and a sore throat from blood loss. i always hold off on taking pills unless absolutely necessary. i should have started supplementing with iron earlier, but i got scared off by reading about the dangers of iron overload. i am definitely not going to be at that point for a long while, so i am supplementing. maybe as my iron/ferritin stores fill up, i will be less cold intolerant by the summer and i will try swimming in the ocean (1.5 hours a way) like 2x a week and the river everyday. thanks for the update!!

### Susie Cahill — 2018-04-10T13:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-success-story-overcoming-fatigue.20575/post-239122>

Good luck with that. jK also said your omega 6 :3 ratio should be below 10. If low on omega 3 you’ll feel the cold more.
Best wishes.

# Thread: I'm loving Playa Del Carmen!!! + a couple questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-loving-playa-del-carmen-a-couple-questions.20576/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mbhemming — 2017-12-13T07:18:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-loving-playa-del-carmen-a-couple-questions.20576/post-230203>

I've been here for 11 days so far and am loving it!

I walk down to the beach with no shoes or shirt. I've been doing work in the Quinta Alegria mall but take frequent breaks to go to the beach. I've been playing beach volleyball at the Royal everyday. I hear you guys often stay there. We play at 11am and 4pm. There is even an outdoor public gym that is really close to my apartment! It's on the corner of Calle 34 and 20th avenue.

I really love it here! It's so easy to get a social life going in this city. After you meet some people(which is so easy) then you will have people messaging you to go out pretty much every night. So far I've met about half tourists and half local mexican people. Tulum is amazing too! Such good vibes there.

I'm going to move here in a years time! I will be able to work remotely as a computer scientist.

Questions:

Am I grounded walking barefoot on the bricks on 5th ave?

Do you guys prefer the Royal because the water at that location has limestone(like the cenotes)?

Obviously there is a decent amount of EMF pollution in the city. Does the fact that there is a strong magnetic field in the area trump the EMF pollution?

Will anyone be here from now to December 23rd and want to meet up?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-13T16:02:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-loving-playa-del-carmen-a-couple-questions.20576/post-230225>

yes

yes and more

yep

nope. I arrive that day.

enjoy

### OptimalSkr — 2017-12-14T21:50:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-loving-playa-del-carmen-a-couple-questions.20576/post-230322>

Cool man good to hear about your success!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-14T23:42:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-loving-playa-del-carmen-a-couple-questions.20576/post-230326>

If you want oysters go to Harry's on 5th Ave. Expensive but the view up there with the Nana Wall open is awesome.

### Omelchenko — 2017-12-13T17:38:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sodium-bicarbonate-for-increased-cell-voltage.20577/post-230237>

I've looked around the forum for the answer to this question and have seen tid bits here and there. But my question is, would sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) be "good" to consume daily in small doses?

I've been reading that it increases cell voltage by being an electron donor and that it also increase cell oxygen as well. I would think both of these things would be beneficial in today's nnEMF world.

Thanks for any replies and help!

### Penny — 2017-12-13T19:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sodium-bicarbonate-for-increased-cell-voltage.20577/post-230244>

>
> I've been reading that it increases cell voltage by being an electron donor and that it also increase cell oxygen as well. I would think both of these things would be beneficial in today's nnEMF world.
>
> Thanks for any replies and help!

I'm a huge an of it - but I use potassium bicarbonate instead - I rub baking soda all over my body after the shower and after I rub epsom salts and mag chloride - my skin gets hot - then I blast it with cold water and it hydrates my skin - JK has said previously that baking soda increases the exclusion zone of water...

# Thread: getting ready/leaving for work before the sunrise- how do you mitigate?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-ready-leaving-for-work-before-the-sunrise-how-do-you-mitigate.20582/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-15T12:23:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-ready-leaving-for-work-before-the-sunrise-how-do-you-mitigate.20582/post-230345>

i go to bed early- 8pm- and from around 5:30 pm (i live in nj) i have blu blockers on and try to cover all skin surfaces, have lights off or dim, etc. i have no screens after 5:30 pm except the TV at a distance and this seems to work and i sleep fairly well- much better than before. i get up around 4 am naturally without an alarm clock, and i use that time to go on the computer to read here and to do work that i can't do in the evening.

what do people do who get up before the sunrise? the sunrise right now here is 7:14 am. since i live 45 minutes from my work, i need to be out the door at 6:35 am so that by the time i get to the town where i work, i can spend some time in the sunrise. should i be wearing blue blockers and covered up from the time i wake up at 4 am to 7:14 am? should i not be on the computer at all before the sunrise?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-15T19:21:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-ready-leaving-for-work-before-the-sunrise-how-do-you-mitigate.20582/post-230373>

What if I told you that bottle is -35C, and the water still liquid. Would you buy this mito-hack? Tonight's mito-hack post from my unpublished book: Water releases 80 calories per gram when turning from a liquid to a solid. Most of our freezers are only a few degrees Celsius below zero, and the specific heat of water is one calorie per degree per gram. This means that our water, as it freezes, warms up the rest of the water until the process stops at 0 degrees Celsius, freezing perhaps ten or twenty percent of the water. This ice may be distributed throughout the bottle, though, as the crystallization process happens very quickly and heat flows slowly. This water is the water we do not want because it is most D20. So understanding how deuterium works with temperature becomes a mito-hackers dream.

### Sue-UK — 2017-12-16T13:10:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-ready-leaving-for-work-before-the-sunrise-how-do-you-mitigate.20582/post-230417>

I remember that a member here had a consult with Alexander Wunsch, and he apparently said that he thought we needed more than 8 hours darkness at night to run the regeneration programmes. I'm aiming for 10 hours min, and for my age and heteroplasmy, using a computer pre sunrise isn't worth the risk, even though my usual daily environment is very good in terms of wifi, light pollution etc.

Pre sunrise my pic would say _My pre-sunrise routine, **stars,** glacial water and grounding to get optimal_.

I think watching the night sky move from darkness to astronomical twilight, or from astronomical to nautical twilight, or nautical to civil twilight or civil to sunrise ... seeing any transition helps the circadian rhythms, via the thalamus, particularly during its "silent phase" (when oscillations temporarily stop). Its also called the "free run" phase (James Oschmann, Energy Medicine, the scientific basis) and is believed to probably be the phase where "brain waves are susceptible to entrainment by external fields. "
I find being outside pre-sunrise healing ... Just looking up at the stars and remembering that the photons hitting my eyeballs left those stars millions of years ago .......... breathing in the cold air, sensing the transition of the earth's magnetic field, grounding etc, sensing a light transition phase under clouded skies when no stars are visible...... Feels great, even if the cold then sends me back to bed to warm up before getting back out later. Comparing that to an (even blue blocked) alien computer sun before natural sunrise, and what external fields my thalamus picks up during its free runs ......

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=230434)
>
> sue, how do you know your heteroplasmy rate? do you know which of your tissues has a high rate of heteroplasmy? how do you know?

Having a diagnosed condition can indicate where there's high heteroplasmy rate tissue, but I've been comparing the conditions on a chart of homotoxicology (from complex homeopathy). I made up a blank chart and can take a case history, (including updating my own ) and make a judgement about which organ system is compromised and at which phase .... It doesn't go in a straight line from left to right, but any to the right of the biological division shows a worsening through to shite heteroplasmy rate.
[](http://www.google.co.uk/url?sa=i&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=images&cd=&cad=rja&uact=8&ved=0ahUKEwib6KSJ3pDYAhWGPhQKHZ5pCREQjRwIBw&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.naturalworldhealing.com%2Fphilosophyofhealth%2Fhomotoxicologytable.htm&psig=AOvVaw3sDVQ4OUqk7fleoRRCjOUK&ust=1513588111989050)
Since it originated, I think there are nuances to incidence, and timing progression of disease because of social and environmental changes, nnemf and blue light toxicity (For example chronic use of a mobile next to the head, blue light and obesity, working longer indoors under artificial light etc), and I'm also looking at it in relation to deuterium accumulation and elimination. For example although obesity is under metabolism, deposition phase, subcutaneous fat could also be on the deposition phase of the skin line. It's wiser to improve redox of all the excretion routes before detoxing (and preferably let the body detox naturally when its ready and in line with the circadian cycle). ..Deuterium tucked away in my SQ fat (or skin tags) could be considered protective - starving myself or overdoing the CT too soon could dump it back into the bloodstream to finish up worsening heteroplasmy who knows where, especially if I'm not getting the right eye signals, my sleep is crap and I don't regenerate properly at night .... I'm currently using the chart as a trend predictor, with the biological division being the heteroplasmy mutant threshold.

### ozbuck — 2017-12-17T01:33:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-230455>

Yeh all the electricity would need to be turned off while it's in use.

I also think that the water would need to be emptied and refilled after a period of time to keep it from not going bad, unless there was a way to filter it.

But, to pick something like this alternative up (possibly second hand for $300) is a good proposition I think.

### Benny Hammond — 2018-04-23T19:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-239734>

this is how i ct at home. i love it man! i change out the water every couple of weeks and dump a bottle of hydro-peroxide in and never have any issues with bacteria that i can tell. you can pick these guys up for 100 bucks on craigslist easily. i drop piece of copper in there that's connected to a grounding rod and a chunk of activated charcoal go into the cold!

i am rearranging the ct chest freezer and i was wondering if i would be risking electrocution by using an immersible pump while i'm inside. what do you guys think?

### drezy — 2018-04-24T18:34:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-239836>

> [Benny Hammond said:](/goto/post?id=239734)
>
> this is how i ct at home. i love it man! i change out the water every couple of weeks and dump a bottle of hydro-peroxide in and never have any issues with bacteria that i can tell. you can pick these guys up for 100 bucks on craigslist easily. i drop piece of copper in there that's connected to a grounding rod and a chunk of activated charcoal go into the cold!
>
> I am rearranging the ct chest freezer and i was wondering if i would be risking electrocution by using an immersible pump while i'm inside. what do you guys think?

If you have a Cornet or any other EM meter do yourself a favor and turn the pump on while holding the meter near it to see why I never run my pump and even have a shutoff for power to the whole circuit when I'm in my tub.

A cheap outlet timer will work well once you figure out about how much flow per day you need.

### Benny Hammond — 2018-04-24T22:31:48+0300

you believe i would be risking electrocution with a submersible pump running while doing ct? i always be sure the freezer is unplugged before i go in.

### drezy — 2018-04-24T23:06:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-239865>

That's not what I said at all. As an electrical engineer I know that if you get a GFCI plug in your circuit and there will be zero chance you'll electrocute yourself.

My point is that if, for some reason, you like the idea of straddling your prostate up on top of a strong alternating magnetic field that would make a cornet meter max out then by all means go ahead if it's important to run your pump exactly while you're also in the tub. Otherwise just design around it.

PM me and we can talk. I love knowing others out there are coming up with different cool systems.

FWIW I circulate my water in between sessions and have rarely seen my tub temp increase 2 degrees during 45 minute use.

perhaps a little confusion - i believe i'm asking if it's safe to jump in the tub with the sub pump on. i know that might appear to be an ignorant question in a sense. i put my arm in when its on and there is no problem. well, i guess if you don't hear from me - cardiac arrest in a ct tub heheh

### drezy — 2018-04-26T15:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-239963>

> [Benny Hammond said:](/goto/post?id=239945)
>
> perhaps a little confusion - i believe i'm asking if it's safe to jump in the tub with the sub pump on. i know that might appear to be an ignorant question in a sense. i put my arm in when its on and there is no problem. well, i guess if you don't hear from me - cardiac arrest in a ct tub heheh

In the context of a forum, when I answer someone I am on the record for all future people with similar questions that find that answer.

If you concern yourself with increasing longer term colon and prostate cancer then you'll want to not be in a cooler with a pump running anywhere near your bottom.

If you care only about short term electrocution then you probably want to ask on an electrician forum. There will be many mothers worried about their pool pump electrocuting their kids that keep dipping in the landscaped koi pond.

### Benny Hammond — 2018-07-28T17:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-246849>

'get out before you start shivering?'
'if you are in longer than 4 minutes your just showing off?'

wtf?

### Lahelada — 2018-07-29T00:03:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-ct-in-a-freezer.20584/post-246860>

33F! This is deep Ct. The Cold thermogenesis protocol stipulates 50-55F.More is not required.The protocol also stipulates socks and hat for a good reason. It aids in keeping your torso temp controllable to protect organs.

i believe the guy in the video is not talking about cold thermogenesis. it seems to me he advocates staying in for only 4 minutes and mentions hormetic response. that is hardly deep ct if you ask me. what do i know? there are a few videos where someone is saying the same i believe.

### Julian — 2018-07-30T12:40:10+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-visiting-water-with-sea-salt.20586/post-230437>

Hey folks,

With all the new information coming out regarding Deuterium Depleted Water, I just wondered if anyone is still adding sea salt to their spring water with the intention of improving hydration?

As I mentioned in a previous post, tt would seem logical that drinking water with sea salt added would help with hydration as a saline solution is used in medical establishments as a front-line treatment for dehydration. Also, you can consider the work and findings of Rene Quinton and his use of marine plasma.

I believe the research of Gerald Pollack advises against adding salts to water though as it may negatively effect the EZ, so what do we all currently feel is optimal?

Thanks,

Phil

it turned up than when, over the years, he was talking about
BUN/Creatinine ratio indicating hydration
he really meant matrix water.
That would be inside mitochondria or possibly including inside cell.

Proper amount of that water depends heavily on body's ability to manage deuterium optimally,
and
when body is lacking
high fat diet and
DDW to the rescue.
All governed by Quantum physics. (High tech).

we still have water H2O, extracellular that we manage by minding our electrolytes.
I think, we do not worry about deuterium here (low tech part of our bodies)
And here we worry about proper levels of
Na, K, Cl, CO2, Mg, Mo, Ca
probably few more.
\------------------------------------

I have been prescribed Levothyroxine currently taking 75 mg but really need to take a higher dose , but find it hard to tolerate the correct dose as it exacerbate my migraines. I currently take canderstartan and have botox injections for migraine. I feel constantly tired and have a daily headache which more often than not developed into a migraine.
I would be extremely grateful for any advise or information.

### WalterNL — 2017-12-17T20:26:15+0200

Search for the protocols/Rx on the blog, you are going to have to focus on improving your redox potential/mitochondrial function, as this will improve everything else. Getting daily sun, grounding/CT, good water, seafood and having a support system are critical. Hashimotos is a serious condition and will require a complete change of your lifestyle and living environment. Avoiding nnEMF, fake foods, artificial light and unnatural environments is key.

# Thread: Youtube with Jack and Jeremy - live

### caroline — 2017-12-18T05:23:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youtube-with-jack-and-jeremy-live.20591/post-230535>

What an amazing and extraordinary event! This was mind blowing, down to earth and REAL - all at the same time!

I am wondering what is your take home from this live stream????

There were so many - I should have been taking notes.

here is some of mine .......

Jack is a people person - his medium is people. You can't really understand this until you meet Jack in person - and touch him!

I have always adored Jeremy - from day #1 actually - he is so real and authentic and lovely and kind and passionate and just does what he needs to do. I loved it when he said he still doesn't know what Deuterium is!

We need to require ourselves to ask better and harder questions

no apologies - do what is right for you

We need tunnel vision for what we want in our life

and the biggie .......

If we only take home 10% of all of this ....we will be a ton better off.

Jeremy talked about the book he is trying to write - he has a wonderful passion for touch and was so eloquent when he talked about his passion.
Just as Jack has so much passion for all of this ....and we are the extremely lucky recipients.

Actually - none of this is new ....Jack has been telling us all this and so much more for ever!

There was so much wonderful content from Jack and Jeremy in this youtube - I can't wait to re listen.

Thank you Jack and Jeremy - from the bottom of my heart.

Both of you light up this world and change lives...... and the one I care about most - mine!

> Actually - none of this is new ....Jack has been telling us all this and so much more for ever!
>
> There was so much wonderful content from Jack and Jeremy in this youtube - I can't wait to re listen.
>
> Thank you Jack and Jeremy - from the bottom of my heart.
>
> Both of you light up this world and change lives...... and the one I care about most - mine!

I have just watched the first 60 minutes and I am floored I stopped on purpose so I can digest and contemplate what Jack and Jeremy have discussed up to this point.
I will do another hour tomorrow and use same regiment for the last hour. This is going to be many VIEWING'S to get all the notes in as the best part about a format such as this is there are nuggets dropped constantly, and what may not be so important to another is a big deal to me and helps connect more dots. I am very impressed!

So far I have learned doing CT constantly how it has benefited me ,,for instance Jack mentioned morning sunlight and defecation within 30 minutes now realizing my circadian rhythms are on point

The big one that hit home was learning my receptors for testosterone are clean from Deuterium so its not a case of increased testosterone but my receptors becoming more sensitive to the Hormone.
As I have noticed the last 4 months or so my sex drive has increased and especially the hours after Tub CT it constantly builds, now I know why.

The questions have been great so far and I love the comfortable casual flow between Jack and Jeremy, the ambience is spot on helping the viewer to focus on both with no distractions.
