# Thread: Live show with Jack, Jeremy and Ben
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-show-with-jack-jeremy-and-ben.20592/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inna — 2017-12-18T14:44:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-show-with-jack-jeremy-and-ben.20592/post-230546>

I am still in awe from yesterdays live talk with Jack, Jeremy and Ben.
It made a huge impression on me, to see a real human beings behind the writings and social media posts.

I am especially in awe from Jeremy’s story! I am a pulmonologist and am very familiar with cystic fibrosis and the sad fate our patients succumb to.
Even though life expectancy in cf has improved from 20 years ago (when i was doing my pulm/cc training), the quality of life really sucks and unfortunately those young people are seriously crippled during those extended years of their lives.
I always had a feeling that we could do better, that my profession is failing those people.
And Jeremy is a living proof of that!
Of what optimal nature based approach could do for people with cf.
It was so refreshing to see Jeffrey living his life to the fullesteven even with burdens of CF which considered deadly genetic disease. Jeremy was talking about touch so beautifully, it definitely touched me on many levels, i had tears in my eyes just from listening.

. I am inspired, and would love to do anything i can be part of the solution.

Ben did wonderful job filming the show. I loved the lightening, So soothing fot the eyes (and soul).

I wholeheartedly share Jacks view of contemporary “evidence based” BS medicine.
I became a platinum member at Jacks site and would love to get training to be a better doctor for my patients.
Am very excited about new optimal health farm center that Jack is building. Please count me in!!!

I wish i could go to member event in Mexico, however i only have a couple of days off and have to be at work 12/29, might be still worth it to go for just 2 days.

I can tell for sure, my dopamine levels were risen just from watching the live show, and i can only imagine how much more it would be from meeting Jack and members of this site in person.

Much love to you all!

Inna

### JanSz — 2017-12-18T15:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-show-with-jack-jeremy-and-ben.20592/post-230553>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

you talk about diseases mimicking genetic disease
to figure it out you suggest D depletion trial

Question:

what level of deuterium in tissues you would try to achieve and for how long?

### JanSz — 2017-12-18T16:55:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-show-with-jack-jeremy-and-ben.20592/post-230564>

Jeremy explains how to kiss grandma.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-18T18:24:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-show-with-jack-jeremy-and-ben.20592/post-230573>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=230553)
>
> @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
> at time 3:17:10
> you talk about diseases mimicking genetic disease
> to figure it out you suggest D depletion trial
>
> Question:
>
> what level of deuterium in tissues you would try to achieve and for how long?
>
> ..........

Real answer is no one knows. My threshold is that you compare your age to expected heteroplasmy rate via Wallace's work and then start at tissue ppm of 130-135. Try to get as low as possible to lower heteroplasmy from decades 3-10 as much as you can. The Redox labs will help you track this as you go. MRI of T1 images is how I do it in NS. I think it is most accurate measure with labs and that is why I use them.

### kris90 — 2017-12-19T16:21:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-show-with-jack-jeremy-and-ben.20592/post-230669>

I feel very privileged to have made these discoveries through this community at the young age of 27. What minor health hiccups I experienced in my last 10-15 years have all vanished, and based upon how I feel and function, I would assume my heteroplasmy rate is relatively low, or normal for my biologic age.

I will just continue to prioritize sun, grounding, cold, local water, ketosis w/ DHA, and seasonal carbs going into my 3rd decade of life in the next coming years and that's all I really need. We'll see how I do, and if things change, then relocation will be considered, but for now, I'm doing well at the 45th latitude!

I think context is super important, because we have all lived mismatches for X amount of time, so you need to assess the depth you dug your grave (increase heteroplasmy) to know how much work you have to escape (lower heteroplasmy). Now we know it's all about depleting deuterium come that 3rd decade.

### drezy — 2017-12-19T16:45:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-show-with-jack-jeremy-and-ben.20592/post-230670>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=230553)
>
> Question:
> what level of deuterium in tissues you would try to achieve and for how long?
> ..........

I know it's not as easily thrown into a spreadsheet but I think it's worth tracking those little quirky ways your body shows duress. For me, I've gotten the resolution down to dry cuticles, minor lower back pain, healing speed minor nicks and scrapes on my hands (turns out I can't do a single project without giving some blood to the project). It all sounds mundane, but if two or three of these issues creep once then I think it's proof enough that I'm adrift in the wrong direction either deuterium, sun, cold , or otherwise and it's my job to get on it. I think it's worth mapping these things out for yourself.

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=230669)
>
> I feel very privileged to have made these discoveries through this community at the young age of 27. What minor health hiccups I experienced in my last 10-15 years have all vanished, and based upon how I feel and function, I would assume my heteroplasmy rate is relatively low, or normal for my biologic age.

It's a great find at your age for sure. I think you are way closer to the age you \*should\* be now, at least if you abandon age as a number and instead think of it as a health/hetroplasmy game. I will warn you once you look at it that way every once in a while, if you squint just a little bit in a crowd, you might wonder if you are in the beginning of a zombie apocalypse or some slower burning [Richard Matheson novel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_Matheson#Novels).

### Susie Cahill — 2017-12-21T11:17:47+0200

The webinar was fantastic and yes, please do more ASAP. I found it so easy to listen to and I didn’t want it to end. Ben was right about it being easier to take in. So excited to hear about the farm, that’s just brilliant. When can I book! I’d heard about Jeremy, wow, what a guy. It was great to see him and listen to his plans for his book. It’s sound fantastic and I’m keen to buy a copy as soon as it’s published. # Thread: Replacing my florescent bulb
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-12-19T15:03:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230660>

Hi Everyone,

Where I rent, my kitchen has a florescent light. Can I replace the bulb to make it non florescent. This may be a dumb question but are they made only for florescent bulbs or is the fixture what makes it a florescent bulb. If I can replace the bulb can I use a reptile bulb.

Edit: actually I think if I can just put in an incandescent bulb would be better?

### JanSz — 2017-12-19T15:30:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230661>

You have to get another lamp.

The is variety of incandescent bulbs, when shopping for new lamp have with you the bulb in question.

...

### Billybats — 2017-12-19T17:10:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230677>

That's what I figured I will have to do is get a different light source. So I guess just a regular lamp with an incandescent bulb. Then maybe a full spectrum lamp and bulb for the living room area. Thank you JanSz.

### DavidL — 2017-12-19T17:21:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230679>

Incandescent is better because it contains IR. Some incandescents have measured in the UV range. Halogen are good too because they have infrared. My incandescents are soft white, red, or orange. I used to use the daylight bulbs but that was before I learned about light.

### Billybats — 2017-12-19T19:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-my-florescent-bulb.20595/post-230689>

Thank you.. I have all incandescent except for the kitchen. I wasn't sure if I can just put a incandescent bulb/tube in that fixture but I guess not. I have been thinking of putting a lamp in the kitchen for a while now with an incandescent bulb and taping the switch for the florescent light so we don't turn it on...lol So it's going to be incandescent and candles which we have been using more. Thank you guys.

### Patrick K — 2017-12-19T18:54:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ddw-machines.20596/post-230684>

Hello,
This machine looks like it would do a good job at making ddw:

[http://ctsystems.ua/?page\_id=18](http://ctsystems.ua/?page_id=18)

Anyone ever try it out?

Seems like a pricey but effective machine...

### WalterNL — 2017-12-19T20:04:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ddw-machines.20596/post-230691>

Don't believe this will reduce D content a lot. But if anyone wants to test it...

Literature is based around repeated fractional distillation to significantly reduce D in water using industrial means.

### Patrick K — 2017-12-19T20:22:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ddw-machines.20596/post-230693>

I know ...reading that D freezes at 3.8 celsius...seems pretty simple:
Seems like you need :
1 Thermometer (in the water), keep water between lets say between 2 and 4 degrees to be safe, the bad stuff will harden, you separate the 2 and voilà...
dunno how much of a dent in the D content it would make but if the 3.8 is true...it would logically work.

Will try soon and prob would have to send a sample to a lab that can test D content in ppms.

On a different note: I am in Quebec and this water looks pretty darn good at first glance (not sure...):
[www.eskawater.com](http://www.eskawater.com)
Its basically snow water from up north filtered in rocks (they say it takes 15 years to produce this natural water)....

Anyone using it?

### DavidL — 2017-12-19T20:43:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ddw-machines.20596/post-230694>

I've had Eska. I would suggest emailing the company asking about the isotopes in the water.
Or get it tested by a lab.

# Thread: Curiosity has it's own reason for existing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huma — 2017-12-20T12:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-230714>

Sobbing as I write this, heart gushing pain as I write this.

What breaks my heart the most is my mum. I thought if I try hard enough I could save her from pain. Now as I go deeper down the rabbit hole- I know how hard it’s going to be.

Psychosis
On the autistic spectrum
Can’t internalise her thoughts- constantly talking to herself, looped conversations again and again
Paranoia and delusions
Sleep problems
Asthma, lungs rattle when she breathes
Chronic pain
Oral thrush
Type 2 diabetes
Chronic stress from abuse, lived an isolated life, never got sunlight, meal times, chronic bluelight exposure, meal times all wrong, no exposure to the elements, SAD diet, never ate seafood

Came to Dr Kruse via Wim Hof.

I guess darkness fuels me- knowing I may not be able to save my mum, but having his burning need to at least get my head around Dr Kruse’s masterpiece, and get the message out to the rest of the world. In a way that their low dopamine brains can handle (takes one to know one), in a way that doesn’t freak them out and overwhelm them- with lots of visuals (humans love and often learn better with pictures) and humour. Alleviate their suffering, in a way that inspires them, entertains them, gives them hope to change their lives, and change the destiny of humanity. Only way that’s going to happen for most of humanity is small wins, small wins that build the momentum for change.

With Team Krus’ blessing I want to create the gateway drug for Jackkruse.com. A website in the vein of WaitbutWhy and The Oatmeal, that takes the blogs that Dr Kruse makes freely available, but breaks them down into “infotainment”. People don’t do what good for them, people do what’s “trendy” and “cool”- and follow the herd. We have to create a global movement, get the Beyonces, Kardashians and Justin Biebers of the world wearing blue blockers and get it trending on Insta and Snapchat.

One of my favourite writers Caitlin Moran says “You influence things through culture; that’s how we change things. Like in the U.K., I can trace the fact that we now have equal marriage in our country to a gay Welsh writer Russell T. Davies. He wrote _Queer as Folk_—the original _Queer as Folk_, then took over _Doctor Who_. And then Captain Jack Harkness (a character on Dr Who), kissed another man on prime time. And on the playground on Monday, kids in my daughter’s class, boys, were fighting to play the role of Captain Jack Harkness in school. And you know, he was absolutely accepted straight away and people love that character, and that’s just something that only art can do. Like especially in the world, no equality in ministerial government can you make 10-year-old boys want to play-act to be a bisexual man. There are some things that only culture can do. You know, art is about possible futures.

A little over a year ago, I was a smoking, binge drinking, party pill popping mess. Deep depression and anxiety. Waking up even more exhausted than when I when to bed, suicidal ideation was a refuge from reality.

Grew up in abusive home with plenty of neglect, pulled out of home at 15 and became ward of the state. I have a long way to go, lucky I’ve always had an open mind, would always have taken the Red Pill. But my low dopamine + serotonin muted my childhood curiosity and focus.

I realised in order to be the person I want- actually, need to become, I have to burn away my deadwood, and get comfortable with uncomfort. I’ve gradually been burning away comfort blankets, and wow, has that transformed things.

Oh, in terms of health, never took care of it until I developed symptoms of leaky gut last year. Am going to get highly sensitive CRP, Vitamin D and DHEA and 23&Me tests shortly. Hormones have been a mess since I was little. Born and raised in the donut hole of Oz, work on a huge trading floor with hundreds of computers surrounding me, 12 foot screens beaming down never ending new tragedies and stock quotes. Sandwiched between a datacentre humming with hundreds of servers upstairs, and backup power generators underground below. Flanked by 2 tv stations. And an electrical substation...

...And I used to think I felt drained at the end of a shift because my coworkers were lame…

So here I am stupid enough to think I can make a difference, but smart enough to know how hard it’s going to be.

Here's a draft sample of what I was thinking, blog/comic:

Working Title: How eating a banana in winter will kill you

Goddamn it.

You were doing so well with your diet, but here you are standing in front of the office vending machine again.

\*\*\*insert cartoon:

What you see:

Bright, colorful Willy Wonkaesque machine spurting out rainbow candies, playful giant gummy bears smiling. Trevi-like fountain bubbling with cola. winged candies flying around like angels

Vs reality:

Dimly light fluorescent box, cylindrical can of corrosive chemicals and brown coloring, cylindrical can of orange coloring, red bottle of sugar water, gelatine + corn syrup + chemical in the shape of tiny bears. Tiny squares marked with poison symbol\*\*\*\*

Then the drudgery of your day hits you.

\*\*\*\*cartoon of character trudging through snow on the road by himself into oncoming traffic\*\*\*\*

\*\*\*\*cartoon of Sisyphus-like character rolling up a boulder- but in to style/ foreground of Super Mario\*\*\*

Reporting to your moron of a boss:

\*\*\*\*cartoon of Sisyphus presenting work to boss- boss probably a dinosaur (pterodactyl?) stuck in it’s old ways.
Sisyphus: “I also took the liberty of updating all our templates to refer to the correct segments of the Corporations Act and the Operating Rules”

Dinosaur: “There’s a new company policy, all templates need to have blue triangle on the top right hand side- could you update them all again? \*\*\*\*\*

Dealing with your clients:

\*\*\*\*\*\*cartoon character on phone saying “Yes I understand your frustration sir. I’m here to help, but I haven’t be equipped with the ability to help” character is steamrolled by boulder- blue triangles fly everywhere\*\*\*\*\*

No, fuck that! You’re sticking to the diet,

\*\*\*\*cartoon pulling out banana\*\*\*\*

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-20T16:10:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-230726>

Welcome. You should never focus on mom.........she is her own entity. Focus on you. I mentioned this in the live Q & A with Jeremy and Ben on 12/17. You might have a look at that re-broadcast. Wasting your time on things that are not critical to you is a waste of time. Focus in on you in nature.

There is nothing more true than nature for living system. When you have something that you know is true, even over the long term, you can afford to put a lot of energy into it. The mitochondriac will always focus on nature that is virtually guaranteed to be valuable in the future no matter what else happens in your life.
Nature is a powerful foundation to build your health plan around. I’d take that a step further: focusing on stable knowledge around circadian biology is a powerful approach to build a healthy life around because it creates wisdom in our colony of mitochondria. This is why the quatum clinician will refuse in using the paradigm's numbers of predicting disese for patients. Prediction rain doesn’t matter; but building arks to protect your flock does.

### drezy — 2017-12-20T16:18:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-230727>

Energetic much?Lol.

With similar experiences and a mom that is in deep crap health wise I can recommend, from experience, that the first time you start absorbing all this and get and urge to save everyone or anyone, instead make sure(like the airlines say) your firmly affix your own mask before helping others. In fact, repeat this the first 50 times you get that urge.

I've found that by drastically improving your own health about 10% of people notice and about 10% of them ask "how" and about 10% of those people try it for themselves. For me at least actively pursuing that 1% is exhausting and like looking for a needle in a haystack.

### Inger — 2017-12-20T19:05:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-230742>

Welcome, Huma!
I know how hard it is as a woman not to try to help others on your own cost...we have this mother love/heart deep buried in us... but we can do it! I always remind myself what Jack uses to say, who are you good for if you are not good enough for you? The best gift we can give people around us and the world is to take good care of us . It just pays forward
It does hurt so much to see others go down and not being able to turn around. But still it is their choice but it is so sad because they mostly was just clueless, an later.. it might be too late because they cant even think straight...
Yes, let us shine the light, and start with us   I tell what I learn and do everywhere I go, it sure makes life worth living    especially when you see that people you love get better

### drezy — 2017-12-20T20:14:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-230748>

Maybe it's just my rougher fatherly heart, but I think there is an unintended but powerful gift and an answer to a question that you might not have asked unless you were a mitochondriac and it shouldn't be overlooked:

If I choose to live like a modern zoo human how will it play out for me?

Your Mother and siblings answer that question for you whether you asked it or not.

Out of all the tests mentioned here on the forum, I think this one is sadly very informative but it is so expensive and heartbreaking. It costs you something that cannot be repurchased or replaced.

Jobs, cash flow, or other perceived boundaries to optimizing your environment aside, please do not fail to apply what you learn from this most expensive of tests.

I think that when you sign up to being a mitochondriac you sign up for some heartbreak. It's in the small print but it's writ large across these forums.

### Leslie hamud — 2018-01-01T22:43:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-231388>

Huma, This is my 2 cents worth. I have read research that our children leave stem cells in their momma bear. The research I read on this was that it was a survial skill, as the new born is not ready to walk or take care of themselves. Nature gives the mother the extra stem cells for repair and to further the surveil chances of the child. I feel to some degree that there is still a relationship with that stem cell even thou it no longer resides in us. That maybe, if we, the children, take care of our self we are in turn taking care of that stem cell that lies in the momma bears belly somewhere. Its like striking the tuning bar, maybe a C note and the other tuning bar across the room in a C note vibrates. So what Im saying is taking care of yourself is taking care of that stem cell in your mamma and if it starts to vibrate high enough you may very change her course of direction and if not you will become the light for her to follow. Only if she choices thou.... Thats where letting go and trusting is important. Take care...

### Huma — 2018-01-09T12:27:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-232073>

Moving all health tracking notes here to centralize/ keep me accountable.

Road to My Dezombiefication

May 29 2016 30th Birthday- decide to run away to yoga retreat- soothes distraught brain.
UPDATE: 02/01/18 hit many nails, no Wi-Fi or cell reception, in nature, got lots of sun, organic unprocessed food, chill people.

June 5- Back in Sydney. Will do yoga for 30 days- see how I feel

June 6- blood test
June 10- Get results, doc concerned by:
Low Vitamin D= 31nmol (50-140)
GGT=103 umol/L (5-35)
ALT= 58 u/L (5-30)

June 16- 10 day Paleo Detox + 7 hours sleep at least per night + half hour jog/run in morning.
Withdrawal from sugar>withdrawal from cigarettes.
17th June- Rage against the Caffeine f\*cken angry and tired- nap thrice
Can't turn to booze, caffeine or sugar- feel like rug been pulled from under me
Wow- lost 3 inches off waist and 3 inches off thighs.
Result:
Didn't realise I was feeling that sh\*tty until I stopped feeling it. Much less foggy. Don't wake more exhausted than the night before!!!! Still super jet laggy feeling- night shift sucks..
Amazing feeling in control- not being goaded by substance.
Steady energy through day- no more peaks and crashes.

July 5th- end of yoga experiment- Back pain and tightness gone!

Decide to continue paleo- exercise is running- UPDATE 02/01/2018 lol only sporadically.

Aug 1- start cold showers in prep for Wim Hof event- don't wanna be only p\*ssy not able to handle icebath.. Feels like shards of glass- like a form of torture- curse way through it. Relatively not even that cold lol :/ Amazing tool to build the self discipline I don't have.
UPDATE: 02/01/18- most of showers still cold- out of preference-refreshing

Aug 2nd blood test:
GGT=92 umol/L (5-35)
AST= 40 umol/L (10-35)
ALT= 58 u/L (5-30)
Tests show no hepatitis or fatty liver.

Aug 13th: Start Mindlab Pro

Aug 14 - Wim Hof event: crowd approx 65% male, skewed to early twenties. Holotropic breathing- very psychedelic but different. Described it as being able to see into my cells. Meet Wim, very warm and charismatic- expect him to say something profound- is flirty instead.Unexpectedly bolder than most- jump in communal icebath without hesitation. Unreal. Still. Intense. Get out and trembling so hard, babbling excitedly. Convince bunch of strangers to jump in again with me. Euphoric and buzzing-beam all the way to home. Get home late 1:30 AM- leave for work at 6. So much energy can't sleep for the rest of night. Actually feel alive.
15th Aug- Buy Wim Hof course and start 2 month program. Feel more centred, brain less cluttered, powerful.
Record is 42 pushups without air in lungs (first attempt was 2)
Late Aug More lean, more muscle definition, 1.5 kilo belly fat not budging though. Skin getting pretty bad for some reason... hormones? Greasy and breaking out >< Looks like puberty again..

### Huma — 2018-01-09T12:43:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-232074>

27th Aug: Weird feeling while in train, whole body flashed hot- and I felt energy coursing through me. Lasted 5 seconds max seconds, felt awesome!

29th Aug: Mindlab pro working..Feel more focused and working memory better. Can hold numbers in head for calculations instead of having to write them down

September 2: 1st crypotherapy session- not nearly as magical as WM icebath

2nd Oct- Jack kruse recommended video on YouTube- mind blown- this guy is amazing. Don't get it- but it feels like it's the keys to the universe! Sign up for forum. Excited then overwhelmed.
Foundations of thinking smashed.
Can't do leptin reset until nightshift is over... have to change my entire life...

3 Oct- Weird hot feeling- not as intense this time- if this is menopause then I like it!

6- Cut 2 hours of screen time- Wi-Fi off at night, laptop grounded
7th Oct- Start grounding for at least 1/2 a day.

October 9th Blue blockers are G.O.A.T.- sleep like a boss for first time in ages. Will wear religiously after dark. UPDATE 02/01/2018 Still wearing religiously

13th Oct- on night shift- 2am on trading floor. Only person there Turn off overhead lights, hundreds of computers on. Panic reading Jackkruse.com. listen to podcast with Aussie lady Feel super weird feeling of wellbeing in whole body.

Read on JK cry < icebaths. Doh! Already bought set of sessions.

Week ending in 29th Oct- do 4 sessions of cryo in a week- get 3 large patches of cold
urticaria on legs.
F%!\*en hell.
Will have to focus on food.

Stop flouride whitening toothpaste.
Buy remineralising toothpaste.
Urgh, teeth actually monstrous unmellow yellow.
UPDATE: 02/01/18- teeth way brighter! (Except two top left, still discoloured- though not as gross as before)

Existential depression for days- take time off work to lie in bed and cry. Didn't want it to be true, world's fucked. Road to optimal is going to be brutal. Can't even LR- still on night shift.

27th Oct- Go to national park- ground all day
5th Nov-Install RO system that removes fluoride.
Nov 7: Go to beach- can't remember last time I went.
Candles in room after dark- talk about mood lighting.
UPDATE 02/01/18 still candling

9th Nov 5:45 wake up-Start of LR
\- drown mackerel in ACV + overly pungent spicy tomato sauce + eggs.
Is breakfast until further notice.
Previous seafood record- 3 times in whole life.
UPDATE 02/01/18- eating seafood 7-11 times a week. Grown to...almost like it?!

Getting so bloody tired in evenings.. sleeping 9/9.5 hours-used to allot 7 hours. Hope this is temporary...

12th Nov, at best getting half hour morning light- 1.5 hours evening, 1/2 h midday.

8th Dec- Try no bulletproof coffees in the morning. shattered- need to lie down for a couple of hours. Not good for whatever kind of fatigue I have- but desperately need to prop self up into semblance of functional person.

14th- beach!
12th- Try no coffee again. Exhausted, limbs heavy, can't focus. Eat too much dark chocolate instead.

16/12/17
Face dunks for 2 weeks- begin at 6 seconds, feel like drowning max record 19 secs-calm. Want to keep pushing record further but no time.
Blood test:
Vitamin D= 75 nmol (was 31nmol) (50-140)
GGT= 60 (was 103 umol/L) (5-35)
ALT= 33 (was 58 u/L) (5-30)
HS CRP 0.6 mg/L (0.0 - 5.0)
DHEAS: 8.1 umol (2.4 -13)

Doc wanted to check:
Testosterone 0.9 nmol (0.2-1.8)
SHBG 156 nmol (30-110)
FAI 0.6 % (0.3-4)
Prolactin 216 mIU/L (85-500)
Basal 17OH-Progesterone 2.3 nmol/L (0.3 - 3.3)

19th- National Park!

19 Dec- Now drinking VOSS + Icelandic water only

20/12/17 smattering of acne gone. Hair shinier, much less split ends (Also stopped bleaching it)

Move on to ice on torso + chest + compression shirt. 1 hour- too easy. Skin numb, light pink

Ice on torso + chest. 1 hour- wtf still too easy- am I doing this wrong? Remember the cryo guy was impressed with ability to stand cold- thought he said that to everyone...Skin numb, light pink

31st Dec- first DIY CT. Water not cold enough 19C. Iceblocks on torso + chest. Stayed for half hour- could have fallen asleep in there.
1st jan, Hear Mum moaning in pain as I eat BAB in sun- feel no emotion- become pragmatic/asshole or too early in morn to get emotional? Feel little fatigued in morning- but during day quite energetic- SA-WEET!

28 Dec. First nude sunbath- nervous/self conscious. Notice amongst only 3 people on ferry that are sitting in sun. Everyone else inside. At beach awkwardly remove clothing- kinda freeze- as if a lack of movement will stave off attention, engage in fierce no-eye-contact-while-staring-determinedly-at the ocean position. Does not deter guys from trying to have a chat.

1 Jan 2018
Anniversary of:
1.3 years of quiting uncontrollable binge drinking/partying .
1.5 years quit smoking
6 months no processed foods/ gluten/dairy or PUFA.
2 months negotiated out of doing 24 h rotating roster. Almost all of the guys on 24h shift work have chaotic personal lives + early onset of chronic disease, even though young + substance abuse
Skin looks amazing for first time, golden and glowy > grey pallor of undead 2 years ago.
Average getting 20 min morning sun + 30 min solar noon + 2 hours before sunset

2nd Jan
1st proper CT.
Start at 9C by the end of 35 min water is 12C.
Keep feet and hands out voluntarily, knees and nipples out involuntarily- more water next time.

2nd CT 45 min 10-12 degrees. Hands out half feet out.

6th Jan Get super sunburnt at beach!

Eating green **plants are deuterium depleted**, which is why vegetarian diets can be helpful.
Ketogenic diets also reduce deuterium levels because fat is naturally deuterium depleted.
**Fats are deuterium depleted** for two reasons:
1\. Because fat contains carbons and hydrogens that are bonded together and
2\. Because if the animal was grass fed, then it will have lower deuterium levels.
Sleep is important because during REM sleep, you get rid of deuterium

### Huma — 2018-01-22T07:54:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-233106>

4th Jan Quit coffee- feel worse for 2 days, then fatigue kind of lifts.
7th Jan- Finish Leptin Reset after 5 weeks out because of schedule.

Much better sleep
Morning dumps are now on point
No body comp change

10th Jan- CTing 3 days a week, average temp 12c/53F average length 45 min

12th Animals like me more?! Weird Snow White Disney movie moment today- a rainbow lorikeet sussed me out and then chilled with me during morning sunning, sitting on my empty breakfast plate in my lap. Dog’s at park want to play.

14th Much more energy and focus! Incredible- now gained at approx 3 hours a day back in productivity- probably still a C- can't wait to experience an A

15th to 19th Jan- Have to do arvo shift 3-11pm first hardcore after sunset blue light exposure + NNEMF in couple of months.
Most at work not even mentioning blueblockers… Or maybe their suspicions have been confirmed, that I in fact am a weirdo.
Sleep quality degrades
By end of week pimples start reppearing.
Energy levels stable.

Have social media strategy and mission statement for webcomic locked down.
Creative ideas to present info are flowing- need to figure out a way to cite references in an interactive way.
Need to work on atrophied drawing skills + learn the science- super excited and loving the process that's evolving.

17th Jan- Knock back promotion that’ll mean being jacked into The Matrix aka Global Financial Markets relentlessly. Stay in current role that I can do in my sleep, lots of downtime to fill brain up with nature’s laws.

23andMe results- no plot twist- am basically fully curry- 99.7% South Asian- haplogroup U2b- Need to book edu consult with Jack about where to move to + confirm he's cool with how I'm approaching the creation of the webcomic.

21th Jan- Still taking Mindlab Pro- feel like it's a crutch now, have irrational fear that brain will fall apart with it.

### Greenie — 2018-01-22T09:08:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-233109>

> [Huma said:](/goto/post?id=230714)
>
> Sobbing as I write this, heart gushing pain as I write this.
>
> What breaks my heart the most is my mum. I thought if I try hard enough I could save her from pain. Now as I go deeper down the rabbit hole- I know how hard it’s going to be.
>
> I guess darkness fuels me- knowing I may not be able to save my mum, but having his burning need to at least get my head around Dr Kruse’s masterpiece, and get the message out to the rest of the world. In a way that their low dopamine brains can handle (takes one to know one), in a way that doesn’t freak them out and overwhelm them- with lots of visuals (humans love and often learn better with pictures) and humour. Alleviate their suffering, in a way that inspires them, entertains them, gives them hope to change their lives, and change the destiny of humanity. Only way that’s going to happen for most of humanity is small wins, small wins that build the momentum for change.

My condolences in having such a challenging time with your mother, Huma. If it is of any consolation, I empathize with your situation having a mother who is an alcoholic of the most vicious type imaginable. In short, there is nothing we can do to change others; the decision to do so is entirely theirs, and this is often an impossibility if the person takes no responsibility whatsoever for their situation (i.e. plays the victim card exclusively).

All of the love and adulation in the world cannot change this fact unless the person decides to change. I, too, went through this with my mother and other dear friends who were drug addicts. Having to let go does not mean loving the person any less. Acceptance of our inherent limitations can be a real bitch but it can also be a monumental relief that frees energies; these energies can be applied to those who respond positively.

We all have a long way to go, but the importance lies in the journey (i.e. flux), not the destination (i.e. stasis); after all, where exactly are we supposed to be going? Is the point of a sublime piece of music about getting to the end, or a delightful dance about the final move?

We can make a difference, but that difference has to start with oneself. I am not a damned bit of use to anyone if I do not tend to myself and find a proper balance. Selfish? Sure, but so is the attempt to act unselfishly.

Dig the cartoon ideas! Art is such a powerful outlet. Music certainly saved my life during my darkest moments. Hang in there! We have far more strength and resilience than we think! Finding a healthy, supportive environment certainly helps.

### drezy — 2018-01-22T14:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-233112>

> [Greenie said:](/goto/post?id=233109)
>
> I, too, went through this with my mother and other dear friends who were drug addicts.

Ill, dysfunctional, and non-optimal mothers gave you at least two gifts:

1.  She brought you into this world and endured the discomfort and pain that it costs
2.  By falling apart based on her choices she effectively gives you a preview of one path that lays in front of you. She dropped road flares on that path for you the same way you'd hope someone would do for you if a bridge were damaged on the highway.

One gift is joyful and one is very sad but both are valuable.

> We can make a difference, but that difference has to start with oneself. I am not a damned bit of use to anyone if I do not tend to myself and find a proper balance. Selfish?

No more selfish than breathing. Just like breathing you can overthink it sometimes.

### Greenie — 2018-01-22T19:40:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curiosity-has-its-own-reason-for-existing.20597/post-233171>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=233112)
>
> Ill, dysfunctional, and non-optimal mothers gave you at least two gifts:
>
> 1.  She brought you into this world and endured the discomfort and pain that it costs
> 2.  By falling apart based on her choices she effectively gives you a preview of one path that lays in front of you. She dropped road flares on that path for you the same way you'd hope someone would do for you if a bridge were damaged on the highway.
>
> One gift is joyful and one is very sad but both are valuable.
>
>
> No more selfish than breathing. Just like breathing you can overthink it sometimes.

Points well taken, though I never meant to imply that nothing positive has come out of my relationship with my mother or that I do not appreciate her sacrifices made. My point was only that the experience ultimately taught me that I cannot change her or anyone else, regardless of how many appeals I offer; this was difficult to accept—hell, it still is at times. Most of us have a capacity from a very early age to recognize which way our proverbial grain goes; such people decide whether to ignore or follow it at any given time.

# Thread: Jan and Sam’s optimum journal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Janet Shold — 2017-12-21T06:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-230773>

I am Janet, a 58 year old mom and grandmother. Sam is my 15 year old daughter, so I’ll be her journalist.

Our haplogroup group is H1A1.

We both seem to have Lyme disease, and anybody familiar with that know that we are always on a search to find ways to fix that. Sam also has hashimotos and vitiligo, both are autoimmune issues. I’ve always had the feeling that Lyme is just something that takes advantage of a modern human who is not in optimal condition, and the failure to be optimal is in our lifestyle. Throwing antibiotics at the one organism (who is clever enough hide and morph And persist just isn’t the answer. That’s my gut feeling, and I feel it bears out in research and experience.

**Healing the Gut**
When I read a few of jack’s posts and blogs, something just clicked. Immediately we got supplies for healing the gut, and are still on that. Wow. Nice, regular poopies. Never underestimate the health power behind a good poop!

**Morning Sun**
Next, we started watching the sun rise. We love the time we spend doing that, as the winter birds are in the yard and surrounding shrubs and trees, and it just feels so good to be out there with them and with the sun. Now, we live on Vancouver island, so we get weeks of cloud.

**Red Fish Blue Fish**
Because we can’t see the morning light everyday, we hack light by avoiding blue light. Blue blocking glasses after sundown and we read by the light of red led bulbs. Holy cow, this alone has Changed our world. LET ME SAY that removing blue light, using blue blockers and reading in the evenings by red light only has already made a profound change for us. Sam (daughter) has been plagued from birth with incredible insomnia. Many nights she would be awake until daylight. She was constantly, chronically unable to maintain a healthy circadian rhythm. After a week of minimizing blue light and using blue blockers and red after sundown this girl is sleeping at night and waking with the sun. As for myself, I’m waking every day at 6:15 and I have never been anything but a “night owl”. **Sperti Red.** We just got the red Sperti for getting vitamin d. We haven’t had blood d levels taken yet, though. I have a sun burn. I got a little carried away. My bad.

**Lugols**
My daughter and I have both been taking lugols 37.5 mg a day, for a couple of years. I’ve wondered about increasing to 50 or more.

**Cold adaption**
We did some face dunking, daughter found it hard. I am working with her to get cold adapted, but it’s going to take a lot of encouragement. Repeating all the wonderful benefits of it is helping, because she is very motivated.

I have been taking cold showers daily for 9 days so far, and also cold packs 3 times a week. I find the showers are easier to tolerate if I leave my feet in a pool of relatively warm water. So I’ll do the cleaning under tepid 70f water with the plug in, and then lower the temp in increments until it’s at 45-55. The cold packs are already a park of my freezing to numb before bee stings for the bee venom therapy for Lyme, Ive just added more, larger jel packs! Perhaps once we are more accomplished motichondriacs (dare I say black swans?), I can forget the bee venom, because our bodies will be able to take care of things without it.

The cold showers are hard to do, but the payback is enormous! After a shower one has energy, a clear mind, the aches and pains are gone or diminished. The energy from a cold shower seems to last for hours and hours. I can’t wait to start using a tub and then the nearby ocean and glacier fed lake.

**Diet**
Our diet is low carb, but not absolutely keto just yet. Adding in the seafood.

\- I’m shopping for a nice sized livestock water trough for some good and icey soaks.

\- I’m getting ready to join and have access to all the information. The incredible changes we have already experience with light and cold is just WOW!

\*\*\*\* Thank you for being here, jack. Thank you, experience members for your input, help and guidance\*\*\*\*

### caroline — 2017-12-21T08:04:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-230778>

You are doing a fantastic job Janet - for you and your daughter! You are indeed a black Swan - in my opinion.

Have you thought of taking your daughter camping? or maybe a cabin in the woods? maybe she would put her feet in a cold stream? Nothing pre meditated ...just something fun to do.

One of the ladies here - Freesia - did amazing things for herself with C/T and had her kiddos doing it as well. They were allowed to shout/use a really bad word of there choice. Swearing and using disgusting language makes C/T so much easier!

Maybe plan a trip to the Yucatan ......

### caroline — 2017-12-21T08:05:20+0200

### Lahelada — 2017-12-22T05:03:42+0200

### Janet Shold — 2017-12-31T18:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-231342>

The last question in the Vermont talk about vitiligo made me shit the bed, just like jack said it would when he answered the question! Sam has several spots of **vitiligo**, and has since around the same time the malasma showed up. I have a black light and an infrared light in her room, and a red Sperti.

We get the morning light, come cloudy or clear weather. I need to get her into the cold, and Sam has been very slowly doing it by not using warm water for hand washing, wearing fewer clothes, and using ice packs, but she gets dizzy quickly when showering in **cool** water. I believe this means we have to go very slowly with her. We’ve begun eating seafood regularly.

I am going to get a membership. I’ve been watching and listening to every video and podcast I can find. I may be driving my husband around the bend with this, but I’m insisting he get on board.

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-07T18:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-231894>

I’m sitting on my iPad which is hard wired to the internet! It was a little tricky, but my husband - a non-geek - figured it out and got it done. Our internet is all hard wired as of yesterday, and the router’s wifi has been disabled. I plan to get a router with no wifi in the near future.

Even my grandchildren are avoiding blue light through apps, and one grandson is using blue blocking glasses while on the computer. I’m going to keep on working on them to get their light situation in the evenings hacked properly.

Sam and I always wear the blue blockers after supper. i turn off lights and light some old oil lamps and candles. A little stumbling block on this is my husband, who seems to need a lot of light before retiring for the night. I’ll keep encouraging him.

Sam is working on the cold thermogenesis and is coming along slowly. We all just went through a flu that is going around so I think that slowed her progress on the cold down. I’ve been doing the daily cold showers, and sometimes getting into the tub after the shower and filling it with cold water and some ice.

We’ve been adding more seafood into our diet, but had a setback last week when we had bad prawns. It took several days to clear that meal out!

Morning light: Vancouver island weather is not cooperating! Cloud, fog, rain...I understand that even when it’s overcast there is about 30% of the morning light there to absorb, but man!

I’m going to sign up as a member with skin in the game, but wonder if I can change my name on the forum to be a little more incognito, first. I don’t think I need to share our personal information with the whole interwebs going forward.

> We both seem to have Lyme disease,

\----------------
**A word about CD57+ testing:**
\-------------------

**Treatment**

1.  **If you are dealing with Lyme, it must be treated with 3 weeks of antibiotics to kill the spirochete bacteria** and then try to clear the biotoxins. When this bacteria is killed, expect a serious Herxheimer reaction and your symptoms to get worse before they get better. There is a treatment option for this, however, to mitigate this biochemcal process.

\--------------------------------------

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-09T20:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-232117>

I’ve had lyme with symptoms that range wide, from sore joints, headaches and insomnia to too-tired-to-breath fatigue for years. Lyme persists after antibiotic treatment. I know of many people who have treated with antibiotics, had relief of their symptoms, only to relapse after treatment. This is why we have come here, because I know the only way back to health is jacks way.

It seems that many people are exposed to the spirochete, yet don’t get ill. I didn’t get Noticeably sick until years after a tick bite(s), and then it was a hard crash that put me in bed for a couple of years. As far as we can tell, my daughter was exposed in utero. It really sucks!

[http://ajp.amjpathol.org/article/S0002-9440(17)30894-5/pdf](http://ajp.amjpathol.org/article/S0002-9440\(17\)30894-5/pdf)

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-18T17:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-and-sam%E2%80%99s-optimum-journal.20599/post-232843>

We’ve been to get blood tests and waiting for the results. I sent an email off to support to upgrade our account - I think that’s how it’s done? I’ll put the blood tests results on here as soon as we have them, and post the 23andme reports on our journal.

It’s the dead of winter here, and there has been no morning sun in over two weeks, just feels like a waste of time to go out in the morning to get rained on because it is so DARK. I know we have to get out of here in the winters for sam’s health. Looking at Thailand, because my brother lives there and it’s super inexpensive to rent and eat - and the food is supposed to me incredible. But I’d love to get Sam to the member meetup.

Is there any way to get oysters to NOT taste like oysters? Lol. We’ve been having salmon for supper several times a week, and our dogs have been enjoying my oyster recipes...

### Billybats — 2017-12-21T16:31:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-the-100-000-2018-transformation-jack-kruse-way.20600/post-230793>

Hello All,

Well I wasn't going to tell anyone I was going to do this transformation through bodybuilding site. I wanted to keep kind of a journal to keep me accountable for myself, whether I make it or not. I want to see if I make it the whole way because I didn't last year. Last year I feel really crappy and wasn't physically in the right mind..basically I was ill still overcoming my near death experience. I am going to do it in Jack Kruse guidelines. I will post before pictures..yep you heard me... and no laughing.. lol. I want to do this for myself and I can use the money and who knows where it will go from there. I said the only things that I have changed are the blue blocker glasses and what made a big difference is putting a blue blocker on my tv that never comes off, since doing that my sleep has changed soooooooo much. I also have added a basic multi vitamin and 1/2 dose of b6 I got rid of the other supplements (coq10 and L- gutamine) I know supplements, but they are helping until I can get more morning sun. Overall I am feeling not to bad. So just those changes helped me feel more sure I can to this and feeling fed up with feeling crappy. The changes I am in the process of making are:

My environment- Changing my job soon so I am not on computers as much or at all and my schedule will be later to get my morning sun. I maybe working with toddlers at a private preschool, well see.
My light in the kitchen.
Moccasins with leather soles so I can workout with them outside.

CT- I have been doing some but going to really put my all into it just to see what happens with doing minimal weight lifting.

My diet- Eat optimal which this I haven't done in while, just being honest.

I know there are other things but drawing a blank at the moment. So I am going to try and come to this thread and keep updating.

### Billybats — 2018-01-03T16:37:18+0200

Hey everyone,

I don't know if its this cold weather or what. I don't think its the cold because I like how I feel out there and tomorrow we are suppose to be getting snow and Friday 0 degrees with minus wind chill 40-50 gusts. How do I cold adapt outside, should I be wearing a hat, would it be the same as sitting in the tub with gloves and a hat. The other thing is at work my area doesn't get any warmer the 65 degrees so its colder the any other area of the building and home, I guess that is good but not sure.

I started the Epi-paleo diet and I know I need a lot of healing or is it normal to feel funky in the head and upper back area and my ringing in my left ear is worse. Sooooo I am thinking my body is borderline shot but way better then it was 3 years ago. The feeling in my head is not bad but weird and annoying. It feels like there is just slight swelling if that is possible in the hypothalamus/pituitary area or it's my back muscle being stiff. This gets worse when I try to go keto and not eat sugar/bread. Is this a detox. I can focus okay but last night I had to take aleve and ice pack to my upper back to go to sleep so I didn't wake up with a migraine so I didn't lose work. I feel better and slept ok, not too bad. Now back to the cold weather,

As for my pictures they should be coming this weekend or before the 14th deadline.

As for the diet that I only started yesterday I had eggs for breakfast, tuna with mayo and pickles but a few hours late had some nuts and a few m&ms I know not perfect, then dinner had raw oysters, cooked shrimp with broccoli and mushrooms. I had a craving for sweets alot but I had glycine that was sweet and took some of that. I did stop my multi, l glutimine and coq10. I may start the multi back up not sure. This morning I made a drink with water, turmeric, cinnamon, and lemon to see how that helps my head.

Waiting on that job interview for a teachers assistant for toddlers, even if they have wifi at the school it bets sitting all day at work in front of a computer and not enjoying my work.

# Thread: oysters a key source of 1H.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-12-22T00:44:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-230815>

Why are oysters a mitochondriac's best fuel source for the hydrogen furnace called the matrix? [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2992993/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2992993/)

### Brent Patrick — 2017-12-22T05:57:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-230829>

Bingo WOW thanks Jack I have been thinking about Oysters a lot lately and why they are such a phenomenal food

### LieselK — 2018-02-21T03:38:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-235249>

Speaking of oysters... I've tried searching for answers, but to no avail... I have tried canned ones and they were tolerable. I just bought a container of "fresh" raw ones... what do I do with them? You swallow them whole? Any tips? We had mussels tonight- those were easy!

### drezy — 2018-02-21T04:23:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-235254>

I bitched and whined to my local grocery manager for awhile. Now I purchase 120+ raw unshucked oysters a week and the grocer seems pretty happy. Apparently other are opting for them now because they sometimes get bought out before I get to them.

I'm even known as "the guy who always buys oysters" according to one of the off duty cashiers.

I've been called way worse so I'm cool with that.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-21T05:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-235258>

[https://theconversation.com/amp/the...ters-and-no-its-not-what-youre-thinking-90697](https://theconversation.com/amp/the-surprising-benefits-of-oysters-and-no-its-not-what-youre-thinking-90697)

### Brent Patrick — 2018-02-21T08:37:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-235266>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=235249)
>
> Speaking of oysters... I've tried searching for answers, but to no avail... I have tried canned ones and they were tolerable. I just bought a container of "fresh" raw ones... what do I do with them? You swallow them whole? Any tips? We had mussels tonight- those were easy!

I consume on average 4 dozen a week Raw this is my breakfast , I have them with cracked pepper , lemon juice squeezed on top I also squeeze an extra lemon and have that in a shot glass . Others may chew but I just swallow down the hatch.
make sure you have turmeric to help with absorption of DHA into the brain I make Ghee with **local** grass fed butter and I add liberal amounts of Turmeric Powder to the finished Ghee. Once set I eat a teaspoon with the Oysters each time.

I notice increased cognitive function and excellent energy levels way into the afternoon.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25550171](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25550171)

How are you storing them before use?

### drezy — 2018-02-21T21:49:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-235308>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=235301)
>
> How are you storing them before use?

Watch out I'm going long winded here.

I used to make homemade kimchi, less these days, but I got voted off the island (or at least out of the kitchen fridge) when our ice started tasting like kimchi (fermented fish sauce, fermented shrimp, bubbling cabbage). I have a mini fridge in my garage because of that and now it has become the oyster fridge for similar ice taste reasons.

With her noticeable improvements on the diet I'll get frowns if I don't serve up oysters each dinner so I buy 18-36 at a time unless I can snag a 50 count bag. My little garage fridge does the trick. I have it set so that If I place the oysters in the tip-top of the unit they will be slightly below freezing. Oysters where the water in the shell is just starting to freeze is like a little delicacy that emerges in a few of the oysters stored in that area.

When those little partially frozen gems show up on the serving platter it's pretty much a Mexican standoff at my dinner table and my wife doesn't feel bad at all about leaving me in the dust.

It's all fine by me because her words have magically changed over this whole process.

Before:
"We need to double check with our finance guy on every investment. We also need to touch base with all of our son's. Oh and let's set a goal for the power bill this month. My boss's daughter is graduating can you find out what's in style in the $25 dollar range. I'm worried about the body language of that lady on the team..." <--- All spoken in under 6 seconds

After:
"Hey, when are we going to get a little alone time together?"

I seem to remember you have a husband so it won't surprise you to know that somewhere on that Y chromosome we husbands have evolved the ability to virtually shrink our ears in one of those situations OR virtually grow our ears and become world class listeners in the other situation much in the same way dogs somehow spontaneously become flawless experts at the English language when you say "Who wants to take a walk?"

Much emphasis heroes on personal optimization and rightfully so. It probably should have been obvious that with us both improving our relationship was bound to improve too. We are both still so surprised though at how much more smooth the whole relationship got all around, not just those areas my Y chromosome is especially attuned too.

There is a restaurant near you called Shuckers Oyster Bar. If they can get hands on oysters you should be able to find a source too somehow.

### drezy — 2018-05-15T19:03:56+0300

FWIW I keep mine in a small fridge for 3-4 days at a time just making sure they can "breathe" for the last year. My wife has eaten them more often the me. 85%+ of her dinners for the last year were fresh shucked oysters.

### Inger — 2018-05-15T19:21:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-241252>

I keep them in my fridge after I have washed and brushed them clean. Make sure to put them the right way so the water cant come out if they open up a bit, because sometimes they do. Then they will spoil faster. Stored the right way and in the fridge they keep at least 2 weeks. I have had 3 week old too.. but it is a bit risky . Never got sick from an oyster though! And I pick them all myself at the shore. Very seldom I get one that tastes bad, I just spit it out, thats it. I eat them every day spring, summer and autumn, from 10 to 20 oysters / day.

### Inger — 2018-05-15T19:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-241253>

PS. you can easily store them a few days just on your terrace / outside too, they can tolerate a few days of summer heat, no problem. At least for me that has worked  If the weather is colder, I can keep them outside several days. If I am too lazy to wash them immediately  They often are pretty muddy here you know!

Important is, the oyster is never to be stored airtight. It must be able to breath.

### Billybats — 2018-05-15T19:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-241255>

So you both are saying eating them mostly as my only food for a few days or so is ok. Currently when I eat any other food including other seafood it doesn't feel good, only oysters and seaweed is sounding good. Maybe when I add seaweed with the oysters it will hold me for a while.

### LieselK — 2018-05-16T01:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-241306>

I hear you guys, but I also feel like, if Jack bothered to write it in his published book then I am going to pay attention to it as I can. I'm not stressing about it- just being mindful of what he's saying and what I'm doing. I don't need/want to lose any more weight. I've noticed the difference in body composition for sure and did lose inflammatory weight, but I also feel like I actually need some fat on my body in places other than my gut so I do really want to make sure I'm putting enough nutrients in my body. With 3 kids I can get lazy about taking care of me and it takes constant effort to focus on my needs first. So I'm trying to give myself a template because that is comfortable for me and ensures that I actually put my needs at the forefront. I'm a very routine person. And right now my routine is to slap some avocado on whatever protein I'm eating and snarf some coconut oil :/ He makes it clear that it's not healthy to be ketogenic year-round for most people and that you should definitely incorporate seasonal veggies (carbs) is summer.

### Dean6789 — 2018-05-22T05:53:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-241730>

Great stuff here. Far and away one of my favorite foods.

I've always loved oysters, even before I had any particular thoughts about whether they were good for you or not. I only eat them raw. Although, years ago I did have some steamed. Much better raw. I usually swallow them whole but occasionally chew a little bit. I love the taste of oysters. I also usually squeeze some lemon on SOME of them, but I wonder if the lemon juice rough on tooth enamel or not.

### Inger — 2018-05-22T09:48:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-241742>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=241255)
>
> Thank yooooooou. I put mine in a glass bowl and empty a tray of ice cubes on top and put to the back of the refrigerator. So two weeks tops. Coooool beans. So you both are saying eating them mostly as my only food for a few days or so is ok. Currently when I eat any other food including other seafood it doesn't feel good, only oysters and seaweed is sounding good. Maybe when I add seaweed with the oysters it will hold me for a while.

Billybats, I do not stay full on oysters either, I normally eat something else too when I have them. Or use them as a small meal between breakfast and dinner, about 12-15 oysters. I had about 30 oysters yesterday for dinner but I still had appetite after that, crazy! So I had a big steak on top of them .

At work days (I leave for work around 10:45 AM and come home at 9-10 PM) I normally eat a big breakfast around 8 AM, and then I have 10-15 oysters before leaving for work. Of course I eat a big meal at work too  I think I eat a lot - but might be because my work it physically pretty demanding, working as a waitress on my feet all day, we do not have breaks either, and more that not there are so much work we just stay on our feet and eat our food as fast as we can...and run back to take care of the guests

I am off work today, it is awesome sunny weather, and I am leaving for the oyster beach staying there all day and picking me a 100 oysters or so, because I had my last ones yesterday

I normally have a problem with seafood.
Too much EPA for me, suppresses my AA (verified by multitude of tests).
Also most of the fatty acids test presented on this board by members, had excess of EPA, DHA or both.
Above research does not contain words DHA or EPA.
Above research deals with
Eastern oyster (_Crassostrea virginica_)
But
this research borrows information and refers to:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4926063/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4926063/)
Lipids and Fatty Acids in the Invasive Oyster _Crassostrea gigas_

I am guessing oyster virginica or gigas are generally similar in composition.

Now, in this research fatty acids are addressed, so EPA and DHA
and
large variety of phospholipids are mentioned.

But the most importantly it looks like oysters are the only seafood that contains much more DHA relatively to EPA.
say
about 10% of EPA
and
30% DHA

In my local Asian Supermarket I can get oysters gigas fresh meat for $10/pound
and live eastern oysters of what amounts to about $100/pound or more of meat
Also eastern oysters, boiled in 1/2lb cans
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000EEWZBY/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000EEWZBY/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1)
$26/six pounds so about $4.25/pound

**Question is,
what percentage of daily food should be allotted to oysters.**

> @BrentPatrick Do you happen to how much nutrition is lost by eating cooked oysrers vs raw?

In terms of how much nutrition is lost with cooking boiling etc especially in regards to DHA ..that would depend on times and temptemperature ranges.
Best bet for complete uptake of DHA is to eat raw.
I always eat raw and when out dining will eat oyster Kilpatrick if im with friends who are entry level and give them the experience of eating and enjoying oysters before they experience raw.
Eating cooked/grilled oysters is better than no oysters.
This not being scientific but I know when my blood is saturated with omega 3 if I nick myself shaving I cannot stop the bleed for several hours.
In Australia it is currently winter we have had nice sunny days but low UV...so I have been eating oysters constantly each week to pull in as much full spectrum solar radiation as possible.Everyone around me is sick with the flu I have not been sick once since increasing seafood "cooked" , raw oysters and inclusion of CT the past two winters.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-08-01T03:23:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-247172>

> [Christina Gagnon said:](/goto/post?id=247160)
>
> [@Brent Patrick](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19843/) thanks so much for your info - my lattitude is 38.59 so probably need to consider moving south. Maybe I'll go to the next mitocondriac convention in Mexico too  You probably already know but in order for hemochromatosis to express itself you need to receive the gene from both parents, and if you get the gene from only one parent you can still be a carrier and pass it along to your children so they should be checked too.

"Here is what you won't find in a diet book: REAL SCIENCE OF LIGHT:
This will shock the eye docs (and food guru's writing diet books) because they all were taught to believe in medical school as I was that the anterior chamber of the eye blocks all UV light. The picture below shows that this is false. It turns out recent data over the last ten years now shows that is utter bullocks. Now their disbelief will rise further. The small amount of UV light that does get through is the key stumulus to the melanocortin pathways I wrote about in my Cold Thermogenesis blog from long ago. What does it stimulate? POMC. Recent scientists 92007) have found another surprising link for clinicians beliefs but it has not been well publicized by those in know. The link is between tanning and cancer: p53--a gene long implicated as a cancer suppressor--appears to be key to helping us get a tan to build out more melanin to abosrb even more UV light.

The simple act of catching a few rays that getthrough the lens and cornea sets a complex biological process into motion. In response to ultraviolet (UV) light from the sun the eye is capable of using a second messenger system to activatethe skin cells. This is done via POMC. Skin cells called kerotinocytes begin pumping out melanocyte-stimulating hormone (MSH), which in turn binds to MSH receptors on pigment-producing skin cells called melanocytes which sit right above human fat mass in the subcutaneous space. Thatmass is where leptin sits awaiting to be activated by solar photons to tell the hypothalmus just how much energy is in the body photoelectrically to make a stochastic calculus guess of how muchfood a human would need based upon the incident EMF's from the sun a human actually gets. This starts a cascade of events that leads to melanin production and a nice brown tan that increases our ability to absorb more sunlight because melanin is a UV fluorophore protein. Researchers at Dana Farber went even further. Even artificially raising levels of p53 with small amounts of UV light in cells boosted POMC expression, buttressing the idea that p53 activates POMC. Are you still afraid of the sun? Maybe you should be more worried about your glasses, sunglasses or your IOL implants now huh?In turning on POMC with UV light, p53 coincidentally prompts production of the peptide beta-endorphin, the brain's natural opiate and another POMC product. This opiate is built into the structure ofPOMC and it appears nature has built humans tobecome addicted to solar exposure. How is that fora reversal of forture for our pals in dermatology and opthomology?" JK
[https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn1227-exposing-eyes-alone-to-uv-light-can-trigger-a-tan/](https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn1227-exposing-eyes-alone-to-uv-light-can-trigger-a-tan/)

I am thinking about ivory.
Most ivory in the world comes for Siberia.
**Laptev Sea**
Ivory come from tropical animals.
That suggests that that area used to be tropical. (So the Neandertals maight have lived in tropics or in not as cold climate as we assume now).
Ie; Earth used to spin on different axis then it spins now.
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laptev\_Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laptev_Sea)

Toll noted[\[25\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laptev_Sea#cite_note-vonToll1895-25) sizable and economically significant accumulations of perfectly preserved [fossil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fossil) [ivory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ivory) in recent beaches, drainage areas, [river terraces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_terrace) and river beds within the New Siberian Islands. The later scientific studies demonstrated that the ivory accumulated over a period of some 200,000 years.

> Ivory come from tropical animals.
> That suggests that that area used to be tropical. (So the Neandertals maight have lived in tropics or in not as cold climate as we assume now).
> Ie; Earth used to spin on different axis then it spins now.

I'm serious thinking about small time farming oysters myself. It would involve running a pipe out and pumping in sea water every high tide or so.

### JanSz — 2019-09-17T18:46:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-a-key-source-of-1h.20601/post-278046>

**oysters a key source of 1H.**

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=230815)
>
> Why are oysters a mitochondriac's best fuel source for the
> hydrogen furnace called the matrix?
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2992993/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2992993/)

Wonder if this thread has not got sidetracked.
If I am correct,
I am still not sure if clarifying the situation will change anything.
\------------
What is in the quoted research, is a method of analysis (of oysters).
That method's name is:

Here we apply **high-resolution proton nuclear magnetic resonance (1H NMR)**\-based metabolomic analysis to _C. virginica_
\-------------------------------------------------
The title suggests that oysters are a source of certain (hopefully beneficial) hydrogen.
\-----
The following discussion immediately concentrates on DHA,
because it is one of the most favorites.
\------
The thread was started in Dec 2017, shortly after the discussion on deuterium began.
\------
The abstract of the research contains:

Mitochondrial metabolism appeared most pronounced in the adductor muscle with elevated levels of carnitine **facilitating ß-oxidation, and ATP**, and phosphoarginine synthesis, while glycogen was elevated in the mantle/gills and stomach/digestive gland.
\-------------------------------

These days, Oct 2019, personally, I am on a lookout for increasing energy available to my body, and it looks like it will come from maximizing matrix water production.
That is coming mostly from foods rich in oleic acid, and watching thirst, and not flooding my body with excessive drinking (even when I am drinking only DDW-25).
Sunlight provides information for the system to work,
but
as far as energy is not even close to what this type of food provides (and body requires to function).
\---------------------------------------------------
It happens
that oysters contain only minute amounts of oleic acid, nothing close to pigs lard, butter or avocado oil that are usually considered as good oleic acid sources.

[https://skipthepie.org/finfish-and-shellfish-products/mollusks-oyster-pacific-cooked-moist-heat/](https://skipthepie.org/finfish-and-shellfish-products/mollusks-oyster-pacific-cooked-moist-heat/)

\===========

\===========
**Oleic acid**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oleic\_acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oleic_acid)

Oleic acid is classified as a [monounsaturated](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monounsaturated_fat) [omega-9](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Omega-9_fatty_acid) [fatty acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fatty_acid), abbreviated with a [lipid number](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipid_number) of **18:1 [_cis_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cis%E2%80%93trans_isomerism)\-9**. It has the formula CH3(CH2)7CH=CH(CH2)7COOH

From my post just above this one:
**
Mitochondrial metabolism appeared most pronounced in the adductor muscle with elevated levels of carnitine facilitating ß-oxidation, and ATP, and phosphoarginine synthesis, while glycogen was elevated in the mantle/gills and stomach/digestive gland.**
\-------------------------------
\=============================
Beta oxidation**
In biochemistry and metabolism, beta-oxidation is the catabolic process by which fatty acid molecules are broken down in the cytosol in prokaryotes and in the mitochondria in eukaryotes to generate acetyl-CoA, which enters the citric acid cycle, and NADH and FADH2, which are co-enzymes used in the electron transport chain.

Not to knock them, I think to a retired senior I think a good BM is sometimes a big daily accomplishment after all.

Male babies have the male REM phenomena, so I don't think it's just about sex hormone levels. After puberty I also don't think sex hormone levels are designed to be the same all year - in the wild its related to the light cycle. But it seems to me that mother nature built a programme, via REM sleep, into the blueprint, to keep our equipment healthy all year round, even if sex hormone levels are naturally lower as a result of the season, hibernation, or are age related. Good receptor receptivity (deuterium depletion?) and accepting fluctuating levels seasonally may be healthier for me as a post menopausal woman than trying to achieve the levels of a 25 year old. I could start tweaking my sex hormones exogenously, but I'd need to weigh up the risks very carefully, one of which would be having the levels "same" all year which I see as potentially creating seasonal circadian mismatches, or if my sleep wasn't optimal and I wasn't accessing the programme written into the blueprint, and considering the heteroplasmy rates in my brain and heart.

In T2D and OSA there is no REM sleep:

The article is misleading and wrong when you have the mitochondriac perspective. Why? OSA is associated with transient hypoxia during sleep. Most cancers are associated with high oxygen tensions via the Warburg shift. This is the key. The mitochondriac knows that OSA is braking mechanism cells that are using the development of OSA at night when mitophagy should occur to LOWER oxygen tensions to avoid oncogenesis for as long as possible because a disabled autophagy/mitophagy are prerequisites for oncogenesis. When autophagy/mitophagy is broken oxygenation is low because ECT flow is low as a protection mechanism. The respiratory proteins are stretched out and this favors glycolysis and the PPP over the TCA and urea cycle when O2 is low. This metabolism is favored in obligate glycolytic tissues in humans like embryonic stem cells, RBC’s, and the retina. That is the only reason why OSA and cancer are linked. [https://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2012/05/20/sleep-apnea-tied-to-increased-cancer-risk/](https://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2012/05/20/sleep-apnea-tied-to-increased-cancer-risk/)

### taiyang — 2017-12-22T19:59:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-230861>

1\. I need to eat within 20 minutes of waking.
2\. I need to get red light with the morning sun and eat when the sun is up.
3\. for my job (on which i support a family of 4 including supplying dental and health insurance), i wake up in the dark in the winter months (only).

Question- can i make a fake morning sun environment within which to eat my high protein breakfast and align my hormone signalling? or do i just take the damage of circadian mismatch until spring? any morning sun devices out there???

thanks.
