### taiyang — 2017-12-24T13:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-231005>

Hi Jan- I saw your high morning cortisol on your graph. Do you have any blood sugar issues? i am wondering if high cortisol could be reflected in higher morning blood sugar readings. my morning fasted blood sugar readings have gone down with mitochondriac hacks, and i am wondering if this phenomenon is related to lower cortisol.

For me, paying attention to first- blacking out my sleeping environment, second- wearing blue blockers in the evening/avoiding screens in the evening except for the TV from a distance, third- taking off my glasses when out in the sun, and fourth- turning off the circuit breaker to my bedroom has led to lower morning fasting blood sugar. i don't monitor my blood sugar throughout the day since i'm a little lazy and i am just trying to prevent going back to being pre-diabetic.

here is some data:
in november 2014
age- 49 and suffering from hot flashes at night, poor sleep headaches
average morning fasted blood sugar - \*\*\*106\*\*\* from 22 mornings (forgot to take my bg some mornings)
highest value 119
lowest value 94
weight- around 133 lbs.
in november 2015
age- 50 still suffering from poor sleep/headaches due to hot flashes
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*93\*\*\*from 28 readings
highest value 103
lowest value 82
weight- around 135-139 lbs.
in november 2016 (first summer i spent walking in the city for hours, but with glasses on and a visor. no sunblock)
age 51- still suffering from hot flashes. started playing around with intermittent use of 5 HTP. sometimes it helped with sleep, but not if i had bad hot flashes. then only ice on my head and aleve worked for the head aches and hot flashes. i took it rarely, though.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*102\*\*\* from 29 readings (at this point i sometimes would take two readings consecutively and saw my meter could read 86/91 0r 84/90) I was also getting more and more desperate because of poor sleep and was also rating my hot flashes as well as my blood sugar. i saw that i might have a high morning blood sugar reading of 84-107 and no hot flashes the previous night or extreme hot flashes and a morning blood sugar of 96-106. so my hot flashes did not seem to track with my blood sugar.
highest value 109
lowest value 86
weight around 135-138 (I stopped caring about my weight and hardly have any notes about it. I was really focused on my sleep since my memory was going).
in november 2017 (second summer i spend walking around the city for hours. still have glasses on and a visor. still no sun block)
age 52- i started completely blacking out my bedroom, but forgot when that began. the most important changes aroune october 19 2017 are the uvex glasses and no screens except for tv at a distance after sunset. i start to not wear glasses outside and crack my windows when driving. no supplements at all.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*88\*\*\* from 19 readings
highest value 96
lowest value 81
weight around 137-140 lbs (still not noting it a lot since sleep and cognitive function are my priorities. also fewer notes on calendar because i started sleeping better so not as desperate)
\*\*\*i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom on Dec. 7. I had a hot flash that night, but now is the first time I'm seeing a blood sugar in the 70's. from Dec. 7 to Dec. 24 my average blood sugar is \*\*\*82\*\*\* from 20 readings (some days I forget to measure, some days I take two readings consecutively and get results like 78/80 or 77/87). i am going fuller into perimenopause at 52 as i get my period on dec. 5 at day 60 of my cycle. i am starting to eat more carbs because i feel protected because of the drop in my morning fasted readings. this is probably a mistake, but i am still getting lower fasted readings even with eating more carbs. my weight hasn't gone up and is still around 138 lbs.
i also posted this in the success stories forum.

### JanSz — 2017-12-24T18:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-231018>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=231005)
>
> Hi Jan- I saw your high morning cortisol on your graph. Do you have any blood sugar issues? i am wondering if high cortisol could be reflected in higher morning blood sugar readings. my morning fasted blood sugar readings have gone down with mitochondriac hacks, and i am wondering if this phenomenon is related to lower cortisol.
>
> For me, paying attention to first- blacking out my sleeping environment, second- wearing blue blockers in the evening/avoiding screens in the evening except for the TV from a distance, third- taking off my glasses when out in the sun, and fourth- turning off the circuit breaker to my bedroom has led to lower morning fasting blood sugar. i don't monitor my blood sugar throughout the day since i'm a little lazy and i am just trying to prevent going back to being pre-diabetic.
>
> here is some data:
> in november 2014
> age- 49 and suffering from hot flashes at night, poor sleep headaches
> average morning fasted blood sugar - \*\*\*106\*\*\* from 22 mornings (forgot to take my bg some mornings)
> highest value 119
> lowest value 94
> weight- around 133 lbs.
> in november 2015
> age- 50 still suffering from poor sleep/headaches due to hot flashes
> average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*93\*\*\*from 28 readings
> highest value 103
> lowest value 82
> weight- around 135-139 lbs.
> in november 2016 (first summer i spent walking in the city for hours, but with glasses on and a visor. no sunblock)
> age 51- still suffering from hot flashes. started playing around with intermittent use of 5 HTP. sometimes it helped with sleep, but not if i had bad hot flashes. then only ice on my head and aleve worked for the head aches and hot flashes. i took it rarely, though.
> average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*102\*\*\* from 29 readings (at this point i sometimes would take two readings consecutively and saw my meter could read 86/91 0r 84/90) I was also getting more and more desperate because of poor sleep and was also rating my hot flashes as well as my blood sugar. i saw that i might have a high morning blood sugar reading of 84-107 and no hot flashes the previous night or extreme hot flashes and a morning blood sugar of 96-106. so my hot flashes did not seem to track with my blood sugar.
> highest value 109
> lowest value 86
> weight around 135-138 (I stopped caring about my weight and hardly have any notes about it. I was really focused on my sleep since my memory was going).
> in november 2017 (second summer i spend walking around the city for hours. still have glasses on and a visor. still no sun block)
> age 52- i started completely blacking out my bedroom, but forgot when that began. the most important changes aroune october 19 2017 are the uvex glasses and no screens except for tv at a distance after sunset. i start to not wear glasses outside and crack my windows when driving. no supplements at all.
> average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*88\*\*\* from 19 readings
> highest value 96
> lowest value 81
> weight around 137-140 lbs (still not noting it a lot since sleep and cognitive function are my priorities. also fewer notes on calendar because i started sleeping better so not as desperate)
> \*\*\*i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom on Dec. 7. I had a hot flash that night, but now is the first time I'm seeing a blood sugar in the 70's. from Dec. 7 to Dec. 24 my average blood sugar is \*\*\*82\*\*\* from 20 readings (some days I forget to measure, some days I take two readings consecutively and get results like 78/80 or 77/87). i am going fuller into perimenopause at 52 as i get my period on dec. 5 at day 60 of my cycle. i am starting to eat more carbs because i feel protected because of the drop in my morning fasted readings. this is probably a mistake, but i am still getting lower fasted readings even with eating more carbs. my weight hasn't gone up and is still around 138 lbs.
> i also posted this in the success stories forum.

### taiyang — 2017-12-26T11:38:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-231088>

thanks for answering my questions, jan. i see you have been a member since 2012 and since i have been coming here since the fall of this year, i see that you are helpful to everyone. member participation is key as jack is a busy man. i have a few questions if you have time

\* jan, have you read posts by members who had cancer and had good outcomes with jack's protocols? if so, can you tell me their names so I can read their journals?
\*also, i didn't see CT on your list. do you use it?
\*i am assuming you optimized fatty acid profile by eating more seafood?
\*how did you optimize micronutrients? jack talks a lot about dha being THE food story, but i always felt confused by this as the body needs certain substrates derived from nutrients to function, leading me to think DHA **plus** micronutrients to build substrate are the food story.
I know jack's writings are organized by topic, but i think it would be useful if the members could organize their successes by topic. search doesn't always help or it leads to posts that are only very slightly related to the topic i am searching.

### JanSz — 2017-12-26T16:44:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-morning-hormone-cascade.20604/post-231096>

> [Susie Cahill said:](/goto/post?id=231081)
>
> Hi JanSz
> I’m excited to hear about the Preventa progress. Did you do a baseline test of your deuterium with cignature and if so what test did you do? Also, are they managing your DD in any way? And do cignature require you to be a ‘ patient’ of theirs before selling you Preventa?
>
> I am thinking of you this Christmas JanSz as I know it is the first since your wife has passed. I hope you can draw on all the memories of all the Christmases you shared. Thank you for taking the time to share your knowledge and experience here. Be blessed.

Merry Christmas @[Susie Cahill](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20565/) and @[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/)
and to everybody else who reads this post.
Yes tough Christmas for me but my kids are helping me great deal.
Best wishes to everybody.

When I first learned about CingnatureHealth (from @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) ) I ordered their Cholesterol test.
In return I got from them numerous helpful e-mails with explanations.
Eventually I have send out for testing my breath condensate and urine.
The process takes about 4 weeks, so I do not have results yet.
But that is going to be my baseline.
The initial goal is to get to 130ppm on breath condensate (I think for everybody), or lower depending on ones health condition.
The thought is that increased D promotes growth (good for growing bodies), (not good for those who completed their growth).
I have seen written a 30yo as a breaking point between growth and no growth.

At about the same time I have send my coconut water for D testing.
While doing it I found that the coconut water is in their food category.
That may be significant in the future,
since the deuterium management project consist of three parts.
drinking DDW water
breathing in a way that helps body to manage D
diet using foods low in D

I am assuming that CignatureHealth would not want call me their patient.
Customer would be better description.
They always underline that person seeking medical help should ask doctor for advice and do not change what doctor ordered.
They are working hard to not to cross Big Pharma.
Practically, the way I see it, CignatureHealth works on implementation and further research of dr. Laszlo Boros ideas.

I was not pushed into buying anything, either tests or DDW.
At first I thought that I will wait for test results, but couple days latter ordered 4 months worth of Preventa 25.
Each month worth of water is in one box.
Box have 12 bottles, each 1.5liters.
Due to shipping costs, the 25ppm version is used, weights least.
For drinking I was advised to use 105ppm.
To get 105ppm I have to mix one part of 25 with two parts of any other water.
Any other water, usually tap water, is assumed to have 150ppm D.
(25+2\*150)/3=108.3 close enough
Latter with help of @[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) (and a helpful kick from @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)) I found that (very likely) RO water have 140ppm or there about. (Still waiting for someone to test it and publish, maybe you can do it?)
(25+2\*140)/3=101.67ppm D
That made me drop the Poland Spring water that I was drinking and changing to the cheapest water available in Costco, which happens to be RO water (and saying it on the label).
Most cheap waters have RO in their process, but not all advertise that.

I was advised to use DDW as my water everywhere, drinking and cooking.

Note, when boiling DDW the steam is a light water, that is, long boiling/cooking increases D content.
That is not really significant but worth keeping in mind.
One step in rectification column (under atmospheric pressure) changes D content by about 4%.
8% under vacuum.

Long way to say that CignatureHealth have not pushed me this way or the other.

\========================================

@[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/)

\[qu ote="taiyang, post: 231088, member: 20771"\]thanks for answering my questions, jan. i see you have been a member since 2012 and since i have been coming here since the fall of this year, i see that you are helpful to everyone. member participation is key as jack is a busy man. i have a few questions if you have time

\* jan, have you read posts by members who had cancer and had good outcomes with jack's protocols? if so, can you tell me their names so I can read their journals?

I do not remember such cases.
I have a dear friend in Poland.
Hi have metastatic prostate cancer.
12 years ago his prostate was removed.
Now cancer got to his sacral bone.
I would love if he tried DDW, I am trying to convince him to do it.
He would be a great candidate. If he would use DDW, then monitoring his breath condensate ppm of D would be just to see the D part. More important would be to see how his PSA reacts. That is where the action is.

\*also, i didn't see CT on your list. do you use it?
I am relatively light on CT.
I take daily cold showers, walk bare foot over snow, have a light clothes.

\*i am assuming you optimized fatty acid profile by eating more seafood?
Number #1, for many years I am testing my fatty acids using
22 ------- Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
doing that I found that I have a lots of DHA and even more EPA
note that seafood comes with tons, and tons, and tons, and tons,
of EPA.
That super excessive amounts of EPA kill AA that we need.
Another words,
discussing fatty acids without actual testing them is rather useless, nobody gets convinced.
It is even worst.
There are laboratories (likely own by fish oil companies), they have pre-aranged setup, one can newer be too high in DHA or EPA. Every test ends up with advice, eat more fish oil.
It is even worst than that.
On my last test, rather than using LabCorp I went with another laboratory.
Just in time when @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and Laszlo Boros
started talking about importance of
Tetracosanoic Acid, C24:0
by another name
that new lab, conveniently skipped that one.

\*how did you optimize micronutrients? jack talks a lot about dha being THE food story, but i always felt confused by this as the body needs certain substrates derived from nutrients to function, leading me to think DHA **plus** micronutrients to build substrate are the food story.
I know jack's writings are organized by topic, but i think it would be useful if the members could organize their successes by topic. search doesn't always help or it leads to posts that are only very slightly related to the topic i am searching.\[/quote\]

I use
Spectracell micronutrient analysis
it checks about 34 of them (except for lugols that should be drank to excess).

/////////////

breathing
I got their mask.

Just thinking out loud here:

Has anybody ever tried chewing their seafood for a real long time and let the nutrients absorb through our mucosa?

It makes sense to me. Not only do you get the benefit of getting DHA right into the blood, anything left over

will get absorbed better (if at all) in our guts since they are usually trashed anyway..

I've been getting great results lately and this is one thing (among many others) that I have been doing.

Thoughts anyone?

### Antonis — 2017-12-24T11:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-230995>

Hello everyone, I would like to introduce myself and tell my story.

My name is Antonis, I am 24 years old, 6ft tall and weigh 167 pounds. I am from Cyprus and currently live here in a seaside town called Paphos. The latitude here is 35th parallel north.
I found Jack Kruse about 6 months ago and ever since then I have been trying to follow his protocols the best I can.

Below is a picture of the sunrise today

### Antonis — 2017-12-24T11:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-230996>

Here’s a little bit about my past:
I’ve always been into outdoor activities and sports since I was a kid. I played a lot of video games when I was young. I suffered from severe acne when I was a teenager and took pills for it. The therapy took 6 months. From the age of 12 I started playing tennis and competed in tournaments in my country and in Europe. I stopped playing at 17 when I had to join the army and complete mandatory military service. This I did for 18 months and the routines and duties I had to fulfill were varied and at different hours of the day and night. Often I had to be up at 2am to be on night watch for example.
After leaving the army I got back into sports and stared hitting the gym doing HIIT training. Sometimes did calisthenics outside. I also lifted heavy for a while.
21-22 years old I was out in bars drinking alcohol most nights. I was still eating relatively healthy ie. whole foods, red meat, veggies but I wasn’t eating strict keto or paleo or anything. Sometimes I ate junk food like chocolate and cakes, maybe once a week if that.
I followed many nutrition and fitness blogs and I tried different diet protocols such as intermittent fasting (skipping breakfast), carb loading, eat stop eat etc. They all felt good at the time but now looking back I know I wasn’t feeling the best I could be.

In the past my sleep quality was ok but I never woke up feeling fully rested. My energy was ok but felt tired throughout the day. I felt a lot of stress about different matters in my life and that often made me feel foggy and unfocused.

### Antonis — 2017-12-24T11:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-230997>

So 6 months ago, although I didn’t know if I was leptin resistant or not, I started following the Leptin Rx because it felt like the right thing to do. I following it properly and tried to do as much as I personally could to be optimal. I bought some blue blocker glasses and started to dim the lights at night. After a while I also did CT therapy with cold showers and icy face dunks. After doing this for a few months I felt a difference in the way my body felt and looked.

Currently I live in a 2nd floor apartment right next to the sea. It’s quiet here, away from the hustle and bustle. The apartment backs onto some green protected land, which backs on the beach and I have 180 degrees view of the green land and sea. My balcony doors are always open so that I can constantly hear the sounds of the sea. During the day I sit on the balcony getting the sun - if the sun is out I can get it all day on the balcony. Cyprus has sun all year round. In the summer the sun is out all day long every day and in the winter it’s cloudier. Right now it’s a cloudy with some sunshine here and there.

Here is my view from the balcony before sunset

### Antonis — 2017-12-24T11:54:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-230998>

Now every morning I wake up just before sunrise and prepare my breakfast, which is normally protein heavy. After eating, I head out to the beach and walk barefoot on the sand and in the water. I usually spend around an hour walking on the beach watching the sunrise. I started this a month ago and it feels really good. I do some more grounding during the day where I can.
I eat dinner around the time it gets dark and then after dinner I usually watch a movie with my blue blocker glasses on. Sometimes I read a book. There’s nothing else to do here so I do these things to pass my time until bed.
Recently I bought some red lights that I use in my bedroom at night.

I noticed a few things that are different. My sleep is so much better. I wake up feeling rested and not tired. I also fall asleep quicker. My energy levels are good too. I don’t have sluggish feelings and feel a bit more focused. I feel less stressed about things.

### Antonis — 2017-12-24T11:55:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-230999>

I recently just started including seafood in my diet, which I never did before. I wasn’t a big fish or seafood fan. Currently I’m not eating it that often but when I do I try to eat oysters and fresh salmon. I’m eating nuts like walnuts on occasion. Meals are always freshly cooked, never processed and minimal carbs. I’ll eat sweet potato now and again and seasonal veggies occasionally but mostly I eat fats and protein.

Yesterday's dinner. I tried to make sashimi. Well, you can say am a chef but I tried

> Set near the village of Kakopetria, Saint Nicholas water comes from a rushing fresh spring at the base of the snowcapped mountains of Troodos.
> The water passes through a thousand feet of volcanic soil to be enriched with natural minerals. Saint Nicholas water source was found to be at least 370 years old according to a research carried out by the National Center of Science Research "DEMOCRITOS" (Athens, Greece).[http://keogroup.com/en/portfolio/water/13](http://keogroup.com/en/portfolio/water/13)

Looks like fine water, and it's bottled close to you.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-17T14:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-241403>

This post here might help you too: Getting tired from the sun is a reaction to what the sun can do to a trashed hormone panel of a lifetime of mismatches cause by calcium efflux. What are the human photosynthetic chemicals that sunlight makes? Vit D3, histamine, and sulfhydryl groups of cholesterol in the skin I mentioned in Tensegrity 7 blog post on my website.

What does it destroy naturally? UV light lowers adrenalin the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system. When it drops in someone who is chronically stressed sleep can be induced. This is a sign of how bad your environment really is. UV and IR AM light also lowers aberrant steroids (CT 7) and nucleic acids without any liberation of heat on the skin's surface. This helps to prevent aberrant optical scattering of cell signaling below (nonlinear optics). In this way, it serves as a supreme example of how the surface photochemistry controls the biochemistry of your cells below in fat cells and in your organs. Biochemistry is not deterministic as most believe (think about the weather today while you lived, does it affect you? Its a surface effect too on your biology!).......it is determined by what happens to the surfaces in our eye, skin, and gut. This is why circadian biology is incredibly important. Natural harmony requires diurnal and seasonal destruction and renewal. Without renewal, we cannot sustain. We have to come apart so we don't fall apart.

Nature presents us with a series of great opportunities brilliantly disguised as impossible situations. We are forced to take our atoms and collide them against the light we face in our daily lives. If that light is man made you get a facade of a life.

I told you all long ago the KT event was key to understanding Jack Kruse's perspective on things. This answer has that shot of truth in it........WHY?

**This is why I get so pissed with providers who deal with genetic/SNP's data from 23andme**.......they think, and make those with SNP's believe, they can change the inside of their genome with supplements.....and they can't. This is akin to the story of the KT event and why geologist did not believe the asteroid impact story for so long. When Alvarez postulated the sequence of events that occurred 65 million years ago do you know why geologist thought he was crazy? Because prior to 1990, no one had ever thought that an asteroid from space (outside Earth's realm) could change Earth fundamentally. They just could not see how an outside force from space could change the energy flows to reverse photosynthesis in a short period of time. That is what happened. ........they just thought climate weathering was the key to surface erosion. Modern humans are making the same error in judgment. None of them can fathom that blue light or non native-EMF and a lack of sunlight can change their cells biochemistry deep in their heads and organs. IT CAN AND IT DOES HAPPEN A LOT.

Alvarez's ideas made scientists realize they had to look further outside for their answers about extinctions. Anything that lowers the quantum yield of the sun can affect life below. That was his take-home idea. That is the same idea with a bad SNP profile. I teach people to think the same way as Alvarez. I am telling people that nuclear DNA genes and SNP's are not tied to the endogenous breakdown as is commonly believed. A change in incident light EMF changes the epigenetic stimulus and this is what changes the nuclear genome and SNP's. SNP's are like a lens in your skin and eyes that change the sunlight to marry to your mitochondrial barcode called a haplotype. That allows you to work with hydrogen in your blood plasma in a more efficient fashion. It is tied to the environment you allow and via ubiquitination rate,s they become expressed.........that is when they are a problem. Genomic/SNP's therapies requires an environmental clean up first and foremost and not a pill or surgery. The Rx should not be prescribing to do things the other way around trying to solve the SNP presence. It is very similar to the KT event perspective of Alvarez. Do you see it yet, because most in functional medicine whiff on this topic? It is an environmental epigenetic issue tied to an altered environment increasing ubiquitin rates. SNP's just slow down epigenetic changes and this is why in environments with higher exogenous energy flows they get sicker faster than other people.

Consider the skin of the Earth and how it works with the sun and how it affects the Earth below. A sphere within a sphere can create electrical tension. Schumann taught us this in 1951, and yet we still don't realize he was teaching us about how energy can or can't flow from outside in or inside out. This is a quantum thermodynamic problem and it relates to energy and information flow. SNP's allow more or less information to flow from the sun to your mitochondrial by way of your blood plasma. Going to another place on Earth that fits your optical window will show you how this is built into your skin. The Earth is a sphere surrounded by the ionosphere. That tension becomes a pulse wave for thing that live within both spheres. When the tension changes in the environment the pattern of signaling changes between things in a cell. Blue light and nnEMF change the tension in that sphere to increase ubiquitination rates on all things that live upon its tectonic plates. This change directly affects the negative feedback loop of PER2 in the circadian mechanism I mentioned in the ubiquitin series.

Awareness of your SNP's, alone doesn't enlist change. You all have known this........you have to understand why you must change your environment. This is when you begin to understand how you are built and why you were built this way. Then wellness will visit you.

Learn about the sun........and your skin..........you have a lot yet to learn about the photoelectric effect and biology. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=141&v=1zBBmh8xQhM&app=desktop](https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=141&v=1zBBmh8xQhM&app=desktop)

### Antonis — 2019-01-19T15:58:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-260802>

Last night I slept in the jungle with camp fire. It was really amazing. No nnemf there. Swam in the river while the sun was shining to me. You could survive in the jungle. The only thing I was concerned about was the sunrise. I couldn't watch it in there with all trees and hills around although I was awake since sunrise. I was looking at that direction tho and I was in the sun the rest of the AM light. Can't these people who live in the jungle be optimal because thy can't watch the actual sunrise? Am really wondering.

### Antonis — 2019-01-22T07:41:50+0200

Its low populated area and there are not many tourists as it is rain season. You can balance the nnemf with the strong sun and swiming in the lake. Lots of lakefood here. Next I want to find out if the lake water is ddw lol.

### Antonis — 2019-02-02T07:39:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-262108>

I'm currently in Bandung. Not the best place in Indonesia but it is much better than Jakarta. Came here for couple of nights because it was a connection city. Tomorrow am heading to Pangandaran. It's a small area known for the quality of seafood and surfing. I am going to chill around the beach get as much sun as possible to knock out the bad effect that I had in the cities.
My sleep didn't improve yet. Am shaking a little bit like I have a circadian mismatch. This was happening when I was home too.

### Antonis — 2019-08-09T13:34:16+0300

### Antonis — 2019-08-10T10:15:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-275785>

My routine has changed since last year as

\-Am lifeguard and I spend 12 hours at the beach. Am in the sun 8-10 hours starting from sunrise.
\-Mostly Epi paleo diet
\-Swimming in the ocean and working out in the ocean
\- Blocking blue light after sunset(still using my phone sometimes with filters, airplane mode and blue blockers)
\-No WiFi after sunset
\-Waking up with no alarm
\-Grounding every day at the beach
\-Recently started CBD oil

However I still don't feel very optimal. I don't sleep well. I wake up during the night and I sleep talk. I feel I have some sort of stress. When I wake up in the morning I feel fatigued. I even measured my room with my cornet and it shows low readings. Bad sleep has been happening for the last 5 years. I have the sleep talk issue since kid. My dad sleep talks too.

Possible causes(according to me)
\-Coffee
\-Technology during AM
\-Too much thinking
\-some environmental stressor or emotional stress for some unknown reason.

I am quitting on coffee now and am tech to see how it goes.

### Antonis — 2019-08-10T10:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antonis-journal.20609/post-275786>

Feel free to comment and share your opinion, thanks

# Thread: much improved sleep; no headaches; much reduced hot flashes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2017-12-24T13:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231006>

i am sharing my story with the diabetes and apoe4 online communities.

i am wondering if high cortisol could be reflected in higher morning blood sugar readings. my morning fasted blood sugar readings have gone down with mitochondriac hacks, and i am wondering if this phenomenon is related to lower cortisol.
For me, paying attention to first- blacking out my sleeping environment, second- wearing blue blockers in the evening/avoiding screens in the evening except for the TV from a distance, third- taking off my glasses when out in the sun, and fourth- turning off the circuit breaker to my bedroom (i see a reading of 79 in the morning for the first time ever) has tracked with/been correlated with lower morning fasting blood sugar. i don't monitor my blood sugar throughout the day since i'm a little lazy and i am just trying to prevent going back to being pre-diabetic.

here is some data:
in november 2014
age- 49 and suffering from hot flashes at night, poor sleep headaches
average morning fasted blood sugar - \*\*\*106\*\*\* from 22 mornings (forgot to take my bg some mornings)
highest value 119
lowest value 94
weight- around 133 lbs.
in november 2015
age- 50 still suffering from poor sleep/headaches due to hot flashes
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*93\*\*\*from 28 readings
highest value 103
lowest value 82
weight- around 135-139 lbs.
in november 2016 (first summer i spent walking in the city for hours, but with glasses on and a visor. no sunblock)
age 51- still suffering from hot flashes. started playing around with intermittent use of 5 HTP. sometimes it helped with sleep, but not if i had bad hot flashes. then only ice on my head and aleve worked for the head aches and hot flashes. i took it rarely, though.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*102\*\*\* from 29 readings (at this point i sometimes would take two readings consecutively and saw my meter could read 86/91 0r 84/90) I was also getting more and more desperate because of poor sleep and was also rating my hot flashes as well as my blood sugar. i saw that i might have a morning blood sugar reading of anywhere from 84 to 107 and no hot flashes the previous night or extreme hot flashes and a morning blood sugar of 96 to 106. so my hot flashes did not seem to track with my blood sugar.
highest value 109
lowest value 86
weight around 135-138 (I stopped caring about my weight and hardly have any notes about it. I was really focused on my sleep since my memory was going).
in november 2017 (second summer i spend walking around the city for hours. still have glasses on and a visor. still no sun block)
age 52- i started completely blacking out my bedroom, but forgot when that began. the most important changes around october 19 2017 are the uvex glasses and a hat and no screens except for tv at a distance after sunset. I brush my teeth in dim light or in the dark. i start to not wear glasses outside and crack my windows when driving. no supplements at all.
average morning fasted blood sugar- \*\*\*88\*\*\* from 19 readings
highest value 96
lowest value 81
weight around 137-140 lbs (still not noting it a lot since sleep and cognitive function are my priorities. also fewer notes on calendar because i started sleeping better so not as desperate)
\*\*\*i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom on Dec. 7. I had a hot flash that night, but now is the first time I'm seeing a blood sugar in the 70's. from Dec. 7 to Dec. 24 my average blood sugar is \*\*\*82\*\*\* from 20 readings (some days I forget to measure, some days I take two readings consecutively and get results like 78/80 or 77/87).
highest value 90
lowest value 77
even with the inaccuracies of glucose meters, i can see a clear trend of better blood sugar. i have been using the walmart relion meter for all my readings throughout the years.
i am going fuller into perimenopause at 52 as i get my period on dec. 5 at day 60 of my cycle. i am starting to eat more carbs because i feel protected because of the drop in my morning fasted readings. this is probably a mistake, but i am still getting lower fasted readings even with eating more carbs. my weight hasn't gone up and is still around 138 lbs.

### taiyang — 2017-12-29T20:15:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231244>

the apoe4 community is very interested in my results, but no one in the diabetes online community seems interested. i just told them to did what i did for one lousy night- just turn off the circuit breakers to their bedroom to see if their was any difference in fasted morning glucose- but so far no one has taken me up on my idea. it might've worked for someone- maybe not everyone.
oh well...

### Inger — 2017-12-29T20:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231247>

Cool stuff Taiyang! I sure know I feel a difference when I turn off my circuit breaker - it is just more relaxing and feels better, sleep is better, everything is better

Hard to believe how deep people sleep.... even if you rub it right under their nose and there are clear results, they just are too tied to their old believes...they cant break it
The daughter of my ex has diabetes 1 and as me and him tried to cut carbs and measured bloodsugar and saw how big difference just witrh thsi one thing we got, he suggested her to drop carbs and she did.. and she got awesome results, could drop insulin use huge!.. But soon enough she was back to her old habits.. she could do it just a few months and then she started eating bread and sugars again.
You have to be real brave and curious to totally break with old habits and jump into the unknown, and cange your lifestyle for sure! Maybe you have to be wild  to a certain point that our civilisation/culture today do not support....

### taiyang — 2017-12-30T14:51:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231288>

another morning of excellent fasted blood sugar even when eating more carbs than usual- 77. if this is placebo effect, it sure is lasting a long time. with lifestyle changes of varying degrees, here is what i have noticed:
1\. better sleep. even if i wake up for a period of time, i can fall back asleep, which i couldn't do before.
2\. no headaches. i would often have headaches during the day and even at night if i had especially bad hot flashes.
3\. fewer to no hot flashes. i may now be able to do heavier exercise- like weight lifting. before doing heavy exercise would lead to awful hot flashes/headaches at night. yesterday i did some weight lifting and had a slight warming/insomnia. i was able to fall back asleep.
4\. the 'volume' on my attention has been turned up. if you can imagine needing the sound of the tv turned up- if the sound is a little too soft, you can catch things, but you miss a lot even though you concentrate harder. yesterday i noticed that i was able to 'multi-task' in perception in a way that i haven't been able to in recent memory. for example, my daughter is crazy about the tv show grey's anatomy, and she wants me to keep her company. that means that i have watched some shows multiple times via netflix. yesterday i noticed that even though i had seen a show a while a go, i was able to pick up on a lot of nuances in the actors' expressions that i hadn't seen previously. i noticed more of everything, like the fact that christina's pager lit up with a bright light. before i wouldn't have had the mental energy to focus on incidentals like that. and it wasn't just the fact that i had seen this particular show before either. i have watched a lot of reruns and never had the experience of being able to focus on multiple points like i did yesterday. it reminds me of the experience i had of watching a star trek episode while i was in college. i used to watch star trek with my family when i was little, but had not seen any shows for years and years. then when i watched that one star trek episode in college, i was able to catch all the nuances/sub texts/ in between the lines kind of stuff conveyed by the actors' facial expressions that i wasn't able to catch when i was young and had less insight into facial cues. so anyway, i hope my brain keeps healing.

### drezy — 2017-12-30T23:16:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/much-improved-sleep-no-headaches-much-reduced-hot-flashes.20610/post-231321>

That's an amazing way to go into the new year. Congratulations.

Keep going. Having only spoken up here a month ago, you stand great odds that your brain will not only "keep healing" but improve beyond what you were used to having access to up there. That's been my experience at least.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Flicker-free computer monitors?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-12-24T14:43:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231012>

Hi, does a flicker free computer monitor actually exist? I understand it is advertised as existent but is the flicker-free that is being advertised, equal to what, I suppose, jack kruse would consider absolut-non-existing flickering?

Is jack kruse referring to the flickering caused by the PVM (pulse width modulation) _PWM is used to regulate the brightness of LCD and LED displays._ Or is he referring to the "flickering" caused by the refreshal of the monitor's display?

If, the latter, then flicker-elimination should not exist?

### drezy — 2017-12-24T17:00:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231014>

Nice. You dig deep. It's part of getting to know the risks you are willing to accept.

Yeah I don't think your SCN or topological receptors bother making the academic distinction between ppm and refresh rate/pixel changes.

For your reference Brent Next's solution might be the best comercially available:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/page-3#post-225708](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/page-3#post-225708)

I keep putting in cheap low bids for used kindle units but nobody wants to sell them to me for cheap to make a "monitor" setup like below:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/#post-222100](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/outdoor-office-in-the-sun.19840/#post-222100)

Even that would have a flicker-like effect for every pixel that changed as I typed but that's good enough for my risk tolerance & blue light requirements. The refresh rate won't be high but that's sorta the point...

If any of you young dudes beat me to it here are some instructions (google translate will be your friend):
[https://github.com/nahtethan/dxg-display/blob/master/DXG.md](https://github.com/nahtethan/dxg-display/blob/master/DXG.md)

Hacking, rooting, and jailbreaking are all a part of the mitochondriac toolkit.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-12-24T18:47:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231019>

I appreciate the information, but the monitor u are referencing is simply too low performance... I need at least 100Hz refresh rate and 1080p...

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=231014)
>
> Yeah I don't think your SCN or topological receptors bother making the academic distinction between ppm and refresh rate/pixel changes.

I really hope the above isnt true...  That is really the QUESTion of this post to figure out. Lord have mercy.. It does seem logical, but I am still not sold that refresh rate = flicker effect???

### drezy — 2017-12-24T19:38:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-231024>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=231019)
>
> I appreciate the information, but the monitor u are referencing is simply too low performance... I need at least 100Hz refresh rate and 1080p...

We're bound in opposite directions on that one lol.

I'll split the difference on perspective... The portions of the screen that are changing (color/brightness) quite a bit are flicker-like:

If you mainly just watch documentaries on turtles and pygmy three-toed sloths I think you'll be safer

### Martijn — 2018-10-30T01:26:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-254525>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=231012)
>
> Hi, does a flicker free computer monitor actually exist? I understand it is advertised as existent but is the flicker-free that is being advertised, equal to what, I suppose, jack kruse would consider absolut-non-existing flickering?
>
> Is jack kruse referring to the flickering caused by the PVM (pulse width modulation) _PWM is used to regulate the brightness of LCD and LED displays._ Or is he referring to the "flickering" caused by the refreshal of the monitor's display?
>
> If, the latter, then flicker-elimination should not exist?

Which monitor did you end up getting? Believe it or not, my current Acer monitor has blue led lighting within the monitor. I tried Iris and Flux at 900k to remove both blue and green completely, and it still has a blue background. There is no setting to turn it off. I am looking for a new monitor that is at least 120 or 144 Hz to replace this one as soon as possible.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2018-10-30T06:59:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-254541>

I have a filter on top of the screen from lowbluelights.com it filters up to 500nm. I have 240hz screen, im thinking higher is better but Im not sure though if its true.

### Sun Disciple — 2018-10-31T00:20:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-free-computer-monitors.20611/post-254600>

>
> I have a filter on top of the screen from lowbluelights.com it filters up to 500nm. I have 240hz screen, im thinking higher is better but Im not sure though if its true.

I have this too but I also run IRIS full version and run my monitor brightness on max and then bring brightness down through IRIS to help cut the flicker effect. It doesnt work on all monitors though im told. To test get an old cell phone camera and record video of screen to reveal flicker.

# Thread: I was attracted by the light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-attracted-by-the-light.20615/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia Chadwick — 2017-12-25T03:39:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-attracted-by-the-light.20615/post-231041>

Hello,
This is my first post. I happened upon Dr. Kruse as I was listening to ketogenic diet videos on YouTube. When I heard him speak of the importance of sunlight I knew I had happened upon someone who could explain the science of what I had intuitively known all along.
I have always been very particular about light, attracted to sitting in morning sunshine, & loathe fluorescent light. When we were selling my elderly mother's house I took every old fashioned incandescent light bulb out of the house and transferred them to the fixtures in my house.
I often dream of living in a house with floor to ceiling windows to allow in as much natural light as possible. When I try to focus to get tasks done, I have known for years that if I don't get the tedious stuff finished before 2 pm, it just won't get done that day.
I am a morning person and wake up cheerful most days. Being outside in the sunshine with birds & flowers is about the best thing there is.
I live at 41 degrees latitude.
Looking forward to learning more.

### Mariangela — 2017-12-26T23:46:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-and-introducing-self.20617/post-231117>

> [Susie Cahill said:](/goto/post?id=231080)
>
> Welcome Mariangela,
> Gosh, you’ve been through the ringer. Thrilled for you you’ve had such rapid improvement. Can you say what the game changer may have been? Or did you do all the protocols together? Are you doing CT?
> Cheers.
> .

After I stalled at 85% better and not budging, I listened to one of JKs podcasts. In it he said that when he is doing cranial surgery he uses Ice to save the brain. That was my AHA moment. I said give me a bucket of ice water now! My encephalitis was coming back. I had been doing cold therapy 2 years following Wim Hoffman but only did it out doors in winter here in Pennsylvania. So the next day I read a lot of Jacks blogs and watched more podcasts. The next day I had a plan. I went out and got the sunrise in my bare feet with no contacts in. I had the strangest energy exchange feeling happen between me and the earth. I did about 3 minutes. After that I had more energy than I had for many yeas. All my coordination was back. It was incredible. I unplugged the wifi next that day and went back out for 11Am sun and all four limbs on the ground. I did 15 minutes. After that i knew I was healed. I could just feel it! And I knew my gut was healed and I could now eat anything . After that I went to the grocery and bought food - real food. And Ate. I did not get sick. The following day I started CT with a cold tub soak. I went right to the full immersion and stuck my head in. what a relief! It was like putting a fire out in my brain. That was it. I continue with Ct and do Epi-Paleo and all the other protocols I mentioned. For me it was the combination of doing them all as I described. Hope that gives some insight.

# Thread: Violettglas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/violettglas.20618/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Leger — 2017-12-27T17:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/violettglas.20618/post-231137>

I've been experimenting with water for quite a bit of time. A friend recommended I look into Violettglas. I've been playing with it for about a year and a half. From the miron-glas.com website:

> Miron Violet glass blocks the complete spectrum of visible light with the exception of the violet part.
> At the same time it allows a certain part to be permeable for radiation in the spectral range of UV-A, and infra red light. This unique combination offers optimal protection against the ageing processes that are released by visible light, thus lengthening durability and potency of products.

I purchased some of their water bottles. I place the bottles in a light box I built and leave in the sunlight for some number of hours. I then put the bottles in the fridge and chill to ~40f. The water I'm currently using is filtered (Woder 2 stage) tap water. I then pour the water through a Natural Action portable water 'structurer' - [https://naturalactiontechnologies.com/project/portable/](https://naturalactiontechnologies.com/project/portable/). This is my optimal water. It is silkier, denser and has a certain freshness. It's been awhile since I've had fresh glacier water, but this is the closest I've gotten to that water.

### WalterNL — 2017-12-27T18:30:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/violettglas.20618/post-231139>

Cool find, I'm going to get a few of those bottles.

# Thread: Lets have fun- science jokes and other jokes too.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-12-28T00:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231153>

I love this one:

Two atoms are walking along. One of them says:
“Oh, no, I think I lost an electron.”
“Are you sure?”
“Yes, I’m positive.”

And this one:

Never trust an atom… they make up everything!

Now some of them I didn't get where I found those jokes. .lol

### Billybats — 2017-12-28T00:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231154>

Okay a couple more:

A photon checks into a hotel. When asked if it needs a bellman, it responds “No, I’m traveling light”

A proton, a neutron, and helium walk into a bar and order three beers.
The bartender appears with 3 beers and asks the proton, “Are you sure you’re over 21?” The proton replies, “I’m positive.” So the bartender gives him the first beer.
He gives the second beer to the neutron and says, “For you, no charge.”
He throws the third beer in helium’s face. Helium doesn’t react.

### Billybats — 2017-12-28T01:00:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231155>

This is the last one for now. Lol.. my boyfriend and I always say "there's that word again" when we say the word heavy

### Lahelada — 2017-12-28T04:20:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231160>

A biochemist walks into a student bar and says to the barman: “I’d like a pint of adenosine triphosphate, please.” “Certainly,” says the barman, “that’ll be ATP.” ( Uk centric joke  )

Why are quantum physicists terrible in bed? Because when they find the position, they can’t find the momentum, and when they have the momentum, they can’t find the position.

Never trust an atom. They make up everything.

When I heard that oxygen and magnesium hooked up I was like OMg.

### Billybats — 2017-12-28T17:07:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-have-fun-science-jokes-and-other-jokes-too.20619/post-231169>

I decided to make my password "incorrect" because if I type it in wrong, my computer will remind me, "Your password is incorrect."
\---------------------------------------------------------

**A Man Walked into an EMF Consultant's Office ...**

**A man walked into an EMF Consultant's office and inquired about the rates for undertaking an EMF investigation and remediation.

"Well, typically we scope, structure and plan the assignment in advance, and charge $150 an hour (plus sales tax and expenses) for three questions", replied the consultant.

"Isn't that ridiculously expensive?" asked the man.

"Yes," the EMF Consultant replied, "and what was your third question?"

**
\--------------------------------------------------------
**One day an EMF Consultant was walking in the woods ...

An EMF Consultant was walking through the forest one day when a frog called out to him from the side of the path...

It said "If you kiss me, I'll turn into an enchanting, beautiful prince/princess". She/He bent over, picked up the frog, put it in his pocket and continued with his walk.

The frog chimed up again and said, "If you kiss me and turn me back into an enchanting and beautiful princess, I will stay with you for one week." The guy stopped, took the frog out of his pocket, smiled fondly at it, returned it to his pocket and resumed his walk.

The frog then cried out, "If you kiss me and turn me back into an enchanting, beautiful princess, I will stay with you forever ." Again the guy took the frog out, smiled at it and put it back into his pocket.

Finally, the frog asks in exasperation, _"What is wrong with you?_ I've told you I'm an enchanting, beautiful princess; that I'll stay with you and be your girlfriend forever. _Why won't you kiss me?_"

The guy said, "Look, I'm an EMF Consultant. I am so busy keeping my clients safe in these times of rampant electrosmog I don't have time for a girlfriend, but a talking frog – How _Cool_ is THAT!! "

**
\------------------------------------------------------

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-new-home.20622/post-231178>

Hello everyone! I've been a member in this site for almost two years. I bought land last year and we are finally starting to meet with our architect. I am looking for suggestions on how to make the healthiest home. We are planning to run the LAN lines into every room so there's never a need for wireless, kill switches for the bedroom, sky lights and all glass doors opening to our pool and large covered patio. Everyone I start to talk to regarding this lifestyle is clueless. Any suggestions are so welcomed and even vendors if possible. We live in New Orleans.
Thanks in advance! Wish I was in Mexico! I'll have to wait till Mardi Gras...
Happy New Year!

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-11-12T21:56:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-new-home.20622/post-255519>

Looks like it will be a lovely home.

You are lucky to be able to build according to your own specs.

You may have some VOC outgassing from all that particleboard, so take precautions.

I hope you over-insulate your home to make it comfortable and energy efficient for challenging years ahead as our climate now transitions into a particularly bitter Grand Solar Minimum era. An efficient wood stove or non-electrical pellet stove would be a good prepper choice for when grid goes down from excessive cold, snow/ice storms, etc or whatever. With super insulation it wouldn't take much heating to keep it habitable in worst case winter scenario.

I hope you spec'd out electical system (wiring in particular) to cancel out harmful electromagnetic fields in the house.

A special touch would be a few solar (sun) tubes on roof to get as much daylight into frequently used interior nooks and crannies.

Best of luck. Be creative for a healthy and happy home.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-11-13T19:22:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/building-a-new-home.20622/post-255566>

Congrats on having a savvy DH who can partner with you on creating a sustainable and environmentally sound house.

Check out the IBN website for guidelines on safe and sustainable building at [https://buildingbiology.com/principles-of-baubiologie/](https://buildingbiology.com/principles-of-baubiologie/)

Some excerpts from the site:

**HEALTHY INDOOR AIR**

-   Supply sufficient fresh air and reduce air pollutants and irritants
-   Avoid exposure to toxic molds, yeasts, and bacteria as well as dust and allergens
-   Use materials with a pleasant or neutral smell
-   Minimize exposure to electromagnetic fields and wireless radiation
-   Use natural, nontoxic materials with the least amount of radioactivity

**THERMAL AND ACOUSTIC COMFORT**

-   Strive for a well-balanced ratio between thermal insulation and heat retention as well as indoor surface and air temperatures
-   Use humidity-buffering materials
-   Keep the moisture content of new construction as low as possible
-   Prefer radiant heat for heating
-   Optimize room acoustics and control noise, including infrasound

**HUMAN-BASED DESIGN**

-   Take harmonic proportion and form into consideration
-   Nurture the sensory perceptions of sight, hearing, smell, and touch
-   Maximize daylighting and choose flicker-free lighting sources and color schemes that closely match natural light
-   Base interior and furniture design on physiological and ergonomic findings
-   Promote regional building traditions and craftsmanship

**SUSTAINABLE ENVIRONMENTAL PERFORMANCE**

-   Minimize energy consumption and use renewable energy
-   Avoid causing environmental harm when building new or renovating
-   Conserve natural resources and protect plants and animals
-   Choose materials and life cycles with the best environmental performance, favoring regional building materials
-   Provide the best possible quality of drinking water

# Thread: My Optimal Journal- Stacia Whitaker
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-stacia-whitaker.20623/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stacia426 — 2017-12-28T20:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-stacia-whitaker.20623/post-231189>

I am so excited to learn from Dr. Kruse and the members on this site! I want to provide some information about me and my past medical history. I am currently 33 with two children living in central Indiana. I live in a rural town and commute to Indianapolis for work. I work in a Neonatal ICU as a Neonatal NP.

PMH:
Infancy and early childhood: FTT, multiple food intolerances/allergies, multiple hospital admissions

Early childhood and teenage years: multiple broken bones (eight), continued infections with frequent antibiotic use, hospitalization for mono, removal of tonsils due to continued infections, go cart accident with TBI and LOC

Twenties: severe environmental allergies requiring five allergy medications, infertility with multiple ovarian cysts with one cyst being pre cancerous, IBS, continued sinus infections taking antibiotics at least monthly, sinus surgery to remove polyps, muscle weakness, chronic fatigue, joint pain/inflammation, cystic acne, POCS, car accident again with TBI and LOC. I have always been at a healthy weight.

I did become pregnant at 28 in 2013 and had my first child after taking metformin and starting a gluten free diet. I was sick my entire pregnancy with severe hyperemesis. My first child was born with torticollis. During his first years of life he was very sick with: FTT, feeding failure due to severe tongue tie, multiple food intolerances, multiple medication reactions, speech delay, SPD, autism, myopia, esotropia requiring glassess, multiple illnesses including an ICU admission for RSV. We stopped all vaccines after 6 months. He had made great improvements in the past two years with biomedical therapies.

I had my second child at 31 in 2016 without any fertility treatments. I again had hyperemesis. The hyperemesis was more severe this time requiring a zofran pump, three additional medications and home health nursing for home IV fluids. My second child has been healthier. I was able to take a more proactive approach with his diet and health. I was able to eliminate enough foods from my diet to be able to breast feed him until 16 mos. He is developing normally without evidence of autism. He has also had an ICU admission for RSV. He is completely unvaccinated.

Between my two children I became very ill with debilitating joint pain and chronic fatigue. Blood IgG testing reveled severe intolerances to many foods. This is when I started a GAPS/Paleo diet. I was able to heal my gut. I began to feel much better and had much more energy. I was finally able to lose the weight I gained during pregnancy. I was getting sick less often. My environmental allergies were improving after doing some biofeedback therapy.

Thirties: I feel that the improvements that I made in my health are fading. I feel that my hormones are a wreck. I need to lose about 25-30 pounds. I cannot seem to lose this weight despite eating the "perfect" paleo diet. I have felt for awhile now that changing my diet has helped but something else was missing.

My MGM had breast cancer at 40 and died of a sudden heart attack at 50. My mother has multile health challenges including: osteoporosis, weight gain, IBS, food intolerances, joint pain. My 23andme results are pending.

I first learned about Dr. Kruse from an interview that he did with Dr. Dan Pompa. For the last three months I have been listening to every You Tube Video with Dr. Kruse that I can find. I am excited to finally be able to put the missing information together. Any suggestions are greatly welcome! Thanks for reading my long post!

Stacia Whitaker

### Sheddie — 2017-12-29T02:41:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-stacia-whitaker.20623/post-231203>

Welcome Stacia. It might not be positively exciting to ponder and evaluate your NICU work environment for a steady 'assault' by nnEMFs coming at and through you while you're there... Have you run across Dr Kruse mentioning some ornery health observations relative to his time in hospital surgery rooms? This is just what came to me reading your intro, and feeling great compassion. You sound ready to work on health armed with the 'new paradigm' and as an insider to the current state of medical health care. Again, a very big welcome to you. What I have learned has been very helpful, and hopeful, in just about a half a year.

When you have RO system at home, it is usually good, good until time comes when it is not.
RO water is sold everywhere in stores, it is usually the least expensive water.
I assume,
that the companies that made that water, have better quality control then you or me would have at our homes.
That water is still good to use for shower, dishwater or laundry (I hope).

My wife, God bless her soul, had a seawater reef tank.
To replenish water there she would use home RO system.
I was trying once to drink that water, bad taste.
Well, to make that RO water livable for her corrals or anemones or sponges she was adding bucket loads of additives to recreate it to be similar to equatorial salt water.

### Inger — 2017-12-31T14:45:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happy-new-year-everyone.20625/post-231337>

I love these winter holidays, Christmas is so awesome because it is the time when dark turns into light again..slowly and steadily...spring will soon arrive   And New Year is so exiciting, because that is when one year has passed and I think about all the things I can improve, and what I have all learned in the past year. Wow.. I am excited like child, huh!
I have cleaned my home nicely, watered my flowers, having to leave for work soon at a huge new year party.. everything feels good and I am ready for 2018 to come with more amazing challenges and more to learn and beautiful things to experience

2017 truly was a crazy year for me! I learned a ton.. with all its ups and downs

I am so grateful for you all, being on this journey together is for sure something special
2018 will be a deuterium depleting year for me, I am so all in
It is rainy and stormy here..all gray.. but I feel high... whats up with this deuterium depletion magic...lol Dipped in my rainbarrel today morning as always, and I am so grateful for it! My deuterium depleting machine, all for free..   Drinking glacial water... diving into the darkness...and enjoying the light even more that now increases every day

Wishing you all a magic 2018 filled with love, health, happiness and and an always expanding awareness.. what a beautiful journey this is!

# Thread: Mariangela's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mariangelas-optimal-journal.20629/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mariangela — 2018-01-01T02:27:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mariangelas-optimal-journal.20629/post-231366>

5 years neuropsychiatric Lyme disease. Never saw a tick bite or had a rash just got sick on a 10 day retreat trip to New York.

It started with nausea, inability to tolerate any food and I started having brain pains. My integrative MD and I were thinking maybe Lyme disease. All tests came back negative for anything Lyme and I did all cutting edge ones too plus the ones he had for other things including breath tests urine and stool analysis too. All negative for anything. Had MRI of brain and neck and neurosurgeon said there was no physical reason for me to be acting like I was and I should see a psychiatrist. At the end of the first month I felt a flu coming on one day and collapsed in 12 hours with temps of 105 and hallucinating. I couldn't walk or stand up and had strange phobias about it all.

It took a month for the flu to clear . Once it was over, my gut was destroyed and I could eat nothing without getting violently sick. I had been a raw vegan for about 11 years but now I was phobic about even looking at vegetables and would even go into a panic attack. Its ok to laugh! I found out I could eat eggs and started on an all animal protein diet. Yeah, go ahead and chuckle again.

The next five years I stayed on an all animal protein diet and thrived. My symptoms became a brain, joint pain and fatigue nightmare though. We clinically diagnosed Lyme disease, bartonella, babesia and systemic candida. I had an enormous variety of symptoms that varied everyday: vibrations, seizures, severe brain pain, burning brain, swollen brain, eye flashes, blurry and double vision; lost my sight for a few seconds one day, slurred speech and inability to talk, loss of comprehension, hallucinations, suicidal ideation, rashes, swollen painful joints, sudden swelling in my body like hands thighs or arms; walking difficulty, swallow difficulty, inability to tolerate sound, hysteria, panic attacks, phobias, rage, memory loss, inability to follow conversation or comprehend words, inability to read or write; electrical shocks from the top of my head down to my feet; insomnia

For the 5 years I stayed with a variety of natural IVs including oxygen therapies. I got 85% better but stalled. And some of the brain swelling was coming back. But getting this far was a miracle for me ! And I was feeling good about it.

I heard of Jack Kruse a few years ago on a paleo blog but never looked him up. Then 2017 sometime in early summer, while cleaning my desk I found a paper with the name Jack Kruse on it. lol. Still didn't look him up but was tempted. Saved the paper too. Then in Aug of 2017 in a conversation with another patient at the alternative health clinic I go to, Jack's name came up and the person said you gotta look him up! I finally did. I spent a fascinating time looking over his web sight and getting very excited about all the intense information that took what I knew up to a whole new level. Plus I saw where I had missed the point of some things and was introduced to sooo many fascinating new concepts. I could barely stop reading blogs. I knew I had found my answer for my full recovery because of the excitement I felt. I wasn't going back to brain hell.

I took notes on what appealed to me or sounded like a match for my illness. So it went like this: day one was the talk with the patient. Day two was looking at the blogs and podcasts. Day 3 I started my plan and ordered the book the fourth phase of water. I started with Leptin Rx. So day 3 i went out and got the sunrise without contacts in with bare feet on the ground. I stayed out about 3 minutes. Came inside and had so much energy it was incredible! All my body movements were fast, on target, coordinated and sustained. in 30 minutes I ate a BAB - which was hard since I usually fasted until 11 or 1. Then I unplugged the wifi since EMF RX was on my list of to do items. Went back outside and got 11AM sun for 15 minutes with all four limbs on the ground. Came inside and I knew my gut was healed and so were the rest of my health issues. I went shopping and bought foods I had not eaten in over 5 years. I did not get sick when I ate them that same day! The rest is history. My Mom saw me on day 3 and said my face looked really different- like it used to look before I was sick. Day 4 I started CT. I went right to the full immersion since I had been doing outdoor Ct for two years. This put out the fire in my brain and spinal cord . NO more brain issues. No more illness!

I started Jack Kruse protocol on Aug 23 rd 2017. I am 4 months in to these protocols: Leptin RX, EMF Rx, CT, EPI Paleo RX, circadium rhythm

I just started Leptin reset protocol since I felt I had all the signs for it

Had Leptin tested a few weeks ago (did not do it at first though to know what is was) Its now 3.9 which I am sure is lower than when I started.

Had these tests too a few weeks ago
results:
HS-CRP: 0.4
Homocysteine: 8.3
Fibrinogen: 388

i do candles at sundown, use blue blocker glasses, sleep on a grounding sheet that is plugged into the ground outside, eat mostly shell fish every day mostly raw , keep my house heat at 68 degrees, went from doing CT water soak EVERY DAY for two months to twice a week now but its winter in Pennsylvania and I get cold outside in shorts and T shirt every day; did full body sun from Aug till mid nov; get as much winter light in my eyes as possible; eating seasonally and on no carbs for winter- easy for me after 5 years of it lol; all exercise is outside including yoga no matter what the weather; I drink living spring water from a local spring ; read Dr Kruses book

I feel like I am in my 20's (I am 57) and to date have had no illness symptoms return. I continue to do more EMF and other hacks as I learn about them.

My main concern is to keep the longevity of my brain working optimal. I do not want the organisms to come back and dont know how much actual brain damage I might permanently have if any. I wear two hearing aides and wonder how much harm they are doing to my brain too.

Onward.

### Mariangela — 2018-01-27T01:23:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mariangelas-optimal-journal.20629/post-233590>

Update on supplements :

Being a light worker as we call it - a ThetaHealer and Yoga Therapist- I have always relied on navigating my supplement doses with energy testing. I use the dosages my dr says to take as my starting point and go from there. Since doing the Leptin Reset, and now the Post Leptin Reset, along with the steps I have done for EMF mitigating and circadian rhythms and CT, I am off most supplements and hormones now. This is really weird to me but happily so. Never have my supplements energetically tested out at zero for such a long time, especially my hormones.

Changes: from taking on average 30 supplements multiple times a day, I am off most except hormones unless I get too much EMF and not enough natural light.

Some of the more interesting Changes:

thyroid started at 90mg-now at 3mg
Estrogen once or twice a week at 1/4 dose
Testosterone everyday at 1/8 dose
Progesterone everyday at half dose

Probiotics were mega doses now at none
DHA none
Vitamin D started at 5000 twice a day now only 1 -5000 tablet once a week
Adrenal support has been none for 3 months now

And like I said there are a bunch more I test zero for on a daily basis. First time ever for that.

Also, really like the bio hacking 101 webinar series.

### Mariangela — 2018-04-14T02:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mariangelas-optimal-journal.20629/post-239384>

I will be 8 months symptom free of all illness in a few weeks. I am off my natural IV therapies and 90% of the bazillion supplements I was taking. Plus I am off thyroid and doing great! I am not really interested in vegetables since I learned about deuterium and was eating meat for 5 years anyway because of no tolerance for vegetables whatsoever. But I decided to give seasonal ones a go just to test out my n=1 . I cooked them. I had no gut problems doing so! That is quite the miracle for me since vegetables would take me down for months of illness and that was just a tablespoon of cooked ones - raw was like eating poison. However since I had no reaction to cooked vegetables, I decided to give raw a go too. Yummy was the result.  All good. However I noticed a sluggishness come over me afterwards so I did a CT session. It took it away. I am eating mostly sea food and have added in sea vegetables. Sea veggies do not make me sluggish. I am getting to the point I am forgetting I was so nasty sick for 5 years.

I am wondering what Jack's take is on peptide therapies. I had been on sermorelin but stopped it a year ago because of cost. There is supposed to be a new version coming out and was curious what the pros and cons of it were. That might be a silly question because I am recovered, so why worry, right? But just wondering if there was any benefit in it that would serve me. I am still on bio hormones, but doses are 1/4 or less the amount I used to take. Going to do a hormone panel and thyroid in a month or so to see how all that is going, even though I feel really fine with it all as is.

### JanSz — 2018-04-14T14:59:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mariangelas-optimal-journal.20629/post-239393>

> [Mariangela said:](/goto/post?id=239384)
>
> I am wondering what Jack's take is on peptide therapies. I had been on sermorelin but stopped it a year ago because of cost. There is supposed to be a new version coming out and was curious what the pros and cons of it were.

You must be low on GH, Growth Hormone since you are talking about Sermorelin.
\-----------------
Sermorelin is a synthetic analog of naturally occurring Growth Hormone-**Releasing** Hormone (GHRH).
\-----------------
So **if you are able** to produce **your own** Growth Hormone,
Sermorelin (or any other of this type peptides) should help in releasing **your own** GH.

That is a great news for those about 25yo or maybe 30yo.
That is for those who usually do not need this assistance.
I have even communicated with one 40yo who felt better after peptides use (but have not tested if he actually got his GH raised.
////////
So the only way to actually raise GH is to use GH injections.
////////
GH injections are definitely more expensive than peptides.

///
Most commonly, to check for one's GH status
IGF-1 is tested in blood.

There is a common problem with a way the testing is usually done.
Usually automatically done test is used by laboratory, that test is highly misleading.
Most often, the technique used by that test is called chemoluminescence.

IGF-1 should imperatively be measured by IRA (radioimmunoassay) (manual technique).
