# Thread: Peripheral Neuropathy and loss of all sexual sensations overnight! Please Help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peripheral-neuropathy-and-loss-of-all-sexual-sensations-overnight-please-help.20654/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 49
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JessicaK — 2018-01-05T21:35:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peripheral-neuropathy-and-loss-of-all-sexual-sensations-overnight-please-help.20654/post-231717>

I don't know if this belongs in this forum subject, But I need help.

I am 34 and I had never been sick or seen a Dr in my life. No drugs, alcohol or cigarettes. No sex before marriage.

Very clean living.

I got Botox 3 months ago, which is the only change I’ve ever made and everything changed overnight.

I lost my appetite, lost weight, became depressed, anxious, scared, foggy, disconnected, lost weight, lost my boobs within a week, my full cheeks are now hollow. My feet and hands are numb. My arms, ears and face, feel like there’s icy hot all over it.

And the worst part for me is the inability to be aroused. Literally overnight, any pleasure sensations were paralyzed. My nipples, which were always extremely sensitive and would instantly turn me on. My clitoris is numb and my vagina won’t open. I went to the gynecologist when I first realized it, she said it was patent and suspected vaginsmus. But how does that happen overnight? She was able to get her finger and then speculum in but couldn't open it. She said it looked normal and healthy though.

And when my husband tried, he could not penetrate and it felt like knives! I purchased some dilators and am working with those. My poor husband thinks I stopped loving him! But even simple pleasures like when he plays with my hair or tickles my arm are now sensation less. No more goosebumps. Foot rubs are painful instead of heavenly.

The dilators seemed to be working and my husband was actually able to penetrate me after 2 months, even though it's still pleasureless and hurts so bad.
So I went to the gynecologist again so she could do a pap smear and rule out infection. Before anything she said it looked dry, irritated, and atrophied! This time it took her about 20 minutes to get the speculum in, last time it slipped right in. It hurt so bad I could have sworn it was a steak knife. Through tears and screams I told her not to stop.

I’m heartbroken! I would take all the other side effects if I could just be intimate with my husband again.

If I had to diagnose myself, I would say I have peripheral neurophathy.

I went from happy, pretty, healthy, and in love to being terrified!

There are many botox support communities that I have found, that all have my symptoms for worse. I’ve only encountered a few people who have healed. But it took years. There are others who are still suffering and it’s been 8 years! And they all have the same story. Perfectly healthy and then life turned upside down over night after botox. And they’ve all been to countless Dr’s who can’t find anything.

I’ve seen an OBGYN, Family practitioner of internal medicine, neurologist, immunologist, and of course phelobotomist and have had over 40 labs of all sorts, and 4 MRI’s (brain, spine, lumbar, thoracic), and nerve testing of my hands, arms, feet, legs, and brain, which all resulted in nothing.

I’ve got an upcoming appointment with an endocrinologist.

While I am happy that I don’t have an auto immune disease or tumor, I am so scared that I will never be the same.

I believe the botox paralyzed my pleasure center. I used to be so full of life. Nothing else has changed. It was literally overnight.

Have you seen this before?

I also found this:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2856357/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2856357/)
Botulinum toxin, one of the most poisonous biological substances known, is a neurotoxin produced by the bacterium _Clostridium botulinum_. _C. botulinum_ elaborates eight antigenically distinguishable exotoxins (A, B, C1, C2, D, E, F and G). All serotypes interfere with neural transmission by blocking the release of acetylcholine, the principal neurotransmitter at the neuromuscular junction, causing muscle paralysis. The weakness induced by injection with botulinum toxin A usually lasts about three months. Botulinum toxins now play a very significant role in the management of a wide variety of medical conditions, especially strabismus and focal dystonias, hemifacial spasm, and various spastic movement disorders, headaches, hypersalivation, hyperhidrosis, and some chronic conditions that respond only partially to medical treatment. The list of possible new indications is rapidly expanding. The cosmetological applications include correction of lines, creases and wrinkling all over the face, chin, neck, and chest to dermatological applications such as hyperhidrosis. Injections with botulinum toxin are generally well tolerated and side effects are few. A precise knowledge and understanding of the functional anatomy of the mimetic muscles is absolutely necessary to correctly use botulinum toxins in clinical practice.

and this:
**Central Nervous System Toxicity After Botulinum Neurotoxin Injection**
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3821148/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3821148/)

I have been thinking about this since yesterday . My heart goes out to you.

Was this your first Botox session? Who did the injection and where?

One of the things that I was reading is that you are taking activated charcoal. There is talk that this can inhibit iodine uptake. I wonder if this had anything to do with the second visit to the gyno being even more unpleasant. I would increment seafood intake and go for oysters too.

Another separate thought is that you are now living in perma-stress that IT might happen and you are training as if you are going for the olympics. That unfortunately does nothing for arousal, as arousal is in the domain of the parasympathetic part of the autonomous nervous system. My thinking is that when you feel ready to try again a glass of Argentine Malbec with a nice candlelit meal would be medicine. Don't treat yourself like a malfunctioning machine.

You have been advised a lot of good things. I would not try them all at once though.I agree with Da MO that posting labs would be good. Thyroid labs, BUN Creatinine ,HS CRP, vitamin D ,LDL /HDL and triglycerides would be especially interesting.

### JessicaK — 2018-01-07T00:35:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peripheral-neuropathy-and-loss-of-all-sexual-sensations-overnight-please-help.20654/post-231841>

Wow! You all have a great understanding of the body. I am trying so hard to read as much as I can. I spend most of the time looking up the definitions for every other word or abbreviations as of now.
Yes I read multiple case studies on Botox which is where I got the supplements list to take. The biggest frustration is that all the doctors don't believe it's the botox and think I'm crazy. I've uploaded it below.

To answer a few questions before I spend hours reading and understanding all of your comments:

I have never had any health concerns. I've never taken medication, antibiotics, or even tylenol.
My dad is a chiropractor so I was raised very holistically. Just a good multi vitamin.

The only pill I ever took was accutane. That was about 5 years ago. But I took it for 6 months in very small dosages and never had side effects while I was on it, besides my lips being a little dry. Nothing that aquafor didn't take care of immediately.

I had 500 units of botox in my forehead and around my eyes for lines.
It was my third time in 10 years. Never any issues before. Stupid I know. I just didn't think it could be dangerous if all these celebrities did it and I hadn't heard any bad side effects besides ptosis. I should have researched first!

The only thing different I did this time was take a zinc lozenge from Zycam for a few days afterward because I got a runny nose. I think that was a bad idea. I read somewhere that zinc binds with botox.

I found this article too-

This one talks about cleaving to SNAP-25:
[http://www.jneurosci.org/content/jneuro/28/14/3689.full.pdf](http://www.jneurosci.org/content/jneuro/28/14/3689.full.pdf)

@[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) WOW!!!! Those are a lot of suggestions and I can't wait to look into them all! And yes I saw those articles about Botox as well. I feel so stupid for putting something so dangerous into my body. Thanks for posting those links.
I posted the case study I found as well. I literally have every symptom that she notes. And that's why I started to take Alpha GPC and Citoline CDP Chline (hoping to produce for ACh.
Thank you for the suggested protocols. I need to read more about Mythelene Blue light. I saw an article that it can lead to damage:
[https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/methylene-blue-and-red-light-caution.10770/](https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/methylene-blue-and-red-light-caution.10770/)

Why is blue light ok for the vagina and not for eyes and skin?

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I am definitely going to look into the mitochondria protocols. I spent all day yesterday reading about DDW. I will check out the Cignarture Health website as well.

@[Da-mo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9712/) the neurologist did the blink rate test and it was normal. I will post all my labs and results to everything as soon as I can get to scanner or get a minute to type it all out. That would be so helpful for you geniuses to see what the Dr's missed or didn't test for and should have. Thank you!

@[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) that's interesting about the activated charcoal. Do you think I should stop taking it and see what happens, or just increase the iodine?
Also it was 500 units in my forehead and around my eyes for wrinkles. Preformed by my dermatologist. He has never had any patients report adverse affects, but he did report it to Allergan for me. Allergan called me and they said others have also reported peripheral neuropathy.

@[shiran](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20240/) and @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) I've lived in Las Vegas for 30 years. It's pretty sunny. I am constantly on my phone and computer for my work. So now I've learned I should get some blue light glasses. I will definitely spend mornings in the sun and grounding. I've been reading up on that as well. As far as nnemf go, because I do live in Las Vegas, the McCarran airport is in the center of town. Las Vegas is small so we always see planes flying overhead. I live about 6 miles from the airport. But Las Vegas has only about a 10 mile radius.

No changes besides the botox. Same job for 2 years, same house for 3 years. No changes with family or friends.
Oh my sleep isn't bad, but I did stress a lot for work for a few months when I had some crazy deadlines. I'm sure that doesn't help my nerves repairing. But for the last 2 months I've dropped my work load and been taking it a lot easier.

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) I will also look into becoming a member. Thank you for the suggestion!

I have to go back to work but tonight I will look into everyones suggestions even more and come up with a plan. I'll also post my old labs and let you know how it goes with the endocrinologist.

Thank you all for giving me hope.

@[JessicaK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20929/)

DDW -- Deuterium Depleted Water

It is a technical term that can be applied to all waters other than one, the Standard Water.
So it is meaningless when applied to any water unless amount of contained D, deuterium is specified at the same time.

When one searches for a most deuterium depleted water and at the same time worries about cost,
RO, Reverse Osmosis Water beats hands down all other waters, and at the same time it is usually the cheapest water available anywhere.
That includes all fancy waters, glacial, from cenote waters, icelandic waters, Voss, Pellegrine, Pland spring and any other naturally occurring waters which includes water from water wells on someones backyard.

There may be variations between this or that RO water, but those are variations within category.
Officially tested RO water by CignatureHealth is Aquafina made by Pepsi.
Aquafina have 139.5ppm, that corresponds to δ2H (VSMOW)=(-104.34)

//

### JanSz — 2018-01-08T17:27:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peripheral-neuropathy-and-loss-of-all-sexual-sensations-overnight-please-help.20654/post-231954>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=231951)
>
> Well they have independent testing done. You can contact the third parties and request to see their results. For Qlarivia it's a German laboratory, don't have the name at hand.
>
>
> Yes that checks out. We can be sure 55ppm is man made DDW.

How much RO water to add to 25ppm DDW to get 55ppm?

Assume RO is 140ppm
To get one unit of volume of 55ppm water we need to take
x volume of 25ppm
and
y volume of 140ppm
x+y=1
x=1-y
25x + 140y=55
y=30/115=0.26087
x=0.73913

Qlarivia comes in 1/2 L bottles

add 0.5\*30/115=0.130435 Liters RO to one bottle of Qlarivia
or mix
**4 bottles of Qlarivia with one 1/2 L RO water (=2.5 Liters) (about day's supply in my case)**
that will give
(2\*25+0.5\*140)/2.5=**48.000 ppm mix**

Hurricane Michael sped from the Caribbean Sea into the Gulf of Mexico on Monday, October 8, passing western Cuba and the eastern Yucatan on its way.**Michael passed much closer to Cuba** than the Mexican peninsula, giving Pinor del Rio province a pounding with heavy rains and strong winds. ### taiyang — 2018-01-06T13:23:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-send-a-private-message.20655/post-231769>

how do i send a private message?

### Inger — 2018-01-06T13:37:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-send-a-private-message.20655/post-231770>

On the top of this site on the right are 3 buttons (in the same line where is "home" "forum" "members" but they are on the left), "your name", "inbox", and "alerts"
Just click on the "inbox" and you will enter the message page, and then just message who you want per their forum name . It is very simple and easy

### Inger — 2018-01-06T13:39:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-send-a-private-message.20655/post-231771>

you can also click on a member name, and then click on "start conversation"

# Thread: decrease brightness- increase flicker???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decrease-brightness-increase-flicker.20656/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-01-06T14:23:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decrease-brightness-increase-flicker.20656/post-231773>

i was listening to a podcast with dr. wunsch and ben greenfield in which they were saying that when you use apps that decrease brightness, you actually increase flicker ("But it sounds to me (Ben)like what you’re (dr. wunsch) saying is that some of these LED-based light bulbs, as well as some of the monitors, etc. that you can decrease brightness on, what you’re doing is decreasing the brightness and you’re increasing the flickering..."), which is harmful. is anyone familiar with this? if it is true, it makes more sense for me to disable f.lux and just look at a super bright screen with skin surfaces covered and uvex on. do you agree?

### Inger — 2018-01-06T16:47:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decrease-brightness-increase-flicker.20656/post-231789>

No not exactly

F.lux will not change or increase flickering afaik, but if you turn down the screen brighntess, that will increase flickering.
So you could use f.lux on darkest, and not turn the screen brightness down, and the use blueblockers

Or, you could get Iris blueblocking program on your computer, because I have read, it is designed in a way that even if you decrease screen brightness, the flickering does not increase.

I am not decreasing my screen brightness anymore. I used to, but stopped as I learned about the flickering issue. So I just use Iris on the darkest setting "sleep", it filters all blue and green. Or if I need to see more I just use "health", but only when sun is up. After sun is down and it gets dark I additionally use my blueblockers, and i try to get off my computer at least a few hours before bed. Ideally at 6 PM but sometimes I do stay a bit longer on it.  which is not ideal tho.

When I used f.lux I used it on the darkes setting always, except when outside in sunshine because then I could not see anything

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-06T16:50:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decrease-brightness-increase-flicker.20656/post-231791>

Iris is an anti flicker program

# Thread: Hello from New Zealand

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bryn — 2018-01-07T13:53:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.20661/post-231869>

I’m a newbie from NZ. I have t2d & hypertension and have been working very hard on curing myself of both for the past 2 years. The main method has been by LCHF & some resistance exercise. I came across Dr Kruse this past year and have been reading as much as possible. Have the Leptin Rx, about halfway through (on my iPad!). Have filled out the signature with my main details. Stopped metformin last year, bgl’s run a bit higher than I’d like. Stopped inhibace 3 months ago and reduced my atenalol by half just before Christmas. BP is ok but higher than before, maybe trending down.

### caroline — 2018-01-07T23:42:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.20661/post-231911>

Hi Bryn ...and welcome.

Please start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Jack more health history ... and Your Mom's and Gran's too.

Have you done 23&me test?

Have you watched Jack's two Vermont talks? and all his podcasts?

where in New Zealand? city? work?

Are you following all the Deuterium stuff?

### Bryn — 2018-01-08T08:55:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-zealand.20661/post-231940>

Thank you Jack & Caroline for your welcomes! I’ve been prowling around today looking for the optimal journal section, but my dopamine must be a bit low, I can’t find it. Can someone point me in the right direction please.

To answer your other questions, I have a plan to do 23&me but need to save up for that. I have retired and have a limited income currently. I have worked as a midwife for most of my working life.

My mum and some of her siblings have t2d as well as her mum also. My mum is 91, on insulin. Has been well but slowly fading at the mo.

I live in New Zealand on a small peninsula into the Tauranga harbour, outside of the main city but sadly now getting very built up. I’ll add a map. Good fishing but I can’t handle the boat alone.

How many different diseases are you going to try and "prevent" if you are using the wrong tools for the etiology????

Jack's discussion on altered electric fields on the skin around ink make me feel like people get addicted to the nnEMF environment that their tattoos create in the same way people get addicted to blue light. People often reject this concept because ancestral tribes also used to tattoo themselves. You can see in some of Alexander Wunsch's presentations that some of this body art would glow in the dark. I think it's possible that these ancient peoples were trying to enhance their wireless connection to the native electromagnetic fields on earth with their tattoes and body art. But in a microwaved world, this effect backfires...

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-28T05:02:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2018-mexico-members-event-mitohacking-lesson-5g-warning.20662/post-235914>

Why cold cenotes, strong tropical sun and calcium carbonate holes in the ground holding 65 million years of groundwater matter?
1\. Limestone filters water to deplete it of deuterium over time.
2\. The pH at room temperature is ~7 meaning there are about 10^-7 moles of H+ per liter of water at room temperature. As the temperature increases, the ability of water to ionize in this way increases and so the concentration of H+ in solution will increase and hence the pH will drop. This favors the formation of H+ bonds in an aqueous heat bath we call a cell. Just how big a deal is it when you are a true blue mitochondriac? In all cases, raising the temperature invokes thermal vibrational and entropic effects. This tends to preferentially stabilize H+ over D bonds. Mother Nature knew exactly what she was doing when she made our stolen bacteria at our core innovate uncoupling proteins and haplotype variations. The more heat you liberate the more deuterium you excrete and the faster ATP is made because the spin rate of the ATPase increases and the TCA cycle performs like a Ferrari and not a Nissan Sentra. [https://pubs.acs.org/doi/abs/10.1021/ja9530376](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fpubs.acs.org%2Fdoi%2Fabs%2F10.1021%2Fja9530376&h=ATODH4ZjHD8MhbsPSVW1kT9KMhbb5hq4YbT_U9ci-QmAYa1e6mYH8gX5_QSrAgEQT5DMPhCpjUPlVyajhCLGSlPTQuNCFbyyJ8GTRoNFGd6zGD6w-D8q7wf8tIPdfp7SKjvD5h8)

^^^^^^that is why cold exposures eliminate Deuterium. Cold exposure on the skin causes mitochondria to uncouple more..........to release heat.

3\. Why does strong sunlight with UV/IR light do it without the need for uncoupling proteins? Is this why equatorial haplotypes have no need for UCP's? Yep. When the sun hits cell water what happens? The EZ begins. What does EZ stand for? Exclusion zone. What does it exclude? Protons. Anything bigger than a proton is locked out, so the EZ water becomes a quasicrystal of Hydrogen bonds to become a battery. What has double the atomic mass and radius of a proton? DEUTERIUM. How do we clear it so effectively? Deuterium has a magnetic moment which is double a protons. This means it is drawn to tissues with big mitochondrial clearance pathways: liver/gallbladder bile, enterocytes, and kidney. Any questions folks?

So why is Mexico the shit for a mitochondriac.....???? Is anyone feeling me now?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-28T05:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2018-mexico-members-event-mitohacking-lesson-5g-warning.20662/post-235915>

So when you call Senor Cruz up.........this should be what your day is like.

The magnetic flux in the crater is massively stronger, and since all cenotes are inside the crater ........what does that mean for deuterium depletion in Mx compared to anywhere else?

yep..........you're waking up now........

"AT&T wireless announced on Tuesday the first cities to get its 5G network. The carrier plans on installing 5G in 12 cities by the end of 2018, and on top of the list is Atlanta, Georgia and Dallas and Waco, Texas. The remaining cities will be announced at a later date."

Source: [https://techcrunch.com/2018/02/27/these-will-be-the-first-cities-getting-5g-from-sprint-and-t-mobile/](https://techcrunch.com/2018/02/27/these-will-be-the-first-cities-getting-5g-from-sprint-and-t-mobile/)
"Just last week, AT&T announced the first handful of cities where it’ll roll out its 5G network later this year. Today at Mobile World Congress, T-Mobile and Sprint did the same.

Sprint’s first 5G networks will go live in Chicago, Los Angeles, Dallas, Atlanta, Washington, DC and Houston.

T-Mobile will fire up 5G in New York, Los Angeles, Las Vegas and Dallas first, promising to have it up and running in 30 cities total by the end of the year."

This water can bury a massive amount of heat capacity inside of it to keep the cell as far from equilibrium as possible.

This makes it a key defense to nnEMF in the environment.

Because mitochondria make heat, understanding how this heat affects other physical properties of water is key. It appears nature has figured out how to delay the onset of entropy by using DDW and heat in unison. It appears to me to be the ultimate move to slow the rate of decay by controlling order and disorder physically by altering deuterium concentrations in cells. This is why the parabiosis studies show the blood of younger animals works so well. It is the water that a colony of young mitochondria make that provides the answer. The water in young blood is the key to wellness and disease resistance.

However, it should be noted, DDW is quite a different animal. In every experimental measurement I have ever seen, they seem to indicate that the changes of surface tension far exceed the corresponding change in density. This means that DDW has a massive effect on the topology of proteins as DDW wets the surface of the protein. This allows proteins to AVOID decay and degradation of ubiquitin marking. It appears the frequencies in sunlight is the key stimulus our cell needs to make this type of water in our colony of mitochondria. **

US

Ericsson**
Seattle, Washington and Plano, Texas

**Verizon** Sacramento, California to lead a three- to five-city 5G rollout in the second half of this year

The pilot will be available to customers in Ann Arbor, Michigan; Atlanta; Bernardsville, New Jersey; Brockton, Massachusetts; Dallas; Denver; Houston; Miami; Sacramento; Seattle; and Washington, D.C

_**AT&T** 5G Evolution_
5G Evolution in 23 major metros;
Atlanta; Austin; Boston; Bridgeport, Connecticut; Buffalo, New York; Chicago; Fresno; Greenville, South Carolina; Hartford, Connecticut; Houston; Indianapolis; Los Angeles; Louisville; Memphis; Nashville; New Orleans; Oklahoma City; Pittsburgh; San Antonio; San Diego; San Francisco; Tulsa, Oklahoma and Sacramento, California offering immersive entertainment experiences like augmented and virtual reality while on the go.

Last year, we also launched pre-standards 5G fixed wireless trials in Austin, Texas; Waco, Texas; Kalamazoo, Michigan and South Bend, Indiana

Att & T is deploying LTE-M technology across our network in Mexico.

||They also completed the first LTE-M international data session between the U.S. and Mexico,the world's first international data session on LTE-M.

**Europe**

San Marino is set to become the first European country to have a super-fast fifth-generation (5G) mobile network under a deal struck by the tiny republic and Telecom Italia.

Hamburg

Turin

UK
will be linked up in 5G testbeds

This is a good list you have given us and we all want to know when 5G networks will be up and running in our areas.

Of course, 5G deployment plans could change at any time. We are rely heavily on Dr Kruse to keep us up to date on rapidly changing 5G technical and health developments.

FYI---There is an interesting little article on lessemf.com _(I'm sure some of the Kruse tribe members have perused this site)_ about 5G in general. It is a little out of date (Aug 2017) but it has some real nuggets I haven't found elsewhere. Below are some tidbits I found fascinating:

First, 5G and the proposed 5G mm waves are not necessarily the same. There will be a number of 5G carrier frequencies, and initial public network rollout will be using carrier frequencies of less than 6 GHz. So many existing RF meters like the Acoustimeter should be able to measure this RF frequency for now.

The short distance mm frequency waves will be used to communicate between base stations (including the new mini-antennaes that are now popping up around buildings on power poles and what have you) and your mobile devices (and presumably public and private wifi routers). They will come at you with frequencies from 24 GHz to 90 GHz. These mm wave networks are in testing stage now.

Existing cell phone handsets can not work with mm waves and so they will be useless until new models with proper chips replace them. That will be very expensive and take time to replace billions of user wireless devices out there.

There is unlikely to be any _widespread_ 5G network rollout before 2020. The operational mm wave networks would likely follow a few years later and initially at least be rolled out in high value city centers.

Also, according to the article:

_"We are working on instruments for detecting the
mm-wave frequencies, but they are unlikely to be
available for sale before 2019. There are
antenna, sensor and power consumption issues that
make it difficult to make cost-effective wide-
band measurements at these frequencies."_

### Lahelada — 2018-07-24T21:01:14+0300

### Lahelada — 2018-08-03T15:25:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-rollout-where-is-it-happening.20666/post-247373>

[https://www.silicon.co.uk/mobility/deutsche-telekom-5g-berlin-232133](https://www.silicon.co.uk/mobility/deutsche-telekom-5g-berlin-232133)
Telekom has integrated the first 5G antennas into their commercial network in BERLIN usig the sub 6GHZ frequency.
"
The three cells in Leipziger Strasse and three in Winterfeldstrasse use the 5G New Radio (5G NR) standard for mobile 5G, and are the beginning of a planned 5G cluster in the city.

The cluster initially spans an area of five kilometres, with an additional 70 cells planned for te city by this summer across a total of more than 20 sites. The firm said it intends to construct a “major 5G testbed” in the city as it prepares for the technology’s deployment."

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-08T18:56:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-rollout-where-is-it-happening.20666/post-252635>

Recent LA Times article talks about 5G infrastructure being speedily deployed in LA and raises a new issue.

Federal government has recently decided to nationalize 5G wireless infrastructure (Dr Kruse mentioned this in a post). FCC'S intervention will give telecoms a break with regard to installation fees that cities can charge for huge numbers of 5G mini antennas on city utility poles. Of course cities are up in arms about this intrusion into their traditional rights to charge fees as they see appropriate.

Excerpts from article:

_"The FCC says the new rule will save money for telecommunications companies, which will redirect those funds to deploy 5G service to less-connected rural areas."

"Industry experts dispute this hypothesis — and leading telecoms say rural areas are not their first priority for 5G service."

"Scott Mair, AT&T’s president of operations, said “small cell technology will start initially for mobile networks in dense urban areas and suburban areas.”

“Rural America will have 5G over time,” he said._

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-11-03T17:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-rollout-where-is-it-happening.20666/post-254978>

I have an Acoustimeter.

Unfortunately,it is not very useful for detecting individual source RF emissions (e.g. cell towers, masts, radio antennas, wi-fi routers, etc) by direction from user. It's antenna is designed mostly to detect ambient RF levels.

A good RF meter for this purpose appears to be [Gigahertz Solutions HF35C](http://www.aitsafe.com/go.htm?go=www.lessemf.com/rf.html&afid=50042&tm=90&im=1#481) -- it has a directional antenna (of course you pay more for this feature). I'm sure there are other suitable directional meters out there.

### James Gerard — 2018-11-04T00:26:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-rollout-where-is-it-happening.20666/post-255002>

So I google searched. Stop 5G and immediately got this blog as one of the results where the 'anonymous' guest talks about an interesting way to stop development of 5G rollout. [http://www.blogtalkradio.com/lostartsradio/2018/05/28/lost-arts-radio-show-179--special-guest-paul-g](http://www.blogtalkradio.com/lostartsradio/2018/05/28/lost-arts-radio-show-179--special-guest-paul-g)

There is an interesting idea about what he calls 'dark fiber' which is fiber optic cable that was installed years ago but never used because the major communication utilities were waiting for it to depreciate so they could hide it on their accounting end and because the communications companies are now private, you can take your local jurisdiction to task over the fiber optic backbone in order to use it. The idea is to take away the fiber optic cable usage going to wireless towers with the power of utility rights. Personally I think they would just run more cable, but if what he is saying is true ( and who knows he's anonymous as a source) People might have a chance to slow it down if not stop it. I have only listened up to this part so far which is between34 and 37 minutes into the blog where he talks about it.

Salford, Manchester,UK

V**odafone first to switch on full 5G in the Uk**

"Live mobile data traffic is now being streamed to and from the Internet exclusively over 5G from a site in Salford, Greater Manchester"

"Providing coverage to MediaCityUK and the surrounding area..". It is enabling Vodafone to test the technology end-to-end using wireless routers and forms part of its 5G trial in seven cities this year."

,"Vodafone UK Chief Executive Nick Jeffery, said: “We are leading the roll out of 5G across the UK, starting with Greater Manchester. A further six cities – Birmingham, Bristol, Cardiff, Glasgow, Liverpool and London – will shortly receive full 5G too. Next year, we will bring 5G to the Scottish Highlands, Cornwall and the Lake District, among other locations."

"Vodafone’s network engineers are already achieving high-speed Internet connectivity and low latency from the Salford site using Active Antenna (Massive MIMO) technology. This effectively provides multiple-data ‘channels’ from a single antenna, coupled with our 3.4GHz 5G spectrum"

### SlamSlask — 2018-11-05T20:21:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-rollout-where-is-it-happening.20666/post-255087>

These are my notes for the 5G rollouts in Denmark/Scandinavia:
"Mastloven" (the lay conserning antennes and mast placements) laws in Denmark and turns out they just agreed on a completly new set of laws om this area (last revised in 1999)
The major point revision here is that there is now very few scenarios where landlords in city districts can say no to new antennas
In the last 10 years they ha"ve been converting even small villages with under 100 citizens to "City Districts"

As far as i know there is 3 network providers in scandinavia:
3 (Hi3G), Telia and TDC

Hi3G (called "3" in Scandinavia) are using atennas from Ericson
They started rolling this out in fall 2018 in Denmark
have seen are few articles that state them having went live in other capitals in 2018
[https://www.ericsson.com/en/press-r...o-develop-transport-network-for-5g-in-denmark](https://www.ericsson.com/en/press-releases/2018/8/hi3g-selects-ericsson-to-develop-transport-network-for-5g-in-denmark)

Telia started rolling out 5G in the other countries except Danmark
This was due to Denmark begin slower to bring out new regulations
The best info i could get was from their customer support who told me that they are testing this around the airport in Copenhagen (Kastrup)
Was not able to get info on which equipment they will be using
[https://www.telegeography.com/produ...k-conducts-5g-demonstrations-at-headquarters/](https://www.telegeography.com/products/commsupdate/articles/2018/08/16/telia-denmark-conducts-5g-demonstrations-at-headquarters/)
[https://di.dk/English/News/Pages/DI-Send-more-5G-funding-to-Denmark.aspx](https://di.dk/English/News/Pages/DI-Send-more-5G-funding-to-Denmark.aspx)

TDC is teaming up with Huewai and seems to be the ones who are the one leading the race a they are allready providing 5G to specific buisness in the major cities

Seems like this is going to be the baseline for connecting the towers

Asked 50 random pepole on street in a major city and only 2 of them had heard about 5G - After alot of searching l only found 1 radio podcast talking about 5G and i spent 15 out of 60 mins litteraly calling pepole who are afraid of the health concerns "Morons" (then again this comes from the 1 channel that is force taxed here in Denmark)

Turns out that the premier ministers in Scandinavia had a little get togheter in May this year to integrate the 5G networks and plan the rollout
It is only about 2 months since Denmark released the new set of Laws that I mentioned before (Released meaning released to the public) at this point it was allready voted for in the parlement
This video talks about the the political aspects

Hearing in the danish parlement:

Full version of this public hearing (some parts are in english) - The danish women is a nurse who talks the effect and some EHS pepole who have trouble keeping a job etc

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-11-13T15:38:21+0200

### Matt Justice — 2019-04-11T15:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-rollout-where-is-it-happening.20666/post-267864>

A bit old, but I saw this for some reason...

I've used an Acoustimeter for a long time for indoor rf policing... works excellent, but is not really a long range rf meter for cell towers, nor does it have a directional external antennae.

However, if it's all you can afford, there is a hack for it. Line a shoebox with two layers of aluminum foil, place the meter inside, and redo all you measurements. If you have indoor penetration from outside sources, that should allow you to determine where it's coming from.

I just wouldn't rely on the numbers for power density, but it's actually a really sensitive little meter... one of my faves.

If you're trying to account for any of the 5g spectrum, disregard everything I said. Acoustimeter frequency range taps out at 8ghz.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-04-12T19:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-rollout-where-is-it-happening.20666/post-267926>

As far at its frequency reach is concerned, I believe it will be useful for some time to come because 5G is not fully developed yet and I think most telecoms are using up to 6 GHz for now in transition period.

We have a daughter who just turned 2. Now we are contemplating if we should have another kid or not. I am really confused about it..... The reason we want another is.... so our daughter doesn’t feel alone..... we are four siblings and have each other’s back all the time. It makes me feel sad that if we don’t give her sibling she will miss so much

But the reason I am thinking so much about it is.... it’s definitely lots of work.... mentally and physically both.I have had a stillbirth before my daughter was born......And above all I will be 35 soon so biological clock is also clicking.

Of course this should always a personal decision . But I would like to know about this community’s opinion on 2 kids vs 1

My Mom and Dad loved each other.
They were dirt poor.
Germans invaded Poland (Sept 1939)
My dad is drafted to defend his Country.
Before he left to fight they were in camps for few weeks to organize.
They had no food, women had to bring food to men in camps.
My parents decided that they want to have a child so my Mom could have some memory about my Dad if he would get killed.
That is why I am here.
My Dad came from WWII in one piece when I was about 5yo and joined us.
My parents were fighting about which one have better idea to raise me as a good person.
Not sure if they succeeded but definitely they tried.
...........
.
From some of your posts it sounds as you are good person.
There is newer to many of good people.
Get some more.

# Thread: Juliet's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/juliets-optimal-journal.20669/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Juliet — 2018-01-08T21:14:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/juliets-optimal-journal.20669/post-231982>

My natural path recently introduced me to Dr Kruse. I became a Gold member after reading his posts. Still reading and listening to the posts/blogs to better understand how this information can help me.

I am 62. Retired from high stress executive job and moved to Scottsdale AZ in 2013 from Singapore. I am 5'8" weigh 126 lbs.

Current Symptoms: SIBO, motility issues (need lemon water in the morning to have a BM), a lot of pain in my intestinal track at night that wakes me up, a lot of gas especially at night sometimes terrible order, frequent urination at night, lack of energy, can feel my body vibrating inside (like an electric current) trouble sleeping usually fall asleep but never more than 4 hours before I am awake, my hands and feet are usually freezing, all this makes me rather down as I lack the vitality to be excited about life.

History: Appendix out 8 years old, broke both arms at different times as a child, mono at 16, extremely emotional periods 13-50, minimal change disease of the kidneys 1993 (put on prednisone for 1 yr to kill my immune system), not sure year but very high liver enzymes doctor couldn't diagnose, complete adrenal exhaustion 1999 (off work 6 months), frequent colds throughout my life, BP usually low but periodically when exhausted drops to 70/50, polyps in 30, then 9 in 2011, since retired no polyps. Two falls: Fractured pelvis in 4 places in Tokyo in 2010, ruptured all 3 hamstring tendons in right leg in 2015 - surgery went well, found parasite in Feb 2017 oregano protocol.

Recent labs: low WBC, dehydration, low thyroid

Diet: Been on an organic paleo diet for a long time used to be able to have a few cheats but now I can hardly eat anything. I have incorporated more fish back into my diet after reading the blogs. And even though it was VERY hard I stopped eating my morning banana. Have lost 6 pounds over the past month which I don't need to do. I do have my hot water/lemon every morning as stated above. Allergic/food sensitivities to eggs, gluten, garlic, onions, turmeric, all beans including green, yellow squashes, thyme, mustard, many nuts to name a few)

Medication: bio-identical hormones since 2011, bio-D-Mulsion Forte, magnesium malate, EPA/DHA, zinc picolinate, ubiquinol, synergy K, Aller-C, Cortrex, biospora ( we have tried other probiotics and they all irritate my gut

Hacks incorporated: I wear the orange glasses at night. Have put amber lights in bedroom lamps. Still trying to understand all the information here and what to do. I bio-hacked my cell phone as instructed on the video Dr. Kruse shared.

Personal: I am a single woman and want my health to be at its optimal level. The challenge is I still need to go out and just not sure how to incorporate all the hacks and live life with others.

Would appreciate any feedback.

### caroline — 2018-01-09T09:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/juliets-optimal-journal.20669/post-232060>

A smart meter is how they read your electricity .......where is your meter? where are your neighbour's meters? They could be facing your bedroom window. You can shield them pretty easily - there are you tube videos.

You need a meter to check your nnEMF. You can get an electrical engineer who specialises in this. Michael Neurt did a series of webinars for us - I think you can still purchase these.

Michel does phone consults and rents out equipment.

Scompy here on the forum has started a group with other members called EMF warriors - check them out.

There is a great you tube video by a Canadian army guy - about smart meters etc. - very scary stuff.

The best way to get going on all this is to just do it and sort out all the science later........ the three legged stool - light water and magnetism

There is a thread here somewhere about the best places to live - it is a lot of info on different places ....the pros and cons.

Scompy has done a guest blog about what it took for him to find an optimal place for him and his family.

Baby steps! keep asking questions!

Have you read Jack's thread about the importance of Newbies?

### caroline — 2018-01-09T09:11:53+0200

one other thing ... How is your sleep?

You know that you don't have to necessarily sleep in your bedroom .....one woman here found the dining room was the only place she could get a decent nite's sleep.

Maybe go camping for a week or two and see how you feel....

also - the leptin reset isn't only for overnight people ..... it is all about resetting the rolex in your brain \[your circadian rhythm\]. Please read the leptin reset easy start guide and the C/T easy start guide.

How is your libido?

I love how you have just dived in head first! That's what I did ....just do it and then figure out the science later. It just all made so much sense to me. This is a life altering optimal journey - enjoy this amazing ride!

### WalterNL — 2018-01-09T10:17:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/juliets-optimal-journal.20669/post-232064>

> [Juliet said:](/goto/post?id=232037)
>
> ok I will find out where to go to actually see the sunrise I have incorporated the face dunks and I love them. working towards my body. Can you do it in a cold pool if there is chlorine? Dr, Kruse am setting up an appointment with you once Christy knows your schedule.

Chlorine is not ideal because it has a low dielectric constant, anything with a low dielectric constant is less able to keep a high charge and can keep charge for a shorter period of time. Our cells need water with a high charge to function optimally. So it's important for drinking water to have little or no substances with low dielectric constants. Here's a list of dielectric constants (pdf): [https://www.vega.com/-/media/PDF-files/List\_of\_dielectric\_constants\_EN.ashx](https://www.vega.com/-/media/PDF-files/List_of_dielectric_constants_EN.ashx)

But that doesn't have to be a dealbreaker for CT. It depends on your N=1.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-collective-insights-podcast-gut-health-redox-quantum-signaling.20670/post-232003>

Hey all,

Did anyone else listen to the recent Collective Insights podcast with Dr. Zach Bush, MD, who sells the Restore4Life "ancient soil" quantum-signalling product?

[Link: http://neurohacker.com/gut-health-microbiome-dr-zach-bush-m-d/
](http://neurohacker.com/gut-health-microbiome-dr-zach-bush-m-d/)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-08T23:51:46+0200

### Sue-UK — 2018-01-09T14:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-collective-insights-podcast-gut-health-redox-quantum-signaling.20670/post-232081>

I was at Micropia in Amsterdam over New Year, [https://www.micropia.nl/en/discover/stories/microworld/](https://www.micropia.nl/en/discover/stories/microworld/) and was fascinated by the exhibits showing how we increase diversity of our bacteria by hugging, kissing, holding hands, breathing, food water etc .... and I was peering through microscopes at symbiotic relationships ....They didn't get as far as mentioning the mitochondria but the microbiome in terms of love was quite interesting ....all that microbiome diversity sharing .....  But at the talk it was mentioned that a particular species of octopus had a symbiotic relationship with bacteria using light. It allowed (or attracted?) the bacteria to its underside during daylight, and the bacteria emitted blue light, effectively obscuring any shadow. To a predator underneath looking for shadows above, the octopus merged with the water and daylight coming down through the water. At night the octopus released (or repelled?) the bacteria as the same trick would have been its downfall under dark skies. I was in awe as to whether the octopus controlled the bacteria via secreting substances that either attracted or repelled in a circadian rhythmic day/night cycle way, or whether it was the interaction of the microbiome and the mitochondria of the organism as a whole .... i.e. light (consciousness?) signalling to the tenants sitting on its underside to get lost or quiet down until daylight ...

Interesting from the transcript of the podcast about air fermentation and bacteria at different times of day:

_"zach bush: \[inaudible\] Yeah, so I like wild fermentation a hundred percent and the problem with all the starters is they’re basically probiotics, right, so you got one species of acidophilus or a few strains of lactobacilli or the like, and so I’m a big fan of doing air fermentation. What that looks like is you make your salt water brine. Takes pennies. Then, you just chop up cabbage, or my favorite is chop up a bunch of turnips, throw those into your brine, and then put a towel over the crock and let that air ferment for two to three days **where the bacteria from the air is populating that**.
One of my favorite things is just to move it out to different sides of your house. **In the morning put it out on your eastern porch or out in the yard where it can get some of that, and then in the evenings put it at sunset**. Move it around the yard a couple times just for an hour or so is enough and then bring it back inside, keep the towel over it the whole time. The bacteria will filter through the little cheese cloth or towel whatever you got over it, just so you’re not dropping leaves in your ferment._ "

The bacteria filtering through the cheesecloth reminds me of the lungs .....

Rather than cabbage or turnips, I'm hoping that getting my whole microbiome out there at different times of day for a bit of air fermentation with some light water and magnetism, and a varied seasonal diet (the compost?) might increase my biodiversity and save me some $$$$ on a compost supplement ...

### drezy — 2018-01-09T21:12:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-collective-insights-podcast-gut-health-redox-quantum-signaling.20670/post-232118>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=232114)
>
> Well, the man I loved left me tho..a few months ago.. for another woman...

We've all been stung I think. I'll even relate a similar story I've never told here on the forums before, from the male perspective:

Out of college I brought a girl to a new city to start a life. We had nothing but had it all, or so I thought. Every morning I would make us both coffee with the best little coffee percolator in the world. That percolator bubbled and gurgled the beat as we both prepared for our days. Over time the gal starting setting eyes( and other body parts) on other guys so I asked her to leave and take whatever she wanted since I'm not very materialistic. Low and behold, as I returned to my apartment that night, along with taking my broken heart she took the percolator. She didn't even know how to operate it. I always made the coffee. My friends still remind me that as phased as I was by the cheating the missing percolator came up often in conversations.

Years passed and I met a way better woman that later became my wife and mother to my child. Oh and we eventually got an espresso machine on a crazy deep discount sale that makes an even better cup of coffee.

Moral of the story: Mother time will heal your heart, but she sure as hell won't return your awesome coffee percolator so don't even hold out hopes for that happening.

[@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)
Interesting from the transcript of the podcast about air fermentation and bacteria at different times of day:

_"zach bush: \[inaudible\] Yeah, so I like wild fermentation a hundred percent and the problem with all the starters is they’re basically probiotics, right, so you got one species of acidophilus or a few strains of lactobacilli or the like, and so I’m a big fan of doing air fermentation. What that looks like is you make your salt water brine. Takes pennies. Then, you just chop up cabbage, or my favorite is chop up a bunch of turnips, throw those into your brine, and then put a towel over the crock and let that air ferment for two to three days **where the bacteria from the air is populating that**.
One of my favorite things is just to move it out to different sides of your house. **In the morning put it out on your eastern porch or out in the yard where it can get some of that, and then in the evenings put it at sunset**. Move it around the yard a couple times just for an hour or so is enough and then bring it back inside, keep the towel over it the whole time. The bacteria will filter through the little cheese cloth or towel whatever you got over it, just so you’re not dropping leaves in your ferment._ "

The bacteria filtering through the cheesecloth reminds me of the lungs .....

Rather than cabbage or turnips, I'm hoping that getting my whole microbiome out there at different times of day for a bit of air fermentation with some light water and magnetism, and a varied seasonal diet (the compost?) might increase my biodiversity and save me some $$$$ on a compost supplement ... \[/QUOTE\]
Hi Sue-UK,
I was just reading some new posts of Dr. Kruse and he said probiotics are not the answer. For about 4 months I have been fermenting seasonal cabbage, radishes, and cauliflower in 2 cups water, 1 Tbsp sea salt, and wakame. My best batches were the ones not with a lid. I will use the towel as a lid. Is this a waste of time? Am I doing better for myself because I am outside grounded at 7:30 am, 9am breakfast, noon, and 2pm lunch. They do taste great, a vehicle for getting sea salt and wakame for iodine.

Hi [@Joyfun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21372/),
Personally, I don't think that you are wasting your time with fermenting your veggies. I also enjoy making my own, and even more so eating them.  I don't know if Dr. Kruse had them in mind when he talked about the probiotics. Until he clarifies that, I am inclined to think that the actual probiotic supplements are at issue here. There are times when they can be helpful, especially they are trully needed. Unfortunately, it has become a case of too much of a good thing.

### Joyfun — 2018-06-07T07:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-collective-insights-podcast-gut-health-redox-quantum-signaling.20670/post-242892>

[@Tanya](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6641/)
Hi [@Joyfun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21372/),
Personally, I don't think that you are wasting your time with fermenting your veggies. I also enjoy making my own, and even more so eating them.  I don't know if Dr. Kruse had them in mind when he talked about the probiotics. Until he clarifies that, I am inclined to think that the actual probiotic supplements are at issue here. There are times when they can be helpful, especially they are trully needed. Unfortunately, it has become a case of too much of a good thing.\[/QUOTE\]
Hi Tanya,
I was thinking over what I said and was thinking about editing that post to indicate that perhaps Dr. Kruse was talking about supplements and probiotics in supplement form. I am glad that you felt the topic of probiotics needs more clarification. I am loving my fermented veggies. Now that it is summer, I am also fermenting watermelon rinds with garlic and dill like my grandmother did. What a wonderful memory of the 1940's and 1950's. They have the crispness and flavor of a kosher dill pickle, and it's free!

### Jack Kruse — 2019-11-20T18:03:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-collective-insights-podcast-gut-health-redox-quantum-signaling.20670/post-282567>

Another study shows why docs like Zach Bush have zero understanding of how sunlight sculpts the microbiome. If he did he would never try to sell any human restore.......he's sell them on vacations to the sun. This is how Vitamin D is created in the body by UVB LIGHT exposure on the skin and NOT by oral supplementation. This ultimately leads to big improvements in gut bacteria populations. Looks like another win for Kruse Longevity Center in stomping out marketers for the public.
Skin Exposure to Narrow Band Ultraviolet (UVB) Light Modulates the Human Intestinal Microbiome
Microbiol., 24 October 2019
Participants who took vitaminD supplements and use the narrowband had no improvement in microbiome diversity.
Participants who used narrowband uvb light only had significant improvements.
Bacteria from several families were enriched in the group that did not take prior vitamin D supplements.
This is the first study to show changes in intestinal microbiome in response to Narrowband-UVB skin exposure.
This evidence suggests the existence of a novel skin-gut axis that could be used to promote intestinal homeostasis and health.
[https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full)

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-11-21T17:46:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-collective-insights-podcast-gut-health-redox-quantum-signaling.20670/post-282603>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=232007)
>
> I have hacked a lot of his stuff and I am not a fan. Will be interesting to see what others say.

I have dabbled a bit with _Restore_ but not too seriously. No remarkable effects from it, but that is just limited **N=1** anecdotal jive.

More interestingly, humic and fulvic acids (also found in _Restore_) are said to help detox heavy metals like aluminum in body-- now that is a lead worth following up on.

# Thread: Mayuri's Optimal Journal - Been Following Dr. Jack Since Feb. 2017
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayuris-optimal-journal-been-following-dr-jack-since-feb-2017.20674/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mayuri — 2018-01-09T03:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayuris-optimal-journal-been-following-dr-jack-since-feb-2017.20674/post-232043>

Genetics:
Maternal Haplogroup J1c
86% Italian

Mother developed dementia, had sleep apnea, 8 small strokes - high carb high sugar diet, high stress and she was seldom outdoors. Neurologist gave her 5 years after diagnosis, rapid dementia - she died 5 years after diagnosis at 76 yrs. old. Grandmother was insulin diabetic - very active traveller, was head of a typing pool all her life, developed pancreatic cancer.

Prior to getting sick, my work was in a University town with mega-technology for 12 years, wifi and modem computers everywhere and very long work hours. I had flu every single winter, never any sunlight (even in the summers). Before diagnosis my doctor told me I had slight fatty liver disease, he said not to worry because everyone had it. I never ate meat or fish, only soya burgers and soya this and that.

Before that, I lived in the tropics for 21 years, never ever was sick. Was in the sunshine everyday with lots of water sports and swimming, although I was still working long hours with computers.

Live in Oz now, we've been following you faithfully and we are doing really well with your protocols. We're out everyday in the sunshine, even when it's freezing out. We're drinking deuterium free water and eating all the good things. Change is hard, the body revolts.

Doing real well, my blood work looks great, and hoping to continue to do well.
The prognosis scares the crap out of one, high recurrence rates - makes one value life more.
Your January 2018 lecture was great.

I am interested in stem cells. Your stem lecture is whoa, need to listen to it a few more times.
Thanks for E V E R Y T H I N G in a big big way!

I want you to watch this video from 34:00 onward. Realize in Europe, the power grid oscillates at 50 Hz and the US it vibrates at 60Hz. This means a light screwed into an EU socket flickers at 100 times per second as the second harmonic. In the USA the power grid oscillates at 60 Hz and a light screwed in will flicker 120 times per second. This flicker rate affects the recycle rate of DHA in the photoreceptors because of how the H+ in NADPH is used to build cell membranes. What else does this affect? It affects the spin of the ATPase because the H+ in NADH at cytochrome 1 comes from the TCA cycle. Did you know that hemoglobin in blood plasma oscillates between the oxy and deoxy state too? This means that the flicker rate of the power grid in your house and job environment can also affect your oxygenation rate. We call this pseudohypoxia. This is why NAD+ and pseudohypoxia are related to disease and aging. This set of circumstances ruins mitochondrial function. Did you know all the intermediates of the TCA cycle all oscillate in this range too? This means that the power grid in your wall can affect the TCA substrates to cause low NAD+ and high glucose rates in your blood to fix the TCA cycle. How does this occur? It is a wireless molecular resonance phenomena between electromagnetic waves and the proteins that make cells up. The principle of molecular resonance is easy to understand. Light is an oscilatory wave from the outside of our body and the chemicals in the photoreceptors are the inner oscillators inside our body. We need no wires or connection for this collision to occur. Mitochondria in the RPE can also be thought as inner oscillators in this wireless system. To get a wireless resonance to operate between the power grid all we need is an electromagnetic oscillation to equal the inner oscillator's absorbance spectrum. At the 34 minutes you'll see we have many oscillators that work in the range of the global power grid. When this occurs this leads to wireless resonance between the power gird's frequencies and the hydrated proteins in many systems that can cause problems we call diseases. The resonance mechanism is capable of vibratory oscillations that move the molecular parts in the various proteins and organelles in cells. Since light is an electromagnetic waves it is propagating radiation that can cause both chemical oscilaltion and a photochemical oscillation in the retina to affect our ability to put DHA back where it belongs in cell membranes. This is one of the core features of leptin resistance at the level of the liver. # Thread: Tattoo Removal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.20678/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jamie Ward — 2018-01-09T11:07:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.20678/post-232068>

I thought I would start a thread... have a small one on upper shoulder and I plan to finally get it removed - however, I am going to do my research correctly so I will attempt to track that here and draw in any wisdom from others.

### WalterNL — 2018-01-09T12:18:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.20678/post-232071>

Don't have any tattoos but it's interesting to find out the optimal way for removal.

> Tattoo removal is most commonly performed using lasers that break down the ink particles in the tattoo. **The broken-down ink is then fought off by the immune system**, **mimicking the natural fading that time or sun exposure would create.** All tattoo pigments have specific light absorption spectra. \[..\]
>
> To mitigate pain the preferred method is simply to **cool the area before and during treatment with a medical-grade chiller/cooler** and to use a topical anesthetic. \[..\]
>
> The normal process of tattoo removal is fragmentation followed by [phagocytosis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phagocytosis) which is then drained away via the [lymphatics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lymphatic_system). Consequently, **it’s the inflammation resulting from the actual laser treatment and the natural stimulation of the hosts’s immune response that ultimately results in removal of tattoo ink**; thus variations in results are enormous.\[..\]
> [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tattoo\_removal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tattoo_removal)

Thinking out loud here: use more CT while tattoo removal is in process. Perhaps add anti-inflammatory foods/spices, anything else to support immune function (in other words follow Kruse's protocols).
Heavy metals will have to be removed from your body, anything to expedite that process could help. It seems like removal is a very localized burning of the skin, so indeed cooling would be important afterwards.
Smart to do it nearing end of summer because your redox will be in top shape.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-09T17:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.20678/post-232094>

I would not do it because often removal is worse than keeping it.

### kris90 — 2018-01-17T18:28:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.20678/post-232755>

Was wondering about removal also as I have 2 tattoos myself. I try not to live with regret though (especially since my daughter's name is on my back) and am thankful that I now know better not to get anymore. I think I'll leave mine alone.

### Pebbles — 2018-03-23T00:06:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.20678/post-237783>

For some reason can not post pics.... however I dried for my mom a mole during a summer ...2-3 month - just painting it with iodine - not lugol, , could be a SSKI but in alcohol instead of water (10-30%)- it is sold in pharmacy here for nail fungus. Painting 2x per day and drying in the sun - fallen off, skin clear.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-06T00:43:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tattoo-removal.20678/post-240595>

Today's tattoo lesson: More on how your surfaces can be affected by a lack of sunlight and methionine in your skin. People with bad urea cycles should never have red tattoos unless they know how to source the ink themselves. Why? Below is a tattoo that this person had for six years. In December of 2014, they noted some changes within the red ink in it and decided to have it looked at. A biopsy came back as a type of skin cancer. The red pigment in the tattoo called cinnabar attacked my immune system so I have to have it removed either in a series of three surgeries or one-time removal with a skin graft. This is just another perspective of how getting a tattoo can be used as a hack. The teaching point in this person was they were blue light toxic and had a broken Kreb's bicycle. This slowed the urea cycle allowed methionine to build up and destroy autophagy for years until apoptosis was destroyed because this area NEVER got any sunlight. What was the result? Cancer in the red. Cinnabar is a compound that has mercury in it. It is safe unless your urea cycle is broken by hydrogen isoforms as you age. Did you know that food with methionine can clear mercury from our body? Mitochondriacs need to make sure if you get a tattoo that you think of all of the things that can go wrong and how your body could be potentially disfigured from it. You can also use your tattoo's as a canary in the coal mine for the efficiency of autophagy and apoptosis if you choose to keep it. # Thread: Cold thermogenesis: fatigue after cold sea swimming
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-fatigue-after-cold-sea-swimming.20680/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Goldenboy — 2018-01-10T19:12:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-fatigue-after-cold-sea-swimming.20680/post-232192>

Hi all -

i'm getting quite cold adapted, living mainly in shorts and a t-shirt, swimming daily in the cold sea and always showering cold (sometimes upping and down temps).

i'm very tired about an hour after sea swimming (temp aprox 14 degrees centigrade) i have relatively manageable ME, though have to be very careful with exercise as it wipes me out). CT seems to be having a similar though not as extreme effect; it makes me need sleep and i'm REALLY hungry afterwards.

any tips: less time in sea; only every other day in the sea; only go in the sea directly after a fat/protein meal etc? what?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-11T03:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-fatigue-after-cold-sea-swimming.20680/post-232219>

> You want to sleep yet eat at the same time post CT?

Yes. I feel very hungry post CT and also have a desire to sleep. This has happened since the sea temperatures have dipped to around 14/15 degrees Celsius where I live.

### drezy — 2018-01-19T18:21:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-fatigue-after-cold-sea-swimming.20680/post-232928>

Lol. That reminds me of the "Doctor it hurts when I do this..." joke.

> [Goldenboy said:](/goto/post?id=232905)
>
> Yes. I feel very hungry post CT and also have a desire to sleep. This has happened since the sea temperatures have dipped to around 14/15 degrees Celsius where I live.

Yeah over the two I'd opt for sleep as well.

From : [https://www.jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-9-quantum-sleep/](https://www.jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-9-quantum-sleep/)

> Meanwhile the research also showed lack of sleep was linked with the activation of genes associated with cell stress and death. Sleep is vital for wellness because it helps maintain myelin, which is critical in maintaining our ability to regenerate tissues when we sleep in autophagy. Becker was the first person to suggest this long ago.

Based on that lane when my body tells me it's time to sleep I don't screw around.

Also if you manage to sleep a little hungry you're doing a great nice fasting session for "free" as well. You'll get to wake up the next day and tackle your BAB like a bear fresh out of hibernation.

Now with CT water temps at 40-44F (4.4 - 6.7C) here everyone in my house sleeps earlier on CT days. I've yet to officially track and correlate the earlier CT sleepiness with solar yield but I'm guessing it's related.

CT seems to be having a similar though not as extreme effect; it makes me need sleep and i'm REALLY hungry afterwards.
>
> any tips: less time in sea; only every other day in the sea; only go in the sea directly after a fat/protein meal etc? what?

That was happening to me - I cured it by - believe it or not - less swimming and more doing nothing while out in the ocean - the only thing I could think is I actually didn't have the energy required to swim even though I felt really energized while out there - I have always been "starving" after a swim - even in a pool - no other sport makes me as hungry - you also might not be eating enough - I mean, you have to burn something if it's fat or sugar or glutamine...

### Goldenboy — 2018-01-20T17:45:33+0200

### drezy — 2018-01-20T18:52:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-fatigue-after-cold-sea-swimming.20680/post-232970>

Quality sleep is great.

My family and I are averaging 3 times per week. Now that it's winter the water temperatures in my parent's pool is getting low (40F/4C) so we've gone from 45 min sessions to 20 minute sessions. I sleep a little earlier on the days. I've never really been good at napping.

Timing it before your nap seems like a good idea to me.

[https://www.magnetage.com/albert-roy-davis-lab-history.html](https://www.magnetage.com/albert-roy-davis-lab-history.html)

It might be helpful in understanding the effects of magnetism on Life. It contains several important warnings on magnetic exposure. The first - the importance of determining the true north and south pole of a magnet. The second - the dangers of exposure to south pole energies. Albert Roy Davis and his partner Dr. Walter Rawls spent many decades researching biomagnetism and developing a large number of tools to apply it. Unfortunately, in our current regulatory environment, much of that research is buried.

There are also articles on that site that discuss how to determine true north that are worthwhile reading.

Here are some of the issues with exposure to south pole energies:

Has a clockwise rotation

Excites

Increases pain

Increases inflammation

Produces an acid effect

Intensifies symptoms

Promotes microorganisms

Inhibits healing

Increases fluid retention

Decreases tissue oxygen

Stimulates wakefulness

Has an overactive effect

Encourages fatty deposits

Polarity of an injury site

Stimulates body function

How to get close to 60ppm

1 part of 25ppm mixed with 1 part of 150ppm
(25+150)/2=87.5ppm
(25+140)/2=82.5ppm
so half and half should be safe to use.

_My first Breathy test was **145.5 ppm** (target <130 ppm) and the speed of the rotor ATP head @#5 at idle is **20.5%** (target >70%). Second test after drinking the DDW is about a month away. The question: Will the ppm continue going down after I stop drinking the DDW of go back up? I would think the worse my environment, the sooner the ppm goes up and the sooner I would have to do another round of DDW. I would think doing regular CT and eating the AM sun and eating some DHA from the sea would by themselves make my efficiency of my ECT and spinning rotor head at idle improve.

Looks like I need to eat more: Olive oil ~130 ppm; Butter ~124 ppm; Fatty acid animal fat ~118 ppm to help keep ppm under 130....these ppm numbers from Science Friday Seminar - László G. Boros, M.D. December 13, 2013 "Deuterium depletion and mitochondrial NADPH production:"_

# Thread: Hypnagogic Hallucinations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypnagogic-hallucinations.20688/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HopingMom — 2018-01-12T04:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypnagogic-hallucinations.20688/post-232292>

Anyone else have experience with these hypnagogic hallucinations? I would describe as "mini dreams" experienced just at on set of sleep or when laying down. I've actually been experiencing this for years and thought everyone else did too, but I guess not. Usually I experience image flashes (like a dream/picture image), as of late it has changed into someone saying words-small phrases or names. It will happen to me anytime I lay down/relax, anytime of day. Before bed it is generally vivid textured/colored images-these don't make sense, but are extremely detailed and beautiful. It is not scary or anything like that.
Is this good/bad? What's the deal? Any info would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!

### av8r — 2018-01-12T11:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypnagogic-hallucinations.20688/post-232308>

I experience that, I also experience audible hallucinations at the threshold between awake and sleep that can be frustrating to say the least. Any sporadic noise, including any snorts or snores I create is amplified in my head and startles me awake. Years ago it drove me to the point of getting a sleep study done… results was repairing my deviated septum… it helped.

# Thread: Happily overwhelmed!

### BiuTze — 2018-01-12T04:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/happily-overwhelmed.20689/post-232293>

Hello all, this is my first post!
This post is meant to be a friendly introduction, a way for me to focus my attention, and maybe get a little direction. I kind of just want to get the past week off of my chest too, I've been doing a lot of things and I'm very excited about a lot of it.
I've been in the middle of a really good healing process for a few solid months, with this last month really making me feel like I'm on fire. I got back from a really cool retreat about 40 days ago, and I've been doing LOTS of research since then. It started off with a documentary about the kogi people of Columbia, which I watched on youtube. I saw a link to Gerald Pollacks Tedtalk the 4th phase of water from there, got curious about NIF and IF lighting to try and produce more within mysef, and eventually I found my way here!
I've made a lot of purchases I am really excited about, one of which is a magnetico which arrives Saturday morning! (super excited about that puppy!) And these lovely blue blocking glasses I am in love with!
Anyways, I have the epi paleo book, among 20 other books, I've been watching podcasts with Jack Kruse for many hours a day.. and I'm a little overwhelmed by it all.
I was hoping someone could maybe point me in the direction of how to proceed.
I live in Minnesota, in the City, I work from 6am-2pm, but I can take breaks pretty much whenever I want as long as it doesn't exceed 30mns, so when the sun is out, I have been trying to get some of it as best I can...
Anyways, I guess I was wondering if anyone from my general area would like to give me some dieting tips, some ways to get around the fact it's almost always cloudy.. that type of thing.
I work in an RG Cooler, so I was thinking of trying to use that to my advantage and try to survive in there in a tshirt and shorts in an attempt to get some CT going..
I'm going to cut this short for now so I can focus my shorts a little more concisely later, thanks for listening!
I'm very excited to continue to grow and learn, I feel infectiously positive, and everyone in my life can tell..
I know this is the right thing for me to be doing, and my motivation levels hasn't been so high in forever!

Have you watched Jack's Vermont talks? These are particularly gold!

Have you thought of moving? You need sun! at least are you able to go to someplace like the Yucatan several times a year?

Jack is all about the three legged stool ...... water, light and magnetism

enjoy!
