# Thread: BiuTze's OJ!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biutzes-oj.20691/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BiuTze — 2018-01-12T19:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biutzes-oj.20691/post-232356>

I'm not too sure where to start, so I'll just copy some of the questions I was asked in another thread to start.
Longitude/Latitude: 44.9537° N, 93.0900° W
Altitude: 702 feet
I'm 31 years old, 155lbs, 5"11, I'm pretty fit, been doing martial arts for 10 years, although I've been out for 7 months with an SI joint related injury, hoping to get back at it within a few months.
I've been drinking RO water for the past 15 years, only within the past 4-5 years have I been using trace minerals in that water occasionally.
I recently got a water filtration system from myzazen, a company in Austrailia. (And a shower filter to reduce chlorine and flouride). I got some DDW I've been adding into this water for past 2 weeks, 1 oz per liter. Man does it taste yummy! (I've also been putting it on my receding hair line and I think it's making a difference!)
I've been working at an organic co-op for 5 years now, I eat a fair bit of meat, with lots of pasture butter, veggies and fruit.. and probably too much pizza and quesodillas.
As far as meat goes I used to mainly eat steak and chicken, but have been eating a lot more fish as of late, trying to get some of that DHA back in my system.
I've been rocking blueblockers for about a week now, and I LOVE THEM! Sleep has been my biggest battle for most of my life, and these puppies definitely made some improvement.
I got a magnetico coming today in hopes that would further assist.
I was grounding on a mat for about 3 weeks, until I recently heard that EMFs could made that a dangerous venture. I've been wearing my leather sole shoes to go outside in the cold instead this past week, it's definitely a lot more potent.
I asked my mom to get some of her and her mom's health info for me, so that'll be coming shortly!
I'm not sure how welcome previous drug abuse habits are here, but it seems worth mentioning, when I got away from that life style that was my biggest driving force to becoming healthy again, I did a number on my brain.. formulating proper sentences was VERY difficult for a good 6 month stretch, but I think it was more the inability to properly sleep that was causing a majority of that.
Please help me know what else I can add to be thorough, and whether or not I should get a little more in depth with the above mentioned drug abuse.
Or a good direction to move towards, I feel pulled by 30 different potential paths as to what would be the best thing for me to direct my attention towards currently.
Thanks for reading!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biutzes-oj.20691/post-232360>

### JanSz — 2018-01-13T20:13:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biutzes-oj.20691/post-232452>

JanSz said: [↑](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?goto/post&id=232398#post-232398)
I got some DDW I've been adding into this water for past 2 weeks, **1 oz per liter.** Man does it taste yummy! (I've also been putting it on my receding hair line and I think it's making a difference!)
\===============

This DDW looks suspicious.

......

> [BiuTze said:](/goto/post?id=232446)
>
> It could be... It's coming from John Ellis... I've really enjoyed it thus far...

Just think about,
assume that John Ellis was able to make water that is 100% clean of any deuterium.
So far nobody was able to do it, but forget about that detail.
When you take one liter of tap water you are dealing with say optimistically 150ppm water.
Now
you are increasing volume of that water by 1 oz without increasing deuterium content.
So now you have a mixture of volume of (1 liter plus 1 oz) that overall have 150ppm

1 oz =0.0295735 liters

that mixture have less ppm's , it is
150/(1+0.0295735)=145.6914 ppm

Ok it is less than in original water.

Keep in mind that you can get Aquafina by Pepsi have 139.5ppm

In case you wonder that is very big difference.

///

I've been adding it into my alkaline water, to energize it so to speak, as per his instructions as ways one may ingest it.

# Thread: Taiyang's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-01-13T13:30:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-232408>

i post less now that i am having more success and because i need to reduce my screen time. i hope to record important changes here and interact with members on the weekends only. this post is super long because i mainly wrote it for myself, not with members in mind. but i always appreciate any thoughts you have- that's why i love the forum. i get new ideas and maybe get misconceptions corrected.

with a few blips here and there, my sleep, and thus my cognitive function, has continued to be greatly improved compared to the days before i paid attention to artificial light. i just got my daytime yellow-tint blue blockers from matt maruca's company ra optics, and i love them. so much cheaper than what i was getting before with my regular prescription glasses and all the extra cost coatings i bought. now i just need to be careful i don't scratch them up because they have no scratch-resistant coating on them. i have worn glasses since i was 5 years old and every time i go to the eye doctor my vision has shown deterioration. so my lenses are coke bottle-like on the edges. i have astigmatism too. i used to wear a patch over one eye for 'lazy eye' to make the other eye stronger. i just remember it was itchy and uncomfortable. protecting my eyes and getting better sleep/brain function has driven me to be what is seen by my family members as coo coo.

on the coo coo note, i am doing more to live in a darkened environment when the sun is not out. before i found this site, i already had completely blacked out my bedroom to the point that i literally cannot see my hand in front of my face. that one single step is what has improved my sleep the most. my whole body relaxes in the darkness when i go into my blacked out bedroom at night. i love the dark the way wim hoff loves the cold. when i have had some bad sleeping nights, it seems the biggest factor is temperature. i need to make sure my family has turned the thermostat down at night to as low as they can stand it, which for them is 65 degrees (i have a sticky note by the thermostat to remind them because i usually go to sleep at 7 30 to 8 30 in the winter, way earlier than they do). on top of that i have to have my window open at least a crack even if it is 9 degrees out. so an important factor might not just be temperature- it might also be the moisture in fresh air and the fresh air itself as compared to the stale, dry air in my bedroom. another important thing i did in my sleeping environment is i permanently turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom (my husband sleeps in a different bedroom because i sleep better that way). this change has been associated with better morning fasted blood sugar, even taking into account inaccuracies in glucose meters, and no hip pain or muscle twitches, which i used to get very occasionally in the middle of the night, but did not know what to do about. i have also been doing other lifestyle changes, so i can't specifically say turning off the circuit breaker has definitely been key to the hip pain/muscle twitches. but i can say that it has had a strong correlation with better morning faster blood sugar based on my daily tracking.

i have also recently started to just do more in the dark. i am very careful not to bump into things. i was already used to going up the stairs, brushing my teeth, and into my bedroom in the dark, but now i also take my morning shower in the semi-dark. a couple of years ago i noticed that i would have really bad hot flashes if i took a shower at night. i like to take really hot showers and the lights in the bathroom are really bright, so i think these two factors were linked to having bad hot flashes (i believe hot flashes and bad sleep are related do low melatonin and low precursor serotonin. artificial light destroys melatonin, and melatonin/serotonin play roles in temperature regulation. estrogen is needed to make serotonin, and my estrogen is declining due to menopause, leading to lower serotonin and melatonin). after seeing the connection, i never take showers in the evening. in the winter i have to get up before the sun for my job. knowing what a big factor taking a shower under bright light was in my hot flashes, i decided to try to take my morning shower with no lights on. we have a row of block glass at the top of the shower stall. i go in right when twilight is beginning so i have a very little natural light to help me. i am getting good at doing everything in the semi-dark, feeling around using the texture of things as a guide, being patient with myself when i drop stuff.

i also started to employ the 'gangsta' look when i ride the train to work. right now it is still fairly dark when i get on the train, and the interior lights of the train are very bright. i noticed a lot of the other riders (not business-suit people) had a stye of baseball cap with a hoodie pulled over it, which they kept on in the train. i was thinking maybe it was not just a style- maybe they were also having issues with the bright lights in the train. well, anyway, i started to mimic them and wear my yellow tint glasses with baseball cap, hood over the cap, and a coat over my hoodie for when it is really cold out. another cool thing i noticed is i'm more tolerant of the cold. i can sometimes be comfortable just in a hoodie at temperature when the other riders on the train need a big winter coat.

so the preceding was my morning routine. i have also changed things at work. i work at an elementary school in the morning and a middle school in the afternoon. the elementary school has just about no windows- nice design-not. it reminds me of the state prison where they just have those little slit windows, although it of course is not that bad...so anyway, lots of artificial light in the elementary school. so my yellow tint glasses are on. when i go outside for any reason, the glasses are off and i tolerate extreme blurriness because of my poor vision. in the car i crack the windows to get any stray sun rays, and i switch to my regular no tint glasses, which i keep on in the middle school since i don't turn on the lights in my room. the middle school actually had a genius who designed the building with lots of windows, so i don't need the yellow-tint to block out artificial blue light.

at the elementary school i try to protect my young students. i got ideas from listing to an emf warriors podcast. we are monitored by our supervisor on exactly how many minutes our young students are on the computer using a reading program. the reading program is excellent, but exposing these children to this much nnemf and blue light is disgusting. i have tried to mitigate this as much as possible without being accused of insubordination- ie for not reaching weekly goal minutes on the laptop using the reading program (which i was admonished for before when i didn't reach goal minutes). students all use wireless laptops, but there are 4 wired computer in the school library. when the librarian is not having class, i can get to use those wired computers with the children about 3 days out of the week (I have small groups). the other one or two days, i have to use wireless laptops. but instead of using the touch pad as i used to do and everyone else does, i hooked up a mouse and place the laptop as far away from the student as i can. with the touch pad, the screen is right in their face, so the mouse helps a little with radiation exposure i believe. some kids don't like to use the mouse, but i make them. i say it is healthier and then go into a spiel where i say the touch pad is all germy and they can get sick from it, but nobody uses the mouse, so it is cleaner (all true). the kindergartners get about 10 minutes/day on the laptop with me for 4 days. they get bored of being on any longer anyway. some of the students have a program generated goal of 80 minutes a week on the laptop using the reading program. that's on top of laptop use in their regular class (i pull them out for 30minutes a day for my class). originally our supervisor said to do a minimum of 40 minutes a week, but many teachers are following the program generated goal and doing much more (and the kids don't like it they say). we had a department meeting yesterday. with 4 teachers i am close with i talked about how other countries are realizing the dangers of wireless and nnemf's. i told them about what i am doing to reduce the children's exposure. one of the teachers is pregnant, and i was going over some things she could do. i told her to really try. and if her husband wasn't on board with it, to be sneaky (like me). i told her to turn off wireless at night, turn her cell phone all the way off, not have blue tooth on when it was on, keep it in air plane mode if she absolutely wanted to have it on when she wasn't using it, to use wired connections including keyboard and mouse whenever possible etc. i said to not have that cell phone on her body. i keep mine in my rolling bookbag. the thing is using laptops with the kids reduces teacher stress a lot if the kids like the program they are doing. so there is a temptation to over use laptops. but i am not giving in to that temptation. so i have started educating 4 teachers who know and respect me. i want to go further, however.

### taiyang — 2018-01-16T13:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-232654>

I still can't believe how good my sleep has been. Sometimes i might wake up in the middle of the night in a more awake state, but i always relax and get right back to sleep. and i am having happy dreams. i think that is a sign of more serotonin and thus more melatonin. before when i would take 5 htp to increase my serotonin, i would often immediately have good dreams that might even make me laugh out loud. unfortunately, the 5 htp wasn't a long term option since it didn't work consistently. now i never use it because i don't want to throw off my endogenous production as jack has alerted us could happen. this past weekend i wasn't getting a lot of sun since it was so cold, but i still made sure i went out in the early morning and evening. even that little bit helped so much. now i have two new outside habits in addition to my good bike riding and walking habits- i play with the doggie in the back yard with glasses off and i take a tool that is like a grasping hook to go clean up my yard and the field/playground area across from my house with glasses off. i figured i might as well do something useful while i get sun. i look for trash then look up to get sun then pick up trash. it's great!!!!!!!!!

### taiyang — 2018-01-28T14:47:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-233665>

well, my methods of sleeping better are occasionally confirmed when i deviate from them. when i am not protecting my skin and eyes from blue light at night because i am under a time constraint and need to work at the computer in the evening, i pay for it with disturbed sleep. this just reinforces to me that i have to plan ahead so that i don't put myself in those kinds of situations. for those of you familiar with lyle mcdonald, he just published on his site a massive book dealing with women's health. i just ordered it. this is the message i left him:
order placed- order info printed out and on fridge!!! early birthday present Lyle, do you address the effects of artifical blue light on the body?? if not, it might be interesting to look into the effects of artificial blue light and other forms of technology specifically on women's bodies. research might be sparse, though.
\-my ultimate solution for reducing/eliminating insomnia, hot flashes and headaches at night came not from any exogenous source (like 5 htp or melatonin which worked inconsistently for me, or hormones which i have never taken due to family history of breast cancer). i simply had to subtract as much as possible artificial blue light from my environment after the sun set. this topic is picking up steam and i see references to it everywhere. in my case it works. i have the uvex blue blockers on after sunset. but i used to do that and that in and of itself was not good enough. i even have to pay attention to the artificial lighting in my house. my husband was going crazy switching every light bulb to LED energy-saving ones. i think it is that light that was really disturbing my sleep, not to mention screens from cell phones and computers. and the f.lux program wasn't enought to help with sleep. i can sleep if i stop all screens at 5:30 pm (in fall/winter) except for a tv screen about 15 feet from screen to my head (i actually measured with a tape measure). when i watch tv from that distance, i need uvex blue blockers on and almost no lights on in that room. i can maybe get away with a light on in an adjoining room. so sleeping has led to my short term memory improving while it previously had been in the toilet. at night i act like someone at the beach who always gets a bad sunburn. i protect my eyes and my skin. i walk around with a fishing hat on at night. you may laugh, but i rather that then people laughing at me because i am repeating something i just told them but had forgotten. just a thought about a possible topic for your book if you haven't already covered it.

### taiyang — 2018-02-17T23:55:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-235020>

last night i had a bad sleeping night and i think i know why. one of my family members wanted me to spend time with them with the light on in the room when it is normally is my lights out time- latest 7pm. so i just put on my fishing hat and uvex over yellow blue blockers and hoped that would be enough. i still got sleepy around my normal time- 8:30 pm, and i still fell asleep easily as i always do. the problem is i wake up in the middle of the night and can't go back to sleep. i give it a good hour to try to fall back asleep, and then i take melatonin. so what i learned, or actually was reminded of, is that i need all lights out except for a tv screen at a distance and the blue blockers on. if a family member wants to spend time with me in these winter months in the evening, it will have to be under these conditions because i need to not lose my mind. and insomnia will absolutely cause that to happen. exogenous melatonin does not work consistently. i saw a lot of robins today- it was around 40 degrees but the temperature is supposed to fall pretty drastically. i hope the birdies can adapt.

### taiyang — 2018-03-11T12:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-236944>

i've gained a noticeable amount of weight , and it is all tied to letting do my guard with my carb intake. i felt that with better sleep and blood sugar, i didn't need to be so vigilant. after starting to eat all the things regularly that i would only give myself very occasionally- strawberries, tangerines, bananas, low carb wraps, popcorn, even regular chocolate, the weight is on me. the cravings for all those things in strong now that i've opened up the floodgates again.
besides eating low carb, the thing that really stripped me of fat back in 2009 when i first read art de vany was doing fasted exercise in the morning and then walking around for hours afterwards before eating my first low carb meal of the day, where i would eat as much as i wanted. about 3 years after following this pattern and keeping in good shape, i started to get horrible hot flashes at night, especially when doing the fasted exercise. i stopped the fasted exercise and weight came on even though i was eating low carb. but no where near the amount of weight i've now gained. yesterday i had my coffee and did my weights and some bike sprints early in the morning. i didn't eat again until 10:30 am when i started to get a little hungry, which is a lot early that i would usually eat after fasted exercise, but i wanted to ease into this. my first meal was boiled asparagus and mushrooms, olive oil, salt, pepper and parmesan cheese. then i ate again at 11:30 am with a ham and cheese low carb wrap, mustard, a tangerine, celery probably some other stuff. i didn't binge out later on it the day, which was a good sign. the most important things, though, were that my mood was good during the day and i had a good night's sleep. this is so important for me because i feel that i can do effective exercise and sleep well at the same time!!! and the other great thing is that my motivation to lose weight and feel good is based on wanting to feel good as i move through the world. it is not at all based on wanting others to see me as looking good. this viewpoint allows me to take my time, not rush, just be in a good, stable mood.
i am looking forward to the month of april when i will go in the backyard in my tan through swimsuit and begin to work on my solar callous. i have multiple moles, but i get checked every year and there is no skin cancer in the members of my family who also have a lot of moles. i will develop my callous slowly, however. i plan to go out the very first day of april and lay on each side only 5 minutes. i have become more cold tolerant, so this would have been very uncomfortable before, but now should be much less so. i will increase my minutes in the sun based on how my skin reacts as seen the following day. i have read that one needs to protect certain parts when wearing tan through swim wear, but i feel if i go slowly starting in april and tan in the mornings only at first, i should be ok. since i am going so slow, my body will give me an early warning if i am over doing it. i can't wait for april- it's almost here!!!

### taiyang — 2018-03-16T12:37:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-237283>

i have a very good morning routine compared to what i used to do. i get at least 5 minutes of twilight before i get on the train. then i get my car in the train lot and drive to a nice spot to get morning sunlight for about 20 minutes. i do work stuff, occasionally glancing up. my eye lids are thin, and i believe i get a lot of benefit from the sun's rays even when i am looking down at my work with my face towards the sun.

but even more more more important than morning sun for me is avoiding artificial light. yesterday i had great morning sun, but i didn't have my yellow glasses for wearing in my windowless work area or for riding the train. then i had to stay up until 9 pm without my uvex because of work (i need to order some amber/red blue blockers from matt!). i had an awful sleep last night. i woke up and just lay there for a long long time. finally i cracked the window to get cold air (i usually do this anyway) and took melatonin. then i was finally able to get to sleep.

i find the cold winter air so therapeutic at night. i breathe it into my lungs with the cozy covers on, and it cools down my whole body. it cools down the pillow so that when i turn over, i have a nice cool area to further lower my temperature.

### taiyang — 2018-03-26T13:31:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-237993>

i am trying our iris and am currently using the black and white "reading" mode- it just feels better to read this way. continuing from my last entry, i find that avoiding artificial light is more important than the amount of sun i get, remembering that i always get some sun. i can get loads of sun and still have an awful night sleeping if i am exposed to our stupid led lights in the house for a length of time, even trying to be covered up. now that it is getting light later, i am trying to exclude light earlier. at 7:30 there is still some light out, but if i am watching tv, i still put on the uvex. at 8 or 8:30 even though i might not be as sleepy, i go right up to my fridge (aka my bedroom), crack the window to let the cold air, and go to sleep. my temperature regulation is tied to my sleep quality and is never perfect, but getting to bed earlier and being exposed less to any artificial light helps my temperature regulation.

i think there are certain types of people who are canaries in the coal mine when it come to being highly sensitive to blue light- anyone with problems producing serotonin or dopamine. these people might just think that their diagnosed condition is the reason they have problems with sleep or with addictive behaviors and might not realize how blue light makes it worse. i have personally seen that people with defects in their serotonin transporters and people diagnosed with ADHD will have problems with blue light more so than others. in the future, these problems with sleeping and addiction will just spread to more of the population. i had heard of the problems with screens, but only through coming to this page did i learn about LED lighting- which my husband is obsessed with.

### taiyang — 2018-03-31T15:22:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-238405>

[http://www.fergon.com/iron-deficiency-and-menopause/](http://www.fergon.com/iron-deficiency-and-menopause/)
"Once a woman reaches menopause, a lack of iron can lead to many other conditions that are uncomfortable, embarrassing, and even degenerative. Some studies have linked iron to hot flashes, and people with iron deficiencies often report poor cold intolerance and body temperature regulation...Women can have an increased menstrual blood flow during the time leading up to menopause and even during menopause. This means that more iron is being released through the blood and leaving the body deficient of iron."

i have erratic bleeding now that i am closer to menopause. i have been diagnosed with anemia off and on for years. my ob/gyn and family doctor refuse to monitor it saying a yearly test is good enough. i am flying blind trying to supplement iron.

recently i have had a heavy period. preceding this period, my sleep has been fair. however, now the past couple days my hot flashes have returned. usually an aleve at night will help reduce my temperature and whatever else it does to allow me to get back to sleep and to feel fairly well rested in the morning. the night before last even the aleve didn't work. so my feeling is my iron has dipped to the point where i am not producing sufficient serotonin->melatonin. iron is needed in the production of serotonin. yesterday i took one iron pill, and even though last night i did have hot flashes, there onset wasn't unit 4 am, an improvement from the night before. today i will take 2 iron pills- morning and evening- and see if my sleep continues to improve. i will also get lots of sun today and eat shrimp. sometimes daytime stress can bring on hot flashes at night- maybe because stress affects serotonin production- but i am not feeling any stress right now as i am on vacation. so i will continue with my iron supplementation in addition to all the normal things i do to get a good night's sleep. i just wish that i had a better way to monitor my iron. right now i use subjective feelings like sore tongue and hot flashes/sleep quality.

### taiyang — 2018-04-01T16:57:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-238454>

thank you, jan. i think i may have tracked down the latest cause of my heavy period. i was eating lots of almonds and peppermint chewing gum, which have a higher relative level of salicylates in them. so that combined with perimenopause and my other underlying factors predisposing me to heavy periods i think has precipitated this latest round. cutting way back on my intake of salicylates should help. i could never take fish oil because its blood thinning effect would also cause me heavy periods. i will wait 8 hours for the aleve that i took this morning to clear my system and if my heavy period doesn't stop by then, i will take my prescribed hormone pills (norethindrone 5 mg) from the last time i had a really bad incident of bleeding. if i get dizzy, i will go to the ER where they will probably give me a shot of Lupron- but i doubt it will get to that point. The Aleve isn't working as well as it usually does, however. thankfully, i am off today. happy spring and assorted other holidays!!

### JanSz — 2018-04-01T17:02:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taiyangs-journal.20697/post-238455>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=238454)

# Thread: cheap blue blockers for elementary schools
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-01-13T13:50:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232409>

laptops are being used more and more in the elementary schools. kids there are not as concerned about peers as the upper grades, so i thought it might be a good place to start with them wearing cheap blue blockers when they are on laptops. i'm thinking of something like the cheapie 'glasses' handed out for free when we had the solar eclipse. couldn't they be produced and sold in bulk to schools? do these type of glasses already exist? maybe it could start out with teachers handing them out to students with attention problems (which is becoming a greater portion of the student population based on my experience). if teachers saw that the kids were better rested, more focused, acting out less, you can bet that teachers would start using and promoting them to parents like crazy. whatever reduces teacher/parent stress will catch on like wild fire.

### shiran — 2018-01-13T15:35:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232412>

The nnemf in schools is a huge deal , it must come from the bottom , from the home the family's , it won't come from the top of the pyramid, to much ignorance. I totally agree with your premise . We should lecture and spread this knowledge out to as much parents as possible to as much pregnant woman , teenagers

### taiyang — 2018-01-13T17:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232424>

shiran, isn't it true that in your country of israel there are restrictions on use of wireless in the schools? i'm looking for youtube clips and documentaries/news reports of how wireless use is being restricted in schools overseas. i'm going to put together a short google slides presentation with a focus on what is being done in other countries.

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-13T18:34:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232439>

We as parents need to work hard to educate each other about the dangers of blue light and nnemf. You are right, changes need to come from parents because school boards aren’t going to do it on their own. It’s going to take some work. Cheap blue blocking glasses would be amazing in schools.

I also think that wifi has no place in schools, and all internet should be connected via Ethernet. The problem is wifi is a lot cheaper, so budget conscious school boards have installed that in most schools.

My daughter has been struggling for years with hashimotos and Lyme disease. We finally got her back into school for grade 9 and she was rocking it for one month in the fall of 2016. She attended a robotics and engineering program, were there was a laptop using wifi for every student in the class. After a month of school she collapsed and hasn’t been well since. I looked at a map of cell towers and that school is surrounded by a high density of them. I’m just clueing in now about eemf dangers - thanks to finding Jack Kruse and this community. We just recently hardwired our home and disabled wifi.

Of course, the school is lit with fluorescent lights, as well.

### taiyang — 2018-01-13T18:37:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232440>

now we need to find a company that already makes cheap paper blue blockers. if these glasses don't already exist, no company will make them unless they think there is a good market for them. we also need to stop the onslaught of online testing.

### shiran — 2018-01-14T08:22:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheap-blue-blockers-for-elementary-schools.20698/post-232511>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=232424)
>
> shiran, isn't it true that in your country of israel there are restrictions on use of wireless in the schools? i'm looking for youtube clips and documentaries/news reports of how wireless use is being restricted in schools overseas. i'm going to put together a short google slides presentation with a focus on what is being done in other countries.

Unfortunately our country is one of the most bombarded in nnemf due to military reasons and the Israelis are pioneers in the tec industry and addicted to tec , so do you think the government cares about emf in schools or fluoride in our water? The blue light is not in their top concern at all. There are some regulations in kindergarten regarding wireless but as the kids grow at schools it worsens . In my kids schools they use cord internet and wireless wifi with some regulations I can check for you the exact program for that. What I did in my sons class regarding the blue light in cooperation with the teacher, as much as she can use sunlight as here main lighting and we covered the fluorescent with a blue blocking, I think the blue blockers for all the kids is a great idea I'm not sure about the Responsiveness of the kids if it dost come from home..it will be a challenge

# Thread: Hi From UK

### Michael CULLEN — 2018-01-13T15:12:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-uk.20699/post-232411>

Hello everyone.

I am in the UK, and really dislike it right now. I try not to hate anything but I want to bloody move!

I have followed Jack's work for 3 months now, and read his book last weekend. I have an EMF meter, I have Truedark glasses. to wear at night I never go on a laptop without blue blocking glasses. I only drink spring water. I am doing my very best, but it is immensely difficult when you can barely move.

Unfortunately my family have had a sudden and tragic death, this has caused significant financial lost, thus we are not in a position to move right now, but we all talk about it.

I was in Lanzarote at Xmas. Detached villa, low nnemf environment, sun every AM(in moderation as I have sudomotor failure), and drinking high silica Gran Canaria spring water. I felt pretty good. Now I feel terrible just 2 weeks later. I mean almost about to give up terrible. I am sick of struggling with my health. I have tried so hard for 15 years to get well. One day I hope to be in the south of Spain, Malaga where I was actually born...........

I will elaborate more on another thread(optimal journey).

God bless and thank you Jack. I wish I had known more a year ago, and I might have been able to save my father. Ignorance is only blissful for a period, then it can lead to death being your acquisition.

Mike

### caroline — 2018-01-14T00:31:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-uk.20699/post-232474>

Hi Mike ......and welcome.

Please do start an optimal journal and give Dr. K. more health info .....and your Mom's and Gran's too.

Baby steps on this journey to optimal health.

Have you watched Jack's two Vermont talks?

the recent youtube event with Jack and Jeremy?

Jack's talk with Nathan from Mexico on new Year's Eve?

Please use the three legged stool as your guiding principle ....... light, water and Magnetism

### Michael CULLEN — 2018-01-14T14:18:47+0200

Hi

Yes I saw those talks! I watched the talk with Nathan on Thursday and told my family I want to go to Playa Del Carmen. I actually started looking at villas out of interest. It is inspiring to know with the right environment I could improve way beyond what I am now. Unfortunately with my fathers passing it means less to no holidays for awhile. He was due to retire this year, and had a fantastic private pension, which now my mother will only receive 1/6 off. Very F'd up. Lanzarote was booked whilst he was alive, and he was supposed to be going with us! You work all your life for nothing in some cases. I do at least have a sister who is a nurse.

I guess one has to remain positive, I do try, but I do agree with Jack that you can not heal in the UK. I awake to darkness, not ideal. That is a big frustration for me because I can not pick holes in what Jack says. It is the truth. I have read a lot of the studies about UV light and know how beneficial AM sun is. That being said I was doing fantastic in 2015 in this shitty environment by supporting redox with Idebenone. I messed my whole system up and can not tolerate this now. It is the synthetic version of Coq10, but stable under low oxygen environments. That being said you have to be able to reduce it to Idebenol in order for it to be a cellular antioxidant. I was actually walking around the block then. I was focused on supplements only at the time. Now I see the bigger picture, but have accidentally caused some damage this year(heavy metals being released from cells etc).

REDOX! I understood this way before I found Dr Jack, because of Dr Paul Cheney. I just did not know how sunlight effects it. Dr Cheney had a clinic for CFS patients. I will talk about his methods more on other threads because it is relevant to Jack's work, and the testing he did could be used by other doctors.

I do have 23andme data

Thanks

### Dottie — 2018-01-14T02:11:42+0200

What are your thoughts on prenatal vitamins if a woman isnt sure she is getting enough DHA?

I try to eat alot of seafood, but I know I am not getting enough DHA. I am currently trying to concieve. Doctors are so pushy about prenatals, that I am becoming fearful if I am doing the right/wrong things. What are your thoughts?

### JanSz — 2018-01-14T17:49:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prenatal-vitamins.20702/post-232528>

trying to conceive is a good thing, wishing you both success
since
you asking this question, I think you are trying it already for some time.

When you say: Doctors are so pushy about prenatals
I think:

what is the content of them
how did they, the doctors, have determined that you are missing it?

how do you know that you do not have enough DHA?
have you tested and it came low?
What about other things in that category?
That category must be in balance (you know).

So,
my position is that you should maximize doing good stuff the natural way
then
test and see what you are missing
then
keep doing good stuff, add modifications for your shortcomings and also add something that you know that you are missing.
\------------------------------------------
Natural stuff:
Live according to good Circadian Rhythm
be naked (without anything sunscreens, moisturizers or eye cover), and be active under the sun.
do not use artificial lights indoor, red is ok, sleep in totally dark room.
do not change hours, ie. do not travel over time zones
at any time you can check your status doing this tests
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6)

but pay special attention to this line:
22 ------- Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

# Thread: Connected movement in nature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2018-01-15T13:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-232593>

How do you guys feel when moving in nature? I there a difference if you are grounded or not, if you have you solar panel covered or not?
Yesterday I felt this weird presence of something guiding me. We were just short time outside at 0°C barefoot and upperbody naked in beautiful sunshine. As I would running over the grass it felt something was guiding me and movement became so instinctive and natural - fluid. We had a indoor course before with movement (lucky me not artificial lights on) and I could notive a difference right away. Completely other feeling in movement. Also if nature wants you to to do something. So strong I never felt it before.

### Inger — 2018-01-15T16:17:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-232609>

My most wonderful moving in Nature is when picking oysters naked on the ocean floor...

I feel just so in the here and now, such a joy and happiness, so perfectly happy and thankful being in the moment... I just cant describe it.... but it is as.. everything is just... perfect!
For me to be grounded and naked in nature is the best!
Somehow it shuts my brain down. I am breathing, my whole body is breathing in sync with everything.... There is no needs anymore, nothing you want or need or miss.... Pure magic
Freedom    Innocence... like a Child.. curous what beautiful looking oysters I will find the next, what crabs or fishes or birds or creatures I will meet...
Barefoot, connected, little or no clothes.. in the wild... That is feeling like... coming home
I am alone, but I do not feel alone, not one bit!

### Christine_L — 2019-03-25T21:35:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266420>

I havent seen the new series! I am using the Youtube videos for now but want to take an in person class for tips and pointers. I feel like a damn turtle trying to get up from the low squat to standing!

### LieselK — 2019-03-25T22:49:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266429>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=266420)
>
> I havent seen the new series! I am using the Youtube videos for now but want to take an in person class for tips and pointers. I feel like a damn turtle trying to get up from the low squat to standing!

You may like the BirthFIt videos. My chiro is the regional director in NC and she has some great videos on it. I do the functional progression outside when I think of it. It mimics how babies build the correct muscles to go from laying to standing. Super cool stuff especially because women are not supporting their cores properly for uterine strength and pelvic health. The functional breathing and just the first couple of movements have been all I've worked on for months and it's astounding at the difference in my core strength! I think a lot of the other types of fitness regimes do not honor womens bodies well- pre, during and post pregnancy. It's very empowering to embrace your body as a women and also build strength! I sometimes do heavy lifts too, but outdoor movement and BirthFit is a great foundation.

### Christine_L — 2019-03-25T23:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266439>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=266429)
>
> I think a lot of the other types of fitness regimes do not honor womens bodies well- pre, during and post pregnancy. It's very empowering to embrace your body as a women and also build strength! I sometimes do heavy lifts too, but outdoor movement and BirthFit is a great foundation.

I totally agree! Will check out BirthFit, thanks for suggesting!

### Karen & Glen C. — 2019-03-25T23:51:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/connected-movement-in-nature.20706/post-266443>

My take on natural movement is 'your way' up to your limits and keep going.
I teach martial arts and the weirdest thing I see on the net is male instructors teaching women how to move like a male.

Its rediculous and completely takes away from the power of natural in motion.

Yeah sure take some tips and ideas but in the end its really just your gender and nature... 'shin-do' (let nature guide you).

"Letting go isn't scary. Holding on is".

### JanSz — 2018-01-15T14:50:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-232600>

get it up to 690

### taiyang — 2018-01-15T14:59:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-232601>

i will only comment on what i have researched. i'm not a doctor or a nurse. a 97 fasting blood glucose is not officially pre diabetic- you need to be at 100-125 for that (126 is the cut-off set for diabetes/ cut-offs for the US), but 97 is on the high side. i'm low carb, so my triglycerides are in the 30s. my fasting insulin is also 3. so the question in my mind is why do you have what looks like nice low insulin (the same as mine-3) but relatively high triglycerides and fasting morning blood sugar (not out of range, maybe just high for my case)? i'm not saying my following comments apply to you- blood sugar control issues are not just limited to insulin resistance; they can also be related to insulin insufficiency, or not producing enough insulin for the particular load of carbs that is suitable for your heritage. there is a snp (single nucleotide polymorphism) of a gene that in studies has been most associated with the development of type 2 diabetes: TCF7L2. It has a a common polymorphism. i think 33% of europeans have it. further, the mechanism involves insulin insufficiency, not insulin resistance. i have that snp. i can high relatively high blood sugar but low triglycerides and a low fasting insulin. maybe you also have that snp? if you get genetic analysis like 23andme, you will know. my morning fasted blood sugar really improved a lot even with eating more carbs, when i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom(if you can't do that, try unplugging everything in your bedroom before you sleep, plus unplug anything in other rooms that share a wall with your bedroom might drop your morning blood sugar). if i were you i would get a glucose meter and strips and start testing. here in the US i can buy them relatively cheaply from Wal mart and i don't have a prescription.

### Antonis — 2018-01-15T15:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-232605>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=232601)

> i will only comment on what i have researched. i'm not a doctor or a nurse. a 97 fasting blood glucose is not officially pre diabetic- you need to be at 100-125 for that (126 is the cut-off set for diabetes/ cut-offs for the US), but 97 is on the high side. i'm low carb, so my triglycerides are in the 30s. my fasting insulin is also 3. so the question in my mind is why do you have what looks like nice low insulin (the same as mine-3) but relatively high triglycerides and fasting morning blood sugar (not out of range, maybe just high for my case)? i'm not saying my following comments apply to you- blood sugar control issues are not just limited to insulin resistance; they can also be related to insulin insufficiency, or not producing enough insulin for the particular load of carbs that is suitable for your heritage. there is a snp (single nucleotide polymorphism) of a gene that in studies has been most associated with the development of type 2 diabetes: TCF7L2. It has a a common polymorphism. i think 33% of europeans have it. further, the mechanism involves insulin insufficiency, not insulin resistance. i have that snp. i can high relatively high blood sugar but low triglycerides and a low fasting insulin. maybe you also have that snp? if you get genetic analysis like 23andme, you will know. my morning fasted blood sugar really improved a lot even with eating more carbs, when i turned off the circuit breaker to my bedroom(if you can't do that, try unplugging everything in your bedroom before you sleep, plus unplug anything in other rooms that share a wall with your bedroom might drop your morning blood sugar). if i were you i would get a glucose meter and strips and start testing. here in the US i can buy them relatively cheaply from Wal mart and i don't have a prescription.

Thank you btw. I will test my dna soon. I want to know where I can live optimally. The glucos was tested one month ago. Before that day i had some carbs plus ketogenic deserts with sugar alternative. Now I cutt of carbs cuz it is winter but still sunny.

### Antonis — 2018-01-16T10:11:16+0200

### taiyang — 2018-01-16T13:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-232653>

That's super. We have to keep in mind, however, that even the best meters (sold in the US) only are required to be accurate with 15 points. So a 92 could really be 92 + 15= 107 or 92-15= 77. Also, make sure to have good habits when testing. I always wash my hands to make sure I don't have any little bit of food on them, which would make a higher blood sugar reading. Then I wipe away the first drop of blood and test the second drop. If I get a strange reading, I test again. I use a lancer (needle holder) made by accucheck which hurts a lot less, and I prick the sides of my pinkie finger most of the time, so it doesn't really hurt. Lastly, never share your lancer (needle thingy) with others because of the risk of transmitting bloodborne diseases. Did you unplug everything in your bedroom before you went to sleep? Why do you think you got a lower reading?

### Antonis — 2018-01-16T13:43:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-232655>

I use a lancer (needle holder) made by accucheck which hurts a lot less, and I prick the sides of my pinkie finger most of the time, so it doesn't really hurt. Lastly, never share your lancer (needle thingy) with others because of the risk of transmitting bloodborne diseases. Did you unplug everything in your bedroom before you went to sleep? Why do you think you got a lower reading?

No worries taiyang we don't have any issues here in Cyprus. The results are right and the range is (75-110) I did not eat or drink anything 12 hours before testing. First time I did the same thing and it was higher glucose. I definitely need a glucose meter to check every time.

### taiyang — 2018-01-27T18:57:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-233638>

I asked my diabetic group as I think c-peptide is used as a first step to screen for problems with insulin secretion- ie not enough insulin being produced. this is the response i got:

**"Your reading of 157 pmol/L is low.**

I believe this suggests a diagnosis of type 1 diabetes. I am not a doctor and this is a doctor’s call. As [@Tim35](https://forum.tudiabetes.org/u/tim35)said, you will need to discuss this with your doctor."

Tim said to go screen for tests for the antibodies associated with Type 1 diabetes. I would say that in my case i am not type 1 (based on symptoms, but i never had a c peptide or antibodies test done), but seem to have insufficient insulin to handle carb meals that are fine for my husband. so the short term strategy is to do what you are doing and 1) eat healthy low carb. then continue to test meals with your glucose meter- test one to two hours after a meal (some meals like pizza will show spikes in bg hours after words, though). i'm not trying to alarm you. maybe you caught a potential problem early so you should be happy. that's how i feel in my situation anyway.

>

I would say that in my case i am not type 1 (based on symptoms, but i never had a c peptide or antibodies test done), but seem to have insufficient insulin to handle carb meals that are fine for my husband. so the short term strategy is to do what you are doing and 1) eat healthy low carb. then continue to test meals with your glucose meter- test one to two hours after a meal (some meals like pizza will show spikes in bg hours after words, though). i'm not trying to alarm you. maybe you caught a potential problem early so you should be happy. that's how i feel in my situation anyway.

### taiyang — 2018-01-28T15:09:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-233667>

another diabetic friend said: "Marginally low reading with normal fasting glucose level not necessarily relevant, though. Would be a smoking gun if high fasting glucose levels and low c-peptide. Doctor would have to evaluate it in context of her overall history." To him, since your fasting blood glucose was normal and not high, that low c-peptide might be nothing. Still, he says you need a good doctor to look at your overall history. You might just be like me- we need to eat low carb to be healthy, but we aren't in danger of becoming type one. that's how i am living my life anyway. and i still monitor my blood sugar at least every morning. if it starts to trend up, i will monitor more. so i am not always sticking myself with a needle.

### DrStephane — 2018-09-23T00:19:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-recent-results-need-some-guidance.20707/post-251550>

This forum or any online venture is not a place to diagnose or practice medicine, however here is my 2 cents base on Functional Medicine values.

1.  TSH 0.876 mIU/L (0.350 - 4.940) **LOW**

    Free T4 14.61 pmol/L (9.00 - 19.05) **HIGH**

    Free T3 3.67 pmol/L (2.63 - 5.70)

    Prolactin 65.3 mIU/L (72.66 - 407.4)

    Insulin 3.0 μIU/ml (2.0 - 20.0)

    VitaminB12 1236 pg/mL (187 - 883)

    Folate acid 4.20 ng/mL (3.10-20.50)

2.  Glucose 97 mg/dL (74-106)

    Uric Acid 3.5 mg/dL (3.5 - 7.2) **LOW**

    Total Cholesterol 393 mg/dL (100 - 200) **HIGH**

    HDL 78 mg/dL (35 - 65)

    LDL 303 mg/dL (60 - 150) **HIGH**

    Triglycerides 59mg/dL (50 - 150) **LOW**

    Urea 44 mg/dL (17 - 43) **HIGH**

    Creatine 0.70 mg/dL (0.67 - 1.17)

    Magnesium 0.86 mmol/L (0.73 - 1.06) **low**

    Iron 82 μg/dL (70 - 180) **LOW**

    TIBC 294 μg/dL (220 - 460)

    Ferritin 223 ng/mL (20 - 300)

    Total Proteins 7.8 g/dL (6.6 - 8.3) **HIGH**

    Albumin 5.2 g/dL (3.5 - 5.2) **HIGH**

    Globulins 2.6 g/dL (1.4 - 4.8)

    Sodium 139 mmol/L (135 - 145) **LOW**

    Potassium 4.84 mmol/L (3.50 - 5.00)

    Bicarbonate (HCO3) 30.5 mmol/L (21.0 - 31.0)

    Total Bilirubin 0.60 mg/dL (0.30 - 1.20)

    Alkaline Phosphatase 88 U/L 30 - 120 **HIGH**

    γ glutamyltransferase 19 U/L (9 - 55)

    Alkaline Aminotransferase 38 U/L (3 - 41) **HIGH**

    Aspartate Aminotransferase 35 U/L (3 - 38) **HIGH**

    Lactate Dehydrogenase 338 U/L (208 - 480) **HIGH**

    Creatine Kinase 158 U/L (26 - 171)

    IgG 988.00 mg/dL (700.00 - 1600.00)
    IgA 130.00 mg/dL (70.00 - 400.00)
    IgM 93.00 mg/dL (40.00 - 230.00)

Again, without a proper history, timeline and setting these values means nothing!

Questions:
Are you following Dr Kruse protocol? if so, did you prior this lab testing? if so, how long prior? If not, apply Dr. Kruse protocol and retest in 3 months

In case this get asked:
Preliminary recommend further testing:
CBC with differential
Vit D
Breath Test

Hint:
You have alot of Online companies that you can put your blood values and get somewhat a reading. This is a cheaper way to start prior finding a Dr. Kruse near your area.

Dr S

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-movie-for-what-doctor-kruse-talks-about.20710/post-232686>

Here is the trailer. Most of the talking is in the beginning and at the end and it isn't much talking at all. It is mostly if not all about him surviving his injuries. You have to watch it to appreciate it. You can't really explain it. I enjoyed watching how he experienced survival and life. My boyfriend was waiting for the revenge part of it. ### JohnnyAZ — 2018-01-16T23:23:07+0200

I was listening to the fourth phase of matter Q&A today and heard something that I want to see if someone could help me understand. Perhaps I am misunderstanding but it sounded as if Jack told one of the callers to go tanning and if they noticed they felt worse then they knew where an issue lied. What I think I am understanding is if there is something wrong with semiconduction the sun can make you feel sick? Can someone correct me if I am wrong and if I am not can someone help me understand this concept better or direct me to the material?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-16T23:59:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-from-sun.20711/post-232694>

On a side note President Trump's vitamin D status is 20...wow, he's rather brown for someone with relatively low D. He's also on cholesterol lowering meds..

### JohnnyAZ — 2018-01-17T00:46:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-from-sun.20711/post-232699>

Makes sense. I have been noticing that sometimes I feel worse when entering the sun than I did when in the EMF environment I just left. Perhaps this is why.

Could this also affect how red light affects you?

### JohnnyAZ — 2018-01-17T01:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-from-sun.20711/post-232702>

Actually let me ask this.

Shortly after Jack mentioned this idea in the Q&A it sounded as if he said that this issue needed to be narrowed down and that it could be protein or water related (there may have been a third thing) and he mentioned “holding water in.” Keeping water inside is a problem I have. Is this telling me something about why my redox is bad?

### brandie — 2018-01-17T22:05:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-from-sun.20711/post-232778>

> [JohnnyAZ said:](/goto/post?id=232702)
>
> Actually let me ask this.
>
> Shortly after Jack mentioned this idea in the Q&A it sounded as if he said that this issue needed to be narrowed down and that it could be protein or water related (there may have been a third thing) and he mentioned “holding water in.” Keeping water inside is a problem I have. Is this telling me something about why my redox is bad?

I think it's telling THAT your redox is bad. The why may or may not matter. Read the redox rx blog again, see what lightbulbs pop up for you. I'm pretty sure doc has said sunlight is a way to improve redox, 90% sure this is how I improved. My D was low and my immune system seemed to be affected, so my focus was upping my D.
If your cellular water is bad (chronic dehydration) then the three legged stool should be your best friend.
I don't think there's an issue with red light adversely affecting anyone. I see red light as ALWAYS good.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-an-mri-for-redox.20713/post-232748>

Does anyone know the specifics on how to read an MRI to learn about your redox potential? I know Dr. Kruse has mentioned it before. I have multiple brain MRI images on my computer from when I was in a car accident. I didn’t get a head injury, but now I realize the images could come in handy to learn about my redox.

I know the basis of MRIs is to get a picture of the light release from excited charged particles when you shoot a magnetic field at them. I don’t really know what to look for when I’m looking at my own images though. Brightness? Dark spots? The more light release the lower the redox?

If anyone knows, give me a heads up

### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-01-20T19:34:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-an-mri-for-redox.20713/post-232972>

Bump. I would like to know the answer to this as well.

Lucien - even though I don't know what exactly to look for, what I can tell you is that sometimes radiologists (the guys who read MRIs) will include verbiage in their reports that is extremely non-specific. Like "stranding" or "hyperattenuation." I haven't heard Jack mention one of these terms as a marker for inflammation in what I have listened/read of his so far - but I'd really like to. You may find your answer in the report, without having to go back and read the initial image.

I couldn't find a good answer on radiopaedia - maybe someone else can.

# Thread: Hello from Utah
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-utah.20718/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 1021km — 2018-01-18T10:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-utah.20718/post-232811>

Hi everyone!

I'm just here to soak up as much knowledge as I can in hopes that one day I can use it to help our species thrive.

Just figured I would introduce myself instead of continuing to lurk in the shadows.

My name is Ken. I have lived in Utah for most of my life, though I have done my fair share of traveling abroad. I value the pursuit of knowledge, simplistic efficiency in systems, purpose of being, and human connection.

Jack, big fan... Keep up the good work, it is appreciated by many, and many more to come. I came to this site as a result of stumbling across a podcast that you did a while back. Though I quickly found myself out of my depth, somehow your message still strongly resonated with me. I believe we know when we hear the truth, even if we don't fully comprehend it yet. Call it a remnant of our tie to the underlying matrix we affectionately deem the universe. That was all it took, I quickly searched out every podcast I could find you in, and set about learning your message. Along the way, I got the impression that there were much more intricate details that you weren't overwhelming the public with, and so I ended up here.

Because I have no specific malady that has yet manifested, I find myself without an ultimate goal to anchor and organize my efforts to learn. As such I tend to bounce around from one interesting topic to the next trying to pickup the language and syntax as I go. I find that is my biggest stumbling block. I am neither well versed in medical academia nor the realm of physics. Yet, I am inexplicably drawn to learn what is taught here. Despite its intricate complexities, the message seems simple enough in application: live like a polar bear, eat like a shark, make like the sphinx. Then, live where your DNA(s) are programmed for while mitigating nnEMF and optimizing your water quality. And as always N=1. Please let me know if I have missed, or misunderstood any of the basics. I am certainly not one who is afraid of correction. If anyone has any suggestions on how to begin eating the elephant, I am all ears. I suppose if I was forced to provide a sub-interest, it would be either longevity or optimization of my home environment.

I look forward to conversing with this great bunch of truth seekers!

# Thread: Greeting from Pittsburgh
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-pittsburgh.20721/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gregory H Hoeper — 2018-01-18T13:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-pittsburgh.20721/post-232819>

Good morning all. My name is Gregory H. Hoeper, and I am a chiropractor from the Northern Pittsburgh area. I am new to practice (just over 8 months), and have been running a functional medicine practice since the day I was licensed and graduated (in 2017). I have had great results with a majority of people (especially in pediatric cases), but it feels as though I am missing something. I use supplements, dietary and lifestyle recommendations, as well as some adjunctive therapies like LLLT, percussive instruments, as well as manual manipulation.

I have been recommending to almost all of my patients a paleolithic ketogenic diet (but didn't emphasize the seafood, which now I do), with compliance taken into account. Some people who come have not gotten to the point where they are ready to give up some foods in the winter (like carbs) and take it to the next level. I have a patient who lives in her house with no sunlight because it makes her sick, and leaves her kids in the evening to sleep in a tent due to surrounding wifi signals away from her children, so that was my first blatant emf sickness (come to find out everything is related to EMFs, magnetism, light, etc). This sn't how I ended up here though.

Anyway, I'm always reading all of the time. Forums, papers, etc trying to get to the bottom of all the issues different people are having, and it has taken me all over the map, so I will tell you how I ended up here.

1) I was visiting my sister (a chiropractor on the other side of Pennsylvania) and she came in at night and looks like she was left in the freezer. Said she drove home with the windows open the whole time for cold exposure. I have done cold exposure, but was introduces by Wim Hoff, so in the shower mostly but occasionally outside. Then she mentioned Jack Kruse and I said "whoever the hell that guy is." Exposure 1.

2) I like to learn about fitness etc., and used to follow someone called Naudi Aguilar. Now when he first started out putting up his original videos (i cam e across him in 2014), they were really great. But over time, he became very toxic, and we actually got in an argument over how the diaphragm works on facebook, and eventually it devolved into some PMs of him being wildly belligerent, aggressive, all those things (I even saved the PMs because he said I was being racist due to Trump winning ?) Anyway, so sometimes I come across his social media videos for a good laugh. One day, I went on and saw a post where he was absolutely disrespecting Dr. Kruse with name-calling and provocative statements (like a teenager). So I checked his last 5 posts on social media, and it was all about Dr. Kruse. But I asked myself a question: "Who the hell is Dr. Jack Kruse, and how did he get this guy so triggered?" Clicked Jack Kruse, say he was a neurosurgeon, say some posts, quantum biology, and said "Another EMFs are bad guy. I know that." Exposure 2.

3) So one day I am browsing "optimising nutrition" facebook group, where people argure about whether 25/50/25 or 23/70/7 or 20/80/0 is the best macronutrient ratio vs. nutrient density vs. some other arbitrary variable is most important in dealing with metabolic/gut dysbiosis/obesity/autoimmune whatever, and people are really getting heated over this. Never seen so many people battle to be the "I'm right" club president ever. And about 20 posts down, I see this comment by Dr. Jack Kruse that says "Obesity begins in the eye, I laid it all out right here in my nourishing vermont 2017 talk." And after that, there was no comments. The group admin asked "what bulbs did you use?" Jack replied, and he said thanks. That was it. The debate over carbohydrates, microflora, insulin, fiber, and protein raged on. And all these red flags went off in my head. I said to myself "Wait a minute, this random ass guy (who I have now heard of on 2 other occasions) pops in and says something radically different than everything all of you are saying, and you just completely ignore it? Wouldn't you at least watch he video, poke holes in everything he says, and continue on?" So I watch the video, and had a panic attack because I realized that everything in my life and practice was going to change. Again." Exposure 3.

So now I am starting to educate my patients on how to reduce EMF exposure, train circadian rhythm, grounding, killing the circuit breaker at night, shutting down wifi, toxic bluelight, and other implementations that I have gathered thus far. I started looking at all of my old patients labs and looked at the values a bit differently, as well as ordering different labs. And again, everything in my life changed. So thanks Dr. Jack Kruse and community for the welcome!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-18T15:33:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-pittsburgh.20721/post-232828>

Welcome.

An excellent article on nutrition and metabolic fats (their) for the treatment of diabetes, obesity and epilepsy in the journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA). The Natural Fat-marine based diet described in this article is a source of reduced deuterium metabolism in our cells (matrix), which flows in the matrix of mitochondria while using oxygen. This is also the key to health control, solving many global metabolism and cancer types as our matrix ages!

You are such a knowledgeable group, I feel privileged to be a part of it .
I wondered if anyone has any ideas how I can encourage my daughter (28yrs) to get on board with the self-help discussed on here.
She is bright and slim - so she thinks her constant carbs are not affecting her too much, she is dyslexic and has Irlens syndrome, which make me think she is especially sensitive to blue light and nnEMF. She works on a computer all day. I have at last got her to wear blue blocking glasses a few days ago, and she tells me she is using them at the right times.
Her pupils are massive most of the time, and I have not been able to explain to her the true significance of this - although I know its not good. I noticed her front teeth yesterday are becoming transparent, and know this is another important sign of impending issues.
I have been sharing what I am learning with her since I found you all in September, and some of it is encouraging her to reassess her choices.
Can you think of any key points that would be crucial to share with her to help her thought processes so she take all this more seriously?
My family all work together in a natural food shop in the UK , and we spend all day taking with people about their health issues, trying to shed some light on them, but sometimes those closest to you are the hardest to help...

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-18T22:44:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-ideas-on-how-to-help-my-daughter-get-her-head-around-all-this.20723/post-232871>

Todays PSA on the flu since it is flu season now: When you get a flu shot and adding blue light tech screens and the effect it has on raising deuterium levels (melanopsin effect) and temperature to create higher cyctokine storms in your immune responses via the NRF2 system you begin to see how link's information can happen in today's world easily. Most influenza outbreaks disproportionately kill juvenile, elderly or already weakened patients; in contrast the 1918 pandemic killed predominantly previously healthy young adults. Modern research, using virus’ taken from the bodies of frozen victims, has concluded that the virus kills through a cytokine storm (overreaction of the body’s immune system). The strong immune reactions (deuterium) of young adults ravaged the body, whereas the weaker immune systems of children and middle-aged adults resulted in fewer deaths among those groups. These cytokine storms are mediated via (NRF2) NF kappa beta and IL-6 levels in the brain and body. You learned about them in my early leptin blogs. Many experts on Sun and solar cycles expect our biggest threat from GSM is widespread crop failures and subsequent starvation of world's vulnerable populations. Crops will fail because of shorter growing seasons and erratic weather patterns (destructive cold snaps in spring and fall, massive hail and wind storms, etc.) This situation is expected to reach crisis proportions by 2028.

**Canada**, as one example, is expected to be no longer a food-producing and exporting nation.

Light from sun will be diminished by by lower solar irradiance and by heavy cloud/dust cover in atmosphere-- caused by cosmic rays plus increased volcanic activity. Magnetism is falling on both sun and earth (sun's heliosphere is protective to solar system, and earth magnetism deflects much of solar wind and cosmic radiation).

Earth's geomagnetic field has been dropping rapidly since 1800s and is accelerating in weakness in last few decades. Earth's magnetic poles are on the move, with North Pole marching towards Siberia and South Pole is moving and is now situated completely off Antarctica. A magnetic pole reversal, or flip, seems imminent.

Ran into Jack's work at the recommendation of a very brainy patient. That was several months ago now. I have been busy with moving and working since then, but have been gradually working my way through everything. There is an amazing amount of material here to digest.

I went skiing yesterday and listened to 8+ hours of webinars. Blew my mind. I can't wait to be truly facile with the science, although I can imagine that will take a while.

Jack, if you're reading this, thanks for all the hard work you've put into this. I don't know what you had to do to get to where you are, but I know enough to know that it is a monumental achievement. I can't wait to learn more.

I am starting a functional medicine practice in Denver, Colorado when I'm not learning about quantum medicine. I was recruited to Denver to found a company dedicated to patient education about environmental health - that is one reason that I have become so interested in this work.

If there is anyone in the Denver/Boulder area, let's hang out and raise our dopamine together! I'm interested to know what others are doing and how it's working for them.

# Thread: Circuit cut off switch
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2018-01-20T14:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-232957>

[https://en.geovital.com/products/electronic-pollution/circuit-cut-off-switch/](https://en.geovital.com/products/electronic-pollution/circuit-cut-off-switch/)

What do you think about this product?

### drezy — 2018-01-20T19:42:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-232975>

As a (mostly) reformed Tech & Automation dork I'd stick to the simpler system of just hitting the breakers or setting up a master switch unless:

1.  The breakers are very difficult to access AND wiring a master switch is a pain/expensive/impossible
2.  You like the idea of having the circuits off even during the day (which is nice ) without having to manage it yourself

Automated systems bring a little more complexity and wiring/circuit prerequisites which may or may not be an issue for your home.

### shiran — 2018-01-21T15:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-233045>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=232975)
>
> As a (mostly) reformed Tech & Automation dork I'd stick to the simpler system of just hitting the breakers or setting up a master switch unless:
>
> 1.  The breakers are very difficult to access AND wiring a master switch is a pain/expensive/impossible
> 2.  You like the idea of having the circuits off even during the day (which is nice ) without having to manage it yourself
>
> Automated systems bring a little more complexity and wiring/circuit prerequisites which may or may not be an issue for your home.

We are building a new home and therefore we thought to combine a convenient and easy solution. Do you recommend it anyway when there are no limitations? thanks Dan

### drezy — 2018-01-21T18:02:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-cut-off-switch.20727/post-233066>

Congratulations on the new home.

It sounds like the company may have field experts that check out your design and use a meter to check. If it all measures clean then it looks cool.

# Thread: Quick mods to a great pork belly recipe
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2018-01-20T20:54:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-232985>

In a previous thread elsewhere on the forum ( [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...erium-depleted-water.20394/page-4#post-232020](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-to-make-deuterium-depleted-water.20394/page-4#post-232020) ) I mentioned a great pork belly recipe I found online ( [https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169](https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169) ).

One modification to that recipe that makes it lower maintenance is to NOT brown the fat layer at first but do it at the end if needed. My oven may need temp calibration, but I kept getting semi-burnt and burnt top fat layers. So the mod looks like this:

1.  Throw some dry brine (salt, sugar if desired, and spices) on the pork belly
2.  Let sit in fridge for 1h - 48hr
3.  Put on a rack(to let dripping fall below) in a broil pan (image below)
4.  Place the broil pan on the middle rack in 325F/160C/gas mark 3 oven (I still use a tin foil hat on top of it for now)
5.  Forget it even exists for 90 min and don't even look it's direction
6.  Pour 12oz/350mL of water in the bottom the broil
7.  Forget it even exists for 50 min and try not to even smell how great it smells
8.  Hit it with the broiler at the end and watch to get it as dark or light as you like

Should look like this at the end:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

No, it didn't blacken, had a good browning on it's top; still not not crispy enough for me and next time I'll broil at the end, or drain off some fat early. ### drezy — 2018-01-22T16:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233134>

> [River said:](/goto/post?id=233131)
>
> Do you drizzle the drippings over the finished pork belly, or save it for later use?

I found that right then and there is a great opportunity to reduce the drippings and strengthen the flavor.

Keep in mind that D2O has a higher boiling point (aka bye bye H2O first) so reduction all the way down to just well flavored fats might be a wise strategy. Water can always be added last minute.

### caroline — 2018-01-27T06:06:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/post-233606>

What I do is even easier ........put the pork belly on baking parchment in a pan. Add salt and pepper and bake in preheated oven 400f for 30 minutes.
Turn and pour off the fat - add salt and pepper and bake for another 20 - 30 minutes. Turns out perfect and crispy everytime!
