>
> I tried to search this because I'm sure its been discussed before, but didn't find what I was looking for. I have read the Dopamine Rx, I like when Dr. Kruse says " You remain silent when you do not care. You speak up when you do…even if you are a pain in the ass." I've tried to speak up to my husband, but he isn't willing to listen. I see his health slipping. He is a CPA, so that means he sits in front of a computer for 40-70 hours a week. He won't wear blue blockers (I'm his optometrist, and I tried to explain blue light with my "doctor hat" on but it didn’t work) , he wont even download flux. He is literally finds a reason to put down every suggestion I make. He will not give up grains and cheese ( I wish I could eliminate these from out house because they are keeping me off track). He makes fun of my grounding shoes. Rolls his eyes when I tell him not put toxic spray sunblock on. The list goes on… he isn't ready for help.
>
>
>
> His dad past away a year ago due to complications of autoimmune disease. His death was the perfect example of how poor our medical system really is, and I have a major chip on my shoulder because of it. His lifestyle was never addressed, he was just put on medication after medication. My husband and his family thinks he died of pulmonary fibrosis. They don't seem to realize that if he was never put on the medication that caused the fibrosis, he may still be here.
>
>
>
> I've listened to Dr. Kruse state that he had a similar issue convincing his wife to change. I think it was the transformation that he made that finally woke her up. I don't have any transformations to make. I'm fit and active. My only "disability" is dystonia that affect my gait when I walk and how I write (both of which I am self conscious about but don't think any thing will ever help).
>
>
>
> I know I need to start small. Has anyone struggled with this? What did you do to improve the situation? How do you not sound like a "know it all". I almost think that if a different doc told him to wear blue blockers, he would be more inclined

I've been there. I shared info on Facebook, no one cared, and the ones I cared about most didn't even engage me. All of whom knew I had been quite ill and improving. I actually got quite PISSED about it. I shared more, my words expressed how frustrated I was that no one cared, or heeded my warnings. Then...

I forgot all about them and focused solely on me. I made an assumption that nothing was incurable, even my incurable condition.

I decided that I can't live to help anyone else in any fashion, only me. If my husband teased me, I flipped him off. He finally realized I was changing and he had better accept my change. I didn't give an ultimatum, my energy did. And in this process (long, emotional process) I became better. My personality returned, is returning, and the me I had become (weak, quiet, indifferent) is almost a distant memory.

I'd never say "leave him". I'd say see if he likes the you that you become.

For me, I decided that life is too short to allow someone to control your thinking or being. I gave my power away to everyone, taking it back is painful, emotional but necessary. I didn't have the strength to do this before I improved.

Incidentally, he's almost fully on board, except moving away from friends and family and 20 years with the same company. He was even ok with oil lamps.

If this isn't a life threatening situation, give him time to see how you've benefited.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-24T15:26:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-233344>

I'm more hardcore. If you are not good enough for you who are you good for. FOCUS on you always. His reaction will tell you all you need to know without any words. Always pay attention to actions in case like this. You learn to be resilient when you focus solely on you; this is how you survive when you know you are capable of making worthy contribution to the world sans the anchors driving you down; you only let it bother you or fight it when you have nothing to lose and so much to gain. When my wife realized I was not waiting on her, and she was being left behind by her own volition, things began to change..............but that change would not have happened unless I played hardball with her.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-24T15:29:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-233346>

Some things cannot be fixed in life. They can only be carried. This is why your redox is tied to your resilence in life. If your redox drops the loads you are carrying around begin to affect you. Your past effects your present and future and you begin to spin your wheels. If you just get up every morning when your load is to heavy to carry and see the sunrise everyday, your redox will rise and you'll begin to see that life's adversities have the remarkable ability of introducing the real you to yourself. This is how the "what's in life, become the "why's in our life and allow us to walk into our passions.

pancreatic cancer, person just got home for hospice care ( I think that means less than 6 months prognosis)(high 70's male)

two female breast cancers (60 yo mom and her daughter, got them at the same time)
intestinal problems, IBS or Crohn's (male in high 50's)
Crohn's multiple surgeries (male in 40's)
meningioma now, twice angioplasy in the past ( female in 80's)
MS (male in 30's)
Parkinson's (65 yo male)
Autism (male 20 yo), his younger brother with spina bifida

..

### Michalis — 2018-01-25T05:52:36+0200

### Da-mo — 2018-01-25T10:11:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-233432>

### LieselK — 2018-02-10T23:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-234515>

My husband is struggling to accept it all, but I'm not trying to explain it to him. I've talked about Jack for years now as my sister was following him so this isn't new. It's just new that I'm following so closely myself and really setting boundaries. And it all requires money or hard work and low dopamine individuals just can't get on board. I am caring for me and my kiddos as much as I can- my early riser toddler and I would sit on the porch under a blanket for the rising sun and he is the one who eats the most seafood and fish with me! My bigger kids are harder- I require them to sit outside in the mornings before they can watch any cartoons, but my husband won't require that. I just do what I can.

I'm sure my husband is also thinking that this is just another one of my crazy ideas because I've always been looking outside the box for better answers. I've definitely dragged him through some things that were not winners! You do you and take care of you. As a momma of 3 I can tell you that if you don't, there will be a day when you have nothing left to give. My world has been dark and hopeless, but 2 weeks into my leptin reset and being strict with my diet it's such a huge difference. I am feeling like "me" again and surely I would think my husband will someday be able to see that and appreciate it. Take care of yourself now before you ever get to a point that you feel so terrible.

### SlamSlask — 2018-02-11T13:47:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-234536>

Jack is on point when he says you should focus on yourself
They will eventually come by and slowly start listing to you I lived with a priorer college who had lots of trouble sleeping when I moved into a room in her house I asked if could turn of the WiFI and cable her computer instead
There was 2 high intensity routers in her walk in closet right next to her bed
So I did and without telling me she started sleeping the whole night without waking up
She still won't wear blueblockers etc but stealth tactics works she now watches sunsets and such most days
Be patient and understand how much you ask of him while I know you do this to help (Which I truly admire) you still have to understand how connected most people are to the "general thinking"

Here are some of the practical things I have done to help my without "annoying" him to much dad (Where I live ATM)
1\. Hacked my dads TV by turning down the blue in RGB - optimal no but better (he still does not know)
2\. Turned off the WiFi and pulled a cable to his couch
3\. Put his phone to 3G only
4\. Installed cabled handsfree in his car ( keeps him from having the phone in his pocket when driving too)
5\. Degaussed the tires on his car (again he does not know)
These are the major things just brand it as something smart/helpful to them it has to be easy to understand untill they accept the knew way of thinking

Hope this gives you a few ideas or at least some hope
Your doing the great work here keep it up

### taiyang — 2018-02-11T15:59:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-234544>

how do you set phone to 3G?

i understand the need to ignore people who will pull you down. however, you cannot 100% ignore a person if you are somewhat responsible for their problems.

i put my daughter on adhd meds at 16, and now at 20 yo she is addicted to them. i am considering whether to cut off the meds since she gets them through my insurance. she needs them to complete college, but maybe i can find something else for her...

she saw my transformation when i went paleo in 2009 and she was 11 years old. so she will eat low carb if she thinks she is gaining weight. she has problems sleeping but will not wear uvex, will not stop alcohol or weed. she doesn't care that i say her continuous use of her cell phone is damaging her sleep. she put her phone on airplane mode once and that was it. she is extremely disorganized anyway, as i am, but now her motivation is in the toilet and she needs an aderall to take a shower.

she usually does not want to hear anything i have to say, but one thing i said seems to at least have made her pause, so i will go back to that message again and again. i tell her she has to think like a scientist. she knows herself best. she is her own expert. i told her to pay attention to the times she is feeling good and try to figure out what factors repeat themselves when she feels that way. same when she is feeling bad.

so it would be nice and less stressful to completely check out of her life, but i could no sooner do that than anyone here could stand by idly while their loved one tried to slit their wrists.

### SlamSlask — 2018-02-11T16:48:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-234548>

@[taiyang](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20771/) Depends on the operating system (android or Ios etc)
Android universal guide: [http://www.droidiser.com/2014/05/force-enable-3g-android-phone.html](http://www.droidiser.com/2014/05/force-enable-3g-android-phone.html)
Iphone/Ios:

(the setting seems to be disabled on some version of Ios)
Try that else you should now know what to search for

### drezy — 2018-02-11T17:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-234550>

> [shannoncorrin said:](/goto/post?id=233304)
>
> I've tried to speak up to my husband, but he isn't willing to listen.

I hope you're able to get through.

I think you really have bigger responsibilities first, though. You have a very good window of opportunity with your young child to model behaviors and norms to the point that it just becomes ingrained in your child's routines.

"We're going out for morning sun now."
"We're doing icy face dunks now."
"We're dimming the lights way down and listening to an audiobook now."

If you encounter any hesitance with your child just promise that you'll tell a good story during the activity like "The story of the boy in my class that was the worst behaved child ever" or some such fun tale. While telling your narrative, through your shared activity, you'll be helping your child build a narrative of "It's just what we do".

Not to be too dark but if your husband doesn't join you and continues down his path he will be providing very useful (though sad) information.

Because children are still developing language skills and might not formalize it in a sentence, I think we forget that children connect the dots 10x faster/sooner than we ever give them credit for.

### Inger — 2018-02-20T10:28:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-235177>

Absolutely... We can never compromize our health and wellbeing for anyone. If a partner cannot tolerate that and leaves, good! If he stays he probably will start to value how full of life and joy you are becoming
I learned something in my partnership break. To let go, and I could see how everything that happens are for a purpose. There is so much to learn about myself in everything. Wow! And that we can never know what will happen tomorrow anyway! I would not have thought my partner wil leave, and then.. I would never have thought he comes back..lol And now I have no clue what tomorrow will bring, I just know today, and what I can do to heal, feel my best and live the fullest
In a few days I am leaving for a 3 week vacation, alone, to my sister in Spain to fill up on sunshine and heal, and I am so excited
A guy from this forum will be my housesitter and take care of my cat when I am away, how kind is that!

### Lahelada — 2018-02-20T16:29:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-235190>

I echo what was said . Absent from your my own life. I separated from my partner because he was not ready to widen his horizon. He had already done everything and was not willing to do more,at least not with me. My moment of clarity came when one morning at waking up and reading Barry's thread on this forum it occurred to me "out of the blue" to check my pulse. As a trained first aider I hit the spot without even looking. That morning I had to look for the point like looking for the correct note on the neck of a guitar. Weak. You have no idea how that concentrated all the transition metal in my back  and finally gave me the backbone to ditch my job, an EMF hell if ever there was one. I moved out of the city. The latter was not enough though. This was starkly put in evidence when a friend, who adheres to the principles we talk about here,came to visit me here this weekend and felt like shit for the better part of his stay. So a move is on the cards again. It might be one more interim move ,depending on how quickly we can bring off this new business based on what we learn here that we will put together with the help of another friend who I also met through sounding off what we talk about here. If you had told me on the day that I resigned my job what would happen at the end of 2017 I would have been like this:  No way,Jose.
But when I finally directed the "big mouth " that in thought I always had to the outside unexpected things began to happen.Absent from my own life, living a fantasy life in my head,a life that could never nourish me. Don't do it. You wake up one day with the mind of a 32 year old with a birth certificate making you out you to be 50 and your mitochondria revealing you to be closer to 60.

### Jenny S — 2018-03-18T05:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/living-with-low-dopamine-husband-who-thinks-im-crazy.20752/post-237393>

I've listened to Dr. Kruse state that he had a similar issue convincing his wife to change. I think it was the transformation that he made that finally woke her up. I don't have any transformations to make. I'm fit and active. My only "disability" is dystonia that affect my gait when I walk and how I write (both of which I am self conscious about but don't think any thing will ever help).

Have you found anything to help with the dystonia - my cousin has it & is not very well.

# Thread: Child started to stutter at 12 years old ... maybe after vaccine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-started-to-stutter-at-12-years-old-maybe-after-vaccine.20753/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sandra — 2018-01-24T04:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/child-started-to-stutter-at-12-years-old-maybe-after-vaccine.20753/post-233312>

I am wondering if there could be a potential link between a child developing a stutter and a vaccine. I have read the other threads here about stuttering and some comments seem like they could be of use but most seem to be for quite different stuttering situations.

My son was 12.5 when he received the following 2 vaccines through the school program.

-   Human papillomavirus (HPV)
-   Diphtheria, tetanus and acellular pertussis (whooping cough) (dopa)

He is now 13.5 and I have noticed that in recent months he has started to stutter. I do not know exactly when this started to happen, but I wonder if it may have anything to do with the vaccine. At the time of the vaccine, he did not develop any other noticeable side effects like fever, vomiting, swelling, etc.

He is seemingly normal in every other way ... sleeps well, does well at school, has lots of friends, is active (rides his bike to schools, swims a couple of times a week and plays club soccer), is confident, not allergic to anything.

When he stutters, it doesn't really seem to bother him that much but he does get a little frustrated at times, but more 'why is this happening' rather than 'I'm not going to talk anymore'. He does not stutter all the time but I've not really been able to correlate it to when he's over-excited, anxious, etc. I also wonder if it could just be a result of him growing and his mouth and brain not quite lining up exactly?

In any case, is there anything I could look at to remove the stutter? I'm wondering if I should take him to see a speech pathologist but if it may be a result of the vaccine, perhaps he needs some kind of detox.

Any comments or advice would be great, thanks


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Do we need to know our why?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-24T13:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/post-233330>

I think we do. Without knowing our why I believe the attainment of Optimal will not happen.

I was getting ready for a medical talk earlier this week and I was jotting some things down about how my surgical judgement has changed from the time I left residency to the present and going over my practice patterns for the talk. It dawned on me the practice of medicine is very much like the practice we use in life to become a more imporved version of ourself. Inevitably, practice isn't the thing you do once you're good. It's the thing you do that makes you good. I was surprised yesterday about the crowd of people I was asked to speak to. The mayor was in the audience and so was some the state health department officials. I had no idea who I was speaking too. I thought it was going to be mostly patients and PCP's in my new referal base. When I saw that my audience was quite different than I anticipated I scrapped my "neurosurgery talk plan" on minimally invasive spine surgery and decided to talk about how my "what", neurosurgery, led me into my my "why", my real passion.........Quantum biology. The response I got from yesterday's talk even astounded me. I think I realized something about myself yesterday. When I speak from the heart I connect people with nature. That connection is the key to getting my flock well. This is the basis of becoming a mitochondriac. I realized yesterday that I help people find their "why" in healthcare via biophysics so their wellness manifests. Do you need some clarity on how to mov your "what's, to the why's that will move your deicsion making process to focus on only the things that matter in your life to keep you well? Then reach out to me and join my flock of mitochondriacs. [https://www.facebook.com/mike.morenz/videos/10153124017336716/](https://www.facebook.com/mike.morenz/videos/10153124017336716/)

### brandie — 2018-01-24T21:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/post-233390>

^^^^THIS!
Some may call it spirituality, but it's just purposeful living.
People kill themselves living for the wrong purpose, money and "success", often.
We are all finding a better purpose!

My daughter just started college. While discussing her major she was focused on being better than those that came before her, making good money and feeling successful (in other's eyes).
After the first week we sat down and talked about what she valued in her future; health, family, well being, happiness

I'm so glad I stubbled into this arena, if for no other reason, than to guide my daughter to a better life, a better way of living than those who've come before her~ knowing her "why".

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-25T00:05:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/post-233400>

I listed my real estate holdings today in a 5 G market............feeling the power of what an industry insider told me on a beach from Mexico 3 weeks ago.

I can no longer wait...........time to act.

### Da-mo — 2018-01-25T09:40:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/post-233430>

Real estate and microwave has been featuring prominently in my observations this past year.

My daughter is a physiotherapist in a clinic in town. High density area with wireless in the building. I have mentioned to her about how the clinic fills with clients who invariably use their phones and devices while waiting and getting treated. I've also had to spend time with family members at the city hospital lately. The roof of every building bristling with antennae and transmitters (obviously a source of income). How do you heal people in such places? At least the neurosurgery ward bans electronic devices - seems they know something the rest of the hospital doesn't fathom.

My 30 something nephew just went ketogenic . . . why? He saw his uncle (me) and thought I was pretty trim for a 50yo while he was having trouble with his weight.

My late 50s triathlete brother in law also just went ketogenic . . . why? because 5 years ago I told him fuelling his performance on carbs was aging him faster. Must have finally clicked for him.

My sister was prescribed SSRIs and melatonin. I thought her doctor must be sub par until she told me that her doc had told her to give up flying (she works for an airline) due to the time zones and radiation. I thought more of her doc after that. Last time I saw her she was no longer taking the drugs - didn't need them. . . . . . why? she had just taken a holiday and bathed in the cenotes while her son (a pro golfer) was playing a tournament in Cancun. She didn't make the connection until I told her what I had learned here. Now she knows why.

A guy I work with has MS. His sister died from it. I tell him lots of stuff from here and he tries them out and has been having small successes. The other day I started telling him about D2O. Today I dropped a bomb on him . . . I told him JK made a big statement . . . . drop your tissue deuterium below 130ppm and your AI will resolve. Then backed it up with the anecdote about pregnant/breastfeeding women AI halting due to deuterium transfer. . . . . .

Today I spent time with a colleague who is having a hard time with stress. He has nothing really to stress about but loses perspective. He had a dark moment while we were talking - dark but healing - then I told him about dopamine and left it there . . . . . . .

Plant the ideas as seeds . . . sometimes they just need a little time and the right conditions to take root and grow.

JK, I want you to know that your insights are helping people well beyond your tribe. They see me at work wearing ice jackets and wearing blue blockers inside and taking them off outside. They see me eat fat and not exercise yet stay trim. They see me avoid the sugary free foods. Initially they think I'm crazy . . . . eventually they ask "Why?"

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-25T16:05:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/post-233466>

glad to hear it. More need to follow your lead.

### Da-mo — 2018-01-25T16:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/post-233471>

### caroline — 2018-01-27T05:28:00+0200

### caroline — 2018-01-27T05:43:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-know-our-why.20756/post-233600>

we have been asking ourselves the last month or so .... what the heck we have done to ourselves - pulling up stakes after 30+years in one place. It has been physically very hard and very emotional and family and friends haven't understood - but .....It has always been about the "why"

This has all been - and only ever been about staying as healthy as possible. It sure hasn't been easy ....but nothing worthwhile ever is.

Luckily we have landed in a community that values being healthy. People here walk and jog and cycle and canoe and swim and surf etc.

We are not exactly sure how we landed here .........quantum entanglement I am pretty sure. It is funny when people ask ask us how we came to this place and we have to admit that we aren't really sure - it just kind of happened!
